observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor (a) require the CA to (i) make necessary adjustments on the uncapitalized PPE repairs and maintenance, erroneous capitalization of regular maintenance for road networks, and misclassification of PPE accounts totaling ₱29,173,115.17; (ii) recognize in the books the completed infrastructure projects amounting to ₱22,392,246.10, as of December 31, 2023; (iii) maintain PPELCs to support the GL balances of PPE accounts and regularly reconcile balances with the GSO property records; and; (b) require the GSO Head to: (i) present the 73 units of motor vehicles to the AT for validation; (ii) determine vehicles that were not yet registered with the LTO and ensure their registration to avoid possible fines and penalties; (iii) instruct the end-users of each vehicle to always preserve and keep copies of OR/CR in the vehicles for any surprise inspection by authorities; (iv) secure the deed of donation from the DOH and a private entity for the donated PPE and request from the City’s Appraisal Committee to render an appraisal report on donated PPE with unknown costs to facilitate their recognition in the books; (v) strictly implement that government vehicles bear government plate and official seal logo of the City with markings “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”; (vi) record the value of parcels of land according to their acquisition cost for appropriate reconciliation with the CAO records; (vii) conduct annual inventory of local roads under the City’s jurisdiction and prepare their corresponding Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and Local Road Network Property Cards; (viii) include in the RPCPPE the e-trikes acquired through donation from the DOE; and (ix) reconcile the results of physical inventory of PPE with the CAO records; and (c) create a Special Inventory Committee to undertake the “One-Time” cleansing of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct (a) the CA to recalculate the funds allowed to be utilized for the payment of BAC honoraria; (b) the CT to cause the transfer of the unutilized funds from the sale of bid documents to GF at the end of the year; and (c) the CT and BAC members to refrain from charging BAC expenses other than BAC honoraria and overtime pay from the sale of bid documents.
observations.reason: The new composition of BAC will adhere to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT and CA to (i) limit the grant of cash advance for CF to three (3) months’ requirements; and (ii) release the cash advance at the beginning of the project implementation period; (b) require the SDO to furnish the CAO, and the AT, with a certified copy of the TL on the previous CA’s LR duly stamped "received" by ICFAU; (c) SDO to provide the AT a copy of the PNP chief certification on cash advance for CF, for verification; (d) require the CAO to issue a demand letter to the SDO in case of his failure to fully liquidate his cash advance (i) within 30 days after every quarter, or from the approved target date of completion of the project/activity, or after the cash advance had been fully utilized, whichever comes first; and (ii) at the end of the year; and (f) the CAO invariably observe the proper use of account title “Prior Period Adjustments” in the recognition of expenses pertaining to prior period.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) thru the CDRRMO, carefully plan the LDRRM PPAs to ensure effective and efficient utilization of the LDRRMF; (b) instruct the CA to (i) submit the DVs and complete supporting documents for the LDRRMF accounts payables totaling ₱21,901,572.50; (ii) adjust or correct the 2023 year-end balance of TL-DRRMF account and the reported cash back-up for CY 2017, 2019 and 2020 TL-LDRRMF; (iii) prepare and submit SLs, GLs, and GJs to support the financial statement account balances; (iv) submit a reliable Report on the Utilization of DRRMF; (v) disclose the details of the Continuing Appropriations in the NTFS; and (vi) submit the DVs and LRs totaling ₱4,984,170.62 for audit; (c) instruct the CT to (i) open a depository account exclusively for the STF-LDRRMF to ensure that funds are used for the intended purpose; and (ii) transfer the unspent CY 2023 LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE from GF to STF under the account” Trust Liability DRRM”; and, (d) instruct the CBO to prepare and maintain a Registry of Cash Donations and Utilization (RCDU).

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) recognize and take action on the need to revise and update the City’s RC through an Ordinance to generate more income for its development projects and programs; (b) instruct the LFC to (i) realistically set target incomes based on historical growth rates and the City’s capability; and (ii) appropriate/set budgets within reasonable or attainable estimates; and (c) direct the CT to intensify revenue collection efforts to meet the budgetary requirements for financing the City’s PPAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the BPLO Chief to (i) further identify and investigate any errors in the BPS-generated reports and report the issues to the service provider for appropriate action; (ii) regularly reconcile records with the CAO and CTO; (iii) investigate the aforementioned suppliers and contractors with underdeclared sales or receipts, subject them to examination of books of accounts to ensure the correctness of taxes, fees, and charges to be collected; (iv) regularly examine the books of accounts of business establishments to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales or receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid pursuant to Section 171 of RA No. 7160; (v) exert effort in learning the formulation and implementation of PILAA and submit a representation to the SP to institutionalize its use; (vi) require the taxpayers to submit a complete documentation of the financial records and ensure that these records form part of their permanent files for easy verification; and (vii) prescribe a standard format of Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts, when applicable, and require the business taxpayers to have it duly notarized upon submission; (b) strictly require the BPLO Chief and the CT to devise measures to validate the declared gross sales or receipts of establishments applying for business permits especially those who are having direct transactions with the City and its barangays; and (c) require the CAO to maintain SLs for the business taxpayers, and reconcile differences with the BPLO.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) BAC and TWG members to submit to the AT copies of approved contracts and POs together with their SDs, within five working days from execution to facilitate timely review and other necessary audit procedures; and (b) CGSO Officer to promptly notify the AT, through submission of copies of IARs and SDs, within 24 hours of accepting procured projects to ensure proper monitoring and compliance.
observations.reason: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) IC to undertake an actual physical count of PPE and prepare a complete and reliable RPCPPE to substantiate the existence of PPE account balances; and (b) the MA (i) reclassify the costs for the dredging of the river to expense and/or prior year’s adjustment account; (ii) in coordination with the OIC-GSO and Municipal Engineer (ME), determine the specific details of the Land Improvements account to facilitate the provision of allowance for depreciation thereon; and (iii) strictly adhere to the proper classification of accounts according to RCALGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

observations.reason: Other deficiencies are discussed in Observation and Recommendation No. 2, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) create an inventory committee tasked with conducting the physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and ensure that committee members are relieved from their regular duties to complete the undertaking and submit an RPCPPE to the MA and AT not later than January 31 of each year; (b) require the GSO/MT and MA to: (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer (ME) to facilitate compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 in keeping and maintaining records for the Road Networks account; and (ii) maintain complete and updated PCs and PPELCs, respectively, for the control and monitoring of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MDRRMO and the MBO Heads to refrain from charging ineligible expenditures against the LDRRMF; (b) MA and BAC Secretariat to submit explanation or justification on noted documentary deficiencies; and (c) MA to verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment to suppliers/contractors.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the MENRO Head to (i) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the SWMP of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of environmental laws; (ii) monitor the barangays without MRFs and provide technical/financial for them to establish their own MRFs; and (iii) review the Pambayang Kautusan Blg. 23 and identify the provisions that require strict implementation and revisions to ensure the attainment of the objectives for enacting the Ordinance; and (b) require the MBO to provide a budget for the rehabilitation of perimeter fence at the Municipality’s MRF.

We recommended that the (a) LCE require the MT to continuously exert her best effort to improve tax collection efficiency and (b) Sangguniang Bayan members to prioritize the enactment of revised MRC in accordance with Section 191, Book II of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MPDO, along with the GFPS and Technical Working Group, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database to facilitate better and efficient formulation of PPAs to truly address GAD issues and concerned in the locality.

We recommended and the LCE (a) to instruct the (a) MA to (i) trace the discrepancies between the reported acquisition costs of the PPE accounts and the lapsing schedule for depreciation to provide a reliable balances for depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation accounts; (ii) exhaust all efforts to retrieve the records of the PPE accounts under the TF books to facilitate their transfer to GF books and provide an allowance for their depreciation; (b) GSO to (i) render a complete RPCPPE for reconciliation with the MAO’s property records; (ii) safekeep the Municipality’s properties; (c) Disposal Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to account for all unserviceable PPEs to facilitate their immediate disposal; and (d) MA and GSO Officer to prepare and maintain their property records.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to facilitate the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for fair presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) MDRRMO to (i) pursue compliance with the required allocation of LDRRMF based on 5% of the total estimated revenue from regular sources; (ii) ensure full implementation of all PPAs programmed under the 70% MF; (b) MDRRMC and MDRRMO to provide specific timetable for the implementation of PPAs incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to facilitate their timely and complete implementation; (c) MA and MT to transfer only the unexpended balance of the current year’s 30% QRF and 70% MF-MOOE to the STF under the TL-DRRMF every year-end; (ii) revert to the GF the unexpended balance of the MF-CO in previous years for the implementation of its intended project; and (d) ensure that disbursements made against the 5% LDRRMF are supported with complete and valid supporting documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) MA to (i) apply the PIM in
the recording of inventories
upon purchase;
(ii) make the necessary
adjustment(s) in the books to
properly recognize inventories
on-hand at year-end and
recognize as expense the
distributed stocks accordingly;
and
(iii) maintain SLCs; 
(b) Supply Officer (SO) to (i)
prepare and maintain SCs for
control and monitoring of
inventories;
(ii) prepare and submit the
SSMI to the MAO to facilitate
the recording of issued
inventories for the month; and
(iii) conduct physical count of
inventories and submit the
results thereof to the MAO and
AT for reference in audit; and
(c) MT and MA to (i) reconcile
inventory records; and
(ii) establish an Inventory
Management process
indicating the required
documents to be prepared upon
receipt/delivery, issuance, and
at month-end, including the
deadline for submission of
reports, and to whom the
reports are to be submitted as
prescribed by the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I to ensure
accuracy of records, promote
operational efficiency, and
encourage adherence to
policies, rules and regulations

We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) MA and MT to maintain
complete PPELCs and PCs,
respectively, for monitoring,
control and reconciliation of
PPE records;
(b) IC to (i) complete the
physical inventory taking of
PPE in accordance with the PIP
to establish the existence and
reliability of PPE account
balances;
(ii) collaborate with the MAO
and MTO to ensure that all
PPEs included in the ICF are
reconciled with their respective
PPE records;
(iii) render a complete RPCPPE
with the conditions and
relevant information on PPE;
and
(iv) ensure that all insurable
assets have insurance coverage
with the GSIS to indemnify for
the value of government
properties lost through theft or
any unforeseen events;
(c) DAC to submit the relevant
documents pertaining to the
disposed PPE with total
acquisition cost of
₱8,476,469.17; and
(d) MA to recognize in the
books the costs of the partially-
completed projects as of year-
end and henceforth require the
Municipal Engineer (ME) to
regularly submit to the MAO a
copy of the status report of on-
going projects for appropriate
recognition in the books.

We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) ME/BAC Chairperson and
MA to undertake the following
actions: (i) identify and address
the root causes of the
deficiencies related to the
supporting documents; and
(ii) submit the necessary
documents for the transactions
with deficiencies;
(b) ME (i) to coordinate with
the contractors concerned for
the installation of project
signboards at designated sites
and provide evidence of their
installation;
(ii) to adhere to DPWH
guidelines in determining the
ABCs to prevent undue
inflation of project cost; and
(iii) as BAC Chairperson,
subject all procurements to
public bidding unless the
conditions are met for the use
of alternative procurement
methods; and
(c) MA to ensure that (i)
advance payments equivalent
to 15% of the contract price are
substantiated with the required
documentation; and
(ii) supporting documents are
sufficient and valid before any
payments are made.

We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) MDRRMO Officer to (i)
submit the updated MDRRMC
and MDRRMO organizational
structure to the AT;
(ii) implement fully PPAs for
disaster prevention, mitigation,
and preparedness at the set time
to avoid losses from hazards as
well as to reduce the intensity
of future disasters;
(iii) prepare and submit the
LDRRMFIP in accordance
with the format provided in
Annex A of COA Circular No.
2012-002, reflecting the
allocation of 30% for QRF and
70% for Mitigation Fund,
including a list of projects and
activities charged to
unexpended LDRRMF from
previous years;
(iv) strictly adhere to the
provisions of NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1
regarding the release and use of
the unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF transferred to the
Special Trust Fund; and
(v) ensure that the MDRRMF
shall be used solely to support
disaster risk management
activities;
(b) MBO to (i) prepare a
separate RAOs for MDRRMF;
and
(ii) ensure that changes in the
annual budget or AIP shall be
supported with approved SB
resolution;
(c) BAC Chairperson/
Secretariat to (i) post in the
PhilGEPS the RFQ and Award
for the procurements covered
by the alternative mode of
procurement – Negotiated
Procurement – SVP and
Emergency Cases; and
(ii) issue BAC Resolutions
recommending the use of
alternative mode of
procurement for approval of the
LCE;
(d) MA and BAC Chairperson
to submit the requisite
documentary requirements
which include list of recipients
and pictures of actual
distribution of welfare goods;
proof of posting of RFQ in the
PhilGEPS website, website of
the Municipality, if available,
and at any conspicuous place
reserved for this purpose in the
premises of the Municipality;
and
(e) MA to (i) transfer the
unexpended LDRRMF under
the account TL - DRRMF in the
Trust Fund books at year-end;
(ii) submit on a monthly basis
the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF; and
(iii) ensure that the necessary
documents supporting payment
claims are valid and complete
before payments are made.

We recommended that LCE
(a) expedite the full utilization
and implementation of the
identified development projects
under the trust fund for
optimum attainment of the
intent of the fund;
(b) require the MA (i) in
coordination with the ME,
BAC Chairperson, and
MSWDO to submit the
documents with noted
deficiencies to the AT;
(ii) exert efforts in identifying
the source of funds for the
unused balances amounting to
₱3,260,092.48;
(iii) if warranted, file the
request for authority to write-
off dormant fund transfers duly
supported with relevant
documents; and
(iv) conduct regular and
periodic verification, analysis,
and validation of the existence
of fund transfers to source
NGAs, and other government
and non-government agencies;
and
(c) require the BAC
Chairperson to (i) post
clarifications, modifications,
changes, or amendments to the
Bidding Documents through
the issuance of the
Supplemental/Bid Bulletin in
the PhilGEPS website;
(ii) cause the posting of RFQs
in the PhilGEPS website in
accordance with Annex H of
the 2016 RIRR of RA No.
9184;
(iii) cause the preparation of
BAC Resolutions
recommending to the HOPE
the mode of procurement and
the award of contract in favor
of the supplier or contractor
with the Single or Lowest
Calculated and Responsive
Quotation; and
(iv) subject all procurements to
public bidding unless the
conditions are met for the use
of alternative procurement
methods.

We recommended that the LCE
require the Local Finance
Committee (LFC), in
coordination with the SB to
(a) request the presence of the
Lessee for the discussion of
workable solutions to address
the issues that hamper the
operation of the public market
and shopping mall;
(b) undertake a full review of
the MOA with Springfont
Realty Corporation to identify
indications of default and assert
viable options for the
continuance or cancellation of
the lease agreement and take
back the leased asset in
accordance with applicable
laws; and
(c) investigate the alleged
payment of ₱1,800,000.00 by
the Lessee to the Municipality
for the advance rental.

We recommended that the
LCE
(a) coordinate with the SB for
the rationalization of the MOA
including options for its
amendment or modification
and payments of royalty fees to
the PFDA for the past 20 years;
(b) require the MT to trace their
records from CY 2004 to
present to obtain information
on the income from fish port
operation and submit the same
to PFDA; and
(c) require the MA to maintain
a separate subsidiary ledger for
a liability account showing the
unpaid retainer’s fee to the
PFDA.

We recommended that the
LCE require the MA to
(a) submit the financial reports
and documents within the first
ten days of the ensuing month
pursuant to COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15,
2009; and
(b) make the necessary
adjustment in the books for all
unreleased checks at the end of
the year, supported with the
Report of Unreleased Checks
certified by the MT.

We recommended that the
LCE require the BAC
Chairperson to strictly comply
with the provisions of the
above-mentioned COA
Circulars on timely furnishing
of copies of contracts/POs and
notification to the AT of the
scheduled delivery of goods
for random verification.

We recommended that the
LCE require the GAD Focal
Person to
(a) submit the details of the
GAD AR and supporting
documents to the AT for
further audit of the expenses
charged against GAD fund;
(b) initiate the capacitation of

the programs and projects and
to determine the efficiency and
effectiveness of the LGU’s
mainstreaming efforts; and
(c) create a Monitoring and
Evaluation Team to evaluate
the outcomes of the GAD
PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE
direct the (a) MA to coordinate
with the ME to retrieve the (a) Not Implemented
project details from the CIP

appropriate PPE accounts, if
warranted; (b) GSO Head to (i)
utilize the prescribed reports (b.i) Not Implemented
format such as RPCPPE,
RPCSP, and RLRN to
accurately document the results
of physical counts of PPE; and (b.ii) Not Implemented
(ii) cause the completion of the
prepared IIRUP to facilitate the

unserviceable PPE; and (c) MT
to update the registration with
LTO and insurance with GSIS
for all motor vehicles owned by
the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Implemented
LCE establish a Titling
Committee, responsible with the
validation of parcels of lands
owned by the Municipality and
processing the titling of the
Municipality’s legally-owned
parcels of land.

We recommended that the LCE (a.i) Not Implemented

We recommended that the LCE
require the MA to (a) review the (a) Implemented

We recommended that the LCE (a) Implemented
(a) require the GFPG and its
TWG to execute fully its

We recommended and the LCE

(a) the GSO to facilitate the

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require
(a) the MA, in coordination with
other Municipal Offices to exert
efforts in identifying the source of
funds for the unexpended balances
totaling ₱1,049,028.32. Otherwise, prepare the necessary documents to request a write-off from the books as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005, if warranted; and
(b) prepare SL for each fund transfer and conduct regular and periodic analysis and validation with the source NGAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct (a) the City Accountant (CA)
to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the CLT account for the inadvertent inclusion
of interest income; and (b) the City Treasurer (CT) to: (i) provide the AT with a copy
of the alleged affidavit for the 650 sheets of lost CTCs under the accountability of the
former DBCs. If warranted, file a request for relief from property accountability in
accordance with the existing regulations; (ii) take steps to recover the used/unused
454 sheets of CTCs and any unremitted funds, by issuing demand letters, and
pursuing legal action or other recovery mechanisms.

We recommended that the City Mayor: (a) designate sufficient disbursing
officers to manage cash advances for the City’s programs and activities, to prevent
the infractions noted in the granting and liquidation of cash advances;
(b) require the CA to issue demand letters to AOs with outstanding cash advances as
at year-end; and (c) issue a directive to strictly enforce the provisions of COA Circular
No. 97-002, including: (i) adherence to the prescribed liquidation periods for cash
advances; (ii) submission of complete documentation supporting LRs; (iii) stamping
“PAID” on all documents supporting LRs after payments to payees; (iv) prohibition
against granting multiple cash advances without liquidation; and (v) liquidation of all
cash advances by year-end.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT to: (i) furnish the CA with a
Certified List of Taxpayers (CLT), showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year, including delinquent accounts beyond 10
years, to serve as basis for setting-up the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning
of the year; and (ii) ensure that the CTO’s system is updated to generate accurate and
comprehensive reports that fully capture all tax-related data; and (b) CA to: (i)
recognize RPT/SET receivables based on the CLT submitted by the CT; (ii) establish
an allowance for impairment in accordance with IPSAS 41; (iii) prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the misstatements in the RPT and SET Income accounts
and Government Equity account; and (iv) apply the accrual method of income
recognition by ensuring that prior years collections are recognized under the
Government Equity account, rather than as current-year income.

We recommended that the City Mayor: (a) direct the CA to (i) prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the accounting errors and recognize the
unrecorded inventories on hand as of year-end; (ii) record the procurement of
inventories under their corresponding Inventory accounts; and (iii) maintain
complete SLCs for all inventory items; (b) create an IC responsible for conducting
regular physical stock-taking and preparing the required inventory reports; and
(c) establish a centralized storage facility for procured inventories to: (i) ensure
adequate control and proper inventory management; (ii) facilitate the preparation
and timely submission of the SSMI supported by RISs to the CAO; (iii) enable the
complete maintenance of SCs; and (iv) ensure regular reconciliation of records
between the CGSO and the CAO.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) require the CA to: (i) prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted accounting errors that led to the net
understatement of the PPE account balance by ₱7,146,964.57; and (ii) maintain
PPELCs to support the reported balances of the PPE accounts and regularly reconcile
these with the CGSO records; and; (b) require the CGSO Chief to: (i) reconcile
property records with those of the CAO; (ii) conduct an annual inventory of local
roads under the City’s jurisdiction and prepare the corresponding Local Road
Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property; (iii) secure deeds of
donation from the DOH and private donors and request the City’s Appraisal
Committee to appraise donated PPE with unknown value for proper recording in the
books; (iv) regularly reconcile the results of physical inventory with CAO records;
and (v) strengthen property control management by strictly applying existing rules
and regulations on PPE; and (c) create a Special Inventory Committee to conduct a
“One-Time” cleansing of PPE accounts through inventory-taking, identification of
PPE items, disposal of non-existing PPE, and preparation of RPCPPE.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) require the CA and CT to coordinate their efforts to:
(i) identify the details and retrieve the necessary records pertaining to the unsubstantiated balance of ₱7,015,222.97 under the Due to NGAs account. If warranted, file a request for write-off to present a fair and accurate balance of the account; and (ii) ensure the completeness and availability of documents for all fund transfers; (b) require the CA to: (i) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous and inadvertent recognition of fund transfer receipts; and (ii) maintain a detailed SL to properly monitor the status of fund transfers thereby avoiding overdrafts and undetermined balances; (c) require the concerned implementing offices to verify and assess the status of implementation of fund transfers that have remained dormant for more than one to 13 years, to facilitate appropriate and immediate action; and (d) submit a written explanation justifying the failure to fully utilize the completed barangay health center in Barangay San Gabriel for its intended purpose.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor (a) require the SP to
recognize the need for a comprehensive revision and update of the CRC through an
Ordinance, with the aim of generating additional income to support the City’s
development projects and programs; (b) instruct the LFC to reassess the target
revenue based on historical growth rates, as well as the City’s capacity, ensuring that
the estimates are both reasonable and attainable; and (c) direct the CT to intensify
revenue collection efforts to meet the budgetary requirements for financing the City’s
PPAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the BPLO Chief to:
(i) identify and investigate any errors in BPS-generated reports and coordinate with
the service provider for necessary system upgrades; (ii) regularly reconcile records
with the CAO and CTO; (iii) investigate and examine suppliers and contractors with
underdeclared sales or receipts; (iv) conduct regular audits of business taxpayers’
books as mandated under Section 171 of RA No. 7160; (v) pursue training and initiate
the enactment of an ordinance to implement the PILAA in the City; (vi) require
taxpayers to submit complete financial documentation and ensure that these from
part of their permanent records for easy verification; and (vii) prescribe a standard
format for the Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts, where applicable, and
require notarization upon submission; (b) strictly require the BPLO Chief and the
City Treasurer (CT) to devise measures to validate the declared gross sales or receipts
of establishments applying for business permits, especially those transacting with the
City or barangays; and (c) require the CAO to maintain SLs for all business
taxpayers, and regularly reconcile these with BPLO records.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CA to: (a) coordinate with
the CHRMO Officer to obtain accurate balances of cumulated leave credits and
recognize the corresponding Leave Benefits Payable and Leave Benefits Expenses for
the leaves of absence earned by the City’s employees; and (b) ensure that the NFS
include adequate disclosure of employee benefits expenses, relevant policies, and other
relevant information in compliance with IPSASs and 39.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CA to: (a) restate the CY
2023 FSs for the prior period errors and adjustments recognized under the PPA
account; (b) provide supporting documents (SDs) for every adjustment charged to the
PPA account for verification; (c) correct the improper and erroneous charges to the
PPA account, including the stale check reversals and recognition of taxes; (d) reclassify
the derecognition of semi-expendable assets as a Change in Accounting Policy; and
(e) ensure detailed disclosure of accounts in the NFS in accordance with IPSAS 3.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) continuously appropriate 20% of
the City’s annual NTA for development projects in compliance with the mandate of the
RA 7160 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; (b) refrain from using 20% DF other
than its intended purpose; and (c) require the BAC and CE to: (i) carefully plan and
schedule all procurement activities to ensure timely implementation and completion
of development projects; (ii) monitor the submission of PPMPs by various Offices to
facilitate the preparation and submission of the APP to the AT and other regulatory
agencies; and (iii) observe the earliest possible time in the conduct of public bidding
and attendance of observers.

We also recommended that the (a) CE (i) explain the noted discrepancies in the
actual completed projects from the program of works and provide appropriate action
thereof; (ii) strictly ensure that projects are implemented in accordance with program
of works and required project specifications; (iii) perform detailed engineering,
surveys and designs on infrastructure projects in accordance with the standards and
specifications prescribed by the City Mayor prior to conduct of bidding; and (iv) issue
the Certificate of Acceptance after the one-year defects liability period and upon
submission of the warranty certificate by the contractor; and (b) CA ensure that
payments for infrastructure projects are supported with sufficient and appropriate
documentation in accordance with the pertinent rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) thru the CDRRMO, implement the
programmed disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs as planned to ensure
effective and efficient utilization of the CDRRMF; (b) instruct the CT and the
CDRRMO Chief to provide a list of cash donations to the CBO Officer to facilitate
the latter’s preparation of RCDU and reconciliation with CAO’s records; (c) direct
the CT to open a separate depository account for the STF of the CDRRMF;
(d) require the CA to prepare and submit the subsidiary records supporting the TL-
DRRMF account balance; (e) require the CA, CT and CBO Officer to determine the
cause of insufficient cash backing of the TL-DRRMF to resolve the issue; (f) require
the CT and CA to revert the STF aged five years and more to GF for reappropriation
for the City’s social services projects; (g) direct the CA to: (i) record in the books the
proposed adjustments to the TL-DRRMF account; (ii) provide a disclosure in the NFS
for the unexpended balance of the Continuing Appropriations of the CDRRMF; and
(iii) immediately submit the source documents to the AT; and (h) require the
CDRRMO Chief to submit a written explanation for the unimplemented capital
outlay projects funded by the Continuing Appropriations of CYs 2021-2023.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the CHRMO to: (i) require
the 163 unverified COS personnel to appear before the AT for validation and the SDs
confirming their resignation or non-renewal; (ii) prepare formal contracts for COS
personnel, clearly specifying their assigned duties and responsibilities; (iii) hire COS
personnel based on necessity and not to perform regular functions; and (iv) strictly
implement the City’s guidelines on the hiring and termination of COS personnel; and
(b) require the CA to support payments for hazard, subsistence and laundry
allowances to COS personnel with the necessary and appropriate SDs to establish the
validity and propriety of payments.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) the CSWDO Officer to:
(i) draft clear and well-defined guidelines, subject to SP approval, on the
qualifications, types, conditions, and limits of financial assistance to ensure equitable
distribution; and (ii) ensure that justifications are provided for financial assistance
exceeding the ₱2,000.00 AICS aid limit; (b) SP members to refrain from granting
financial assistance, which is an executive function, and instead refer requests to the
CSWDO; and (c) CA to: (i) maintain a RAAO for donations to facilitate reconciliation
of records with the CBO; (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
misclassified financial assistance recorded under the Donations account; (iii) verify
the completeness of SDs for financial assistance prior to payment; and (iv) ensure
that cash advances are issued only for authorized, recurring and petty operating
expenses and in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the CHRMO Chief and CA
to: (i) discontinue the payment of TA to officers/employees assigned government
vehicles or who use government vehicles; (ii) provide the necessary SDs for RATA
payments; and (iii) strictly adhere to applicable rules and regulations on the granting
and the submission of required documents; (b) require concerned officials/employees
assigned government vehicles to refund any excess TA payments to which they are no
longer entitled; and (c) direct the CA to: (i) submit the paid DVs for the months April
to December 2023 and September to December 2024; and (ii) strictly comply with the
timely rendition of accounts to the AT to avoid delays in verification and post-audit
activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor, as chairperson of the GFPS (a) ensure
full implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure that different concerns of women and
men are addressed equally and equitably; (b) direct the GFPS Technical Working
Group (TWG) to: (i) notify the DILG in writing of any changes in endorsed GPB and
request a substitute endorsement; (ii) administer HGDG test to validate GAD
attribution for PPAs; and (iii) support the GAD AR with a brief summary of the
reported PPAs, copies of reported policy issuances, and actions taken by the City on
the COA audit findings and recommendations; and (c) require the CAO, CBO and
GFPS to assign RC codes in accordance with the existing regulation to facilitate timely
generation of GUR.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the CA and the CT to maintain
their consistent compliance with BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of
appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof to the BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CA, CT, and CHRMO to
(a) continuously observe the withholding of premiums, loan amortizations, and other
deductions, and improve the timely and full remittance of these amounts to the
respective government agencies, including GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG;
(b) establish coordination among relevant offices to facilitate the processing of
documents necessary for the completeness of remittances, proper recording of
transactions, and timely transmission of DVs to ensure the prompt remittance to the
concerned agencies; and (c) facilitate the immediate remittance of any outstanding
amounts to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT to submit a report
to the CA on unreleased checks as of year-end for appropriate adjustment in the
latter’s books or for cancellation of the corresponding DVs and checks; (b) CA to (i)
refrain from processing claims with insufficient SDs to prevent delays in the
submission of DVs and their SDs to the AT; and (ii) evaluate the staffing pattern of
236
19.8. We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT to submit a report
to the CA on unreleased checks as of year-end for appropriate adjustment in the
latter’s books or for cancellation of the corresponding DVs and checks; (b) CA to (i)
refrain from processing claims with insufficient SDs to prevent delays in the
submission of DVs and their SDs to the AT; and (ii) evaluate the staffing pattern of
the CAO and consider requesting additional personnel to assist in the preparation and
processing of claim documents; and (iii) immediately submit the source documents,
liquidation documents, and financial reports enumerated in the preceding tables to
the AT for verification.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the City Mayor require the (a)
BAC and TWG members to submit to the AT copies of approved contracts and POs
together with their SDs, within five working days from execution to facilitate timely
review and other necessary audit procedures; and (b) CGSO Officer to promptly
notify the AT, through submission of copies of IARs and SDs, within 24 hours of
accepting procured projects to ensure proper monitoring and compliance.

We recommended and the City Mayor concurred to require the (a) persons
liable to cause the settlement of the suspensions and disallowances deemed final and
executory; and (b) CA to (i) record in the books of accounts the issuance and
settlements of the suspensions, disallowances and charges that have become final and
executory as contained in the NFD; and (ii) maintain and properly update the
subsidiary ledgers/records for each official/employee determined to be
liable/responsible for the amount disallowed/charged/suspended.

1.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) instruct the (i) City
Treasurer (CT) to thoroughly verify the accuracy of the Schedule of Unreleased
Checks; and (ii) City Accountant (CA) to revert the 54 unreleased checks, totaling
₱1,857,177.19, to ensure the accurate reporting of CIB and AP accounts at year-end;
and (b) direct the CT to require all collecting officers and deputized BTs to strictly
adhere to the daily reporting and remittance of collections, ensuring these are
recorded in the appropriate accounting period.

2.10. We recommended that the LCE: (a) instruct the CA to coordinate with the
COA Central Office to inquire about the status of the cash advances for confidential
expenses of the former LCE; (b) require the former LCE to submit a written
explanation for failure to liquidate said cash advances; and (c) direct the City Legal
Office (CLO) Officer to initiate appropriate legal measures to locate other AOs and
demand the immediate liquidation of their outstanding cash advances.

3.6. We recommended that the LCE direct the CGSO Officer and the CA to
regularly reconcile their records and coordinate effectively to ensure that inventory
reports are submitted promptly, facilitating the timely and accurate recording of
Inventory account balances.

4.7. We recommended that the LCE require the CA and the CGSO Officer to (a)
continuously reconcile their property records to ensure the accuracy of the PPE
account balances and safeguard the City’s properties against loss; and
(b) make the necessary adjustments in their respective PPE records to address the
discrepancy of ₱2,029,957.30.

5.8. We recommended that the LCE (a) continue the substantial implementation of
PPAs funded by NGAs; (b) promptly address the reasons for the non-
implementation of the three PPAs to facilitate their immediate realization; and (c)
direct the CA to consistently ensure the completeness of supporting documents
before processing payments.

6.18. We recommended that the LCE require the CT and CA to (a) continuously
comply with the regulations on the withholding of taxes from income payments,
premiums/contributions, and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of City
officials and employees, and remittance thereof together with the employer share
within the prescribed period; and (b) exert their best efforts to determine the prior
years’ unreconciled balances.

7.17. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CAssO to extend efforts in
determining the real properties not yet declared by the respective owners or
administrators to levy and collect the corresponding taxes therefrom; (b) CBldgO to
transmit the copies of issued permits and certificates of registration to the City
Assessor within 30 days from issuance thereof to achieve a complete and accurate
assessment of buildings, machinery, and improvements subject to RPT; (c) CA to
recognize the SET Receivables for the year 2024 and observe the prompt recording
of receivables from RPT and SET every end of the year; and (d) CT to coordinate
efforts with the CAssO in reconciling the records on RPUs to facilitate the timely
submission of the List of Delinquencies and accurate recognition of RPT/ SET
receivables in the financial statements at the end of the year.

8.10. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the: (a) LCE require the CT
and BPLO Head to: (i) coordinate efforts to address the identified issues that led to
the inaccurate assessment and collection of LBTs; and (ii) thoroughly review the
TOPs to ensure that the tax base aligns with the gross sales/receipts declared by the
business taxpayers in their financial statements and BIR returns; and (b) SP
members, in coordination with the CT, ensure that existing local revenue ordinances
are consistent with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and BLGF MC No. 001-2020.

9.15. We recommended that the LCE advise the CA to: (a) obtain from the CHRMO
the balance of cumulated leave credits and record the corresponding Leave Benefits
Payable and Leave Benefits Expenses for the leaves of absence earned by the City’s
employees; (b) coordinate with the CHRMO to comply with the disclosure
requirements under IPSASs 1 and 39 by providing adequate information on employee
benefits expenses and relevant policies on employee benefits in the NFS; and (c)
record the payments of terminal leave benefits under the correct account
classification in accordance with the RCALGU.

10.9. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE advise the LDC
to: (a) prioritize the implementation of development priority programs that are
procurement-and-implementation-ready for the budget year through comprehensive
planning and assessments before approving the AIP; and (b) include complete details
of each PPA in the annual budget as well as their specific, major, and immediate
outputs in the AIP to establish clear performance metrics for effective program
evaluation.

11.30. We recommended that the LCE: (a) ensure optimal utilization of the 5%
LDRRMF through strict implementation of the disaster-related programs including
the timebound procurement of DRRM equipment; (b) instruct the LDRRMC to
coordinate with the CBO Officer in monitoring the availability of funds for DRRM
programs; and (c) require the CA to: (i) make the necessary adjusting entries and
record the cost of welfare goods issued/distributed during the CY 2024 pursuant to
the accounting guidelines and RCA for LGUs; (ii) facilitate the preparation of the
Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and (iii) coordinate with the CT and
accomplish the actual transfer of unutilized LDRRMF to the STF at year-end thus,
achieve reconciliation of the Trust Liability-DRRM account with the CIB-LCCA
account and present an accurate balance of the unexpended LDRRMF in the
financial statements.

12.21. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) City HRMO Chief to: (i) assess
the manpower and operational needs of each department and ensure that positions
essential to the office mandates are properly filled to avoid redundancy of functions;
(ii) evaluate the qualifications and performance of JO workers for possible
appointment to vacant plantilla positions; (iii) submit to the AT the appointment
papers of the identified JO workers; (iv) ensure that the SADFs accurately reflect
only the specific duties and functions actually performed by JO workers; and (v)
review and strengthen the policies on the recruitment and engagement of outsourced
personnel, in accordance with existing rules and regulations on the hiring of
consultants and JO workers; and (b) CA to provide the AT with the complete
documentary requirements for the payments to the highly technical consultants and
grants of CNA incentives to contractual employees to establish the validity, necessity,
and propriety of the disbursements.

13.6. We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS)
members to consistently review all GAD-related regulations for enhanced planning
and implementation processes for GAD PPAs.

14.6. We recommended that the LCE send demand letters to the persons liable to
enforce the settlement of the remaining balance of audit disallowances which became
final and executory.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MA to consistently and promptly prepare BRSs to ensure the fair presentation of the CIB-LCCA accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE (a) direct the GSO Officer and the IC to conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the RPCPPE and corresponding PPE account balances; and (b) instruct the MA to: (i) prepare adjusting entries to address the understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation on Infrastructure Asset accounts and misclassification of PPE accounts; and (ii) strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2003-007 and 2015-009, particularly on the application of the estimated useful life of PPE and the prescribed account classification.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) request the SP through the SB to expedite the review of the MRC for updating in accordance with RA No. 7160; (b) require the BPLO and MTO to: (i) strengthen their reconciliation process to address the discrepancies in business permit and tax records; (ii) strictly enforce the implementation of the business permit application procedures to ensure that businesses are fully compliant with both tax payment and permit requirements; (iii) provide a robust tracking system that includes all tax payments, including those made without the corresponding permits; and (c) require the MTO to attach a copy of the ITR or additional supporting documentation as proof of gross receipts to support the computation of tax payments.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continue to make optimal use of the 20% DF for planned development PPAs; and (b) direct MDC members to ensure that all programmed PPAs are clearly identified in the AIP to guarantee full utilization of allocated funds and prevent excessive allocation.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO Head and the MA to: (a) ensure that salary payments to JO/COS personnel are supported by verified ARs; and (b) to conduct periodic assessment of internal controls to ensure their continued effectiveness and adaptability to any changes.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) LDRRMC to (i) prepare a revised LDRRMFIP reflecting all approved realignments, supplemental budgets, and prior year’s unexpended balances, duly supported by LDRRMC recommendations, approved by the SB; (ii) ensure that LDRRMF allocations are strictly limited to eligible, disaster-related expenditures; (b) MA to observe the proper classification of relief goods in the books of accounts; and (c) BAC to strictly adhere to the relevant provisions of the RIRR of RA No. 9184, particularly those governing documentation, posting, and reporting, to uphold transparency, accountability, and compliance in all procurement activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS and Focal Person to continuously implement all GAD-related PPAs and GAD mechanisms to ensure that the intended beneficiaries achieve the desired outcomes, promoting gender equality and empowerment, and that the benefits are fully realized to improve their quality of life.

We recommended that the LCE require the: (a) MA, HRMO Chief and MT to collaborate in identifying the details of prior years' discrepancies and unreconciled balances in the Due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG accounts, through coordination with these agencies and verifying prior years' records, setting a clear timeline for the verification process; (b) the HRMO to promptly provide GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG with updated information regarding the employment status of officials, employees, and newly hired personnel to ensure that withheld amounts align with the agencies’ billing statements; and (c) the HRMO and MTO to establish better coordination and reconciliation of their records, including setting a timeline for the reconciliation process.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to (a) require the persons liable to immediately refund the disallowed PDAF payment amounting to ₱7,326,550.00 once the disallowance becomes final and executory; and (b) ensure that all succeeding transactions comply with existing laws and regulations to avoid future audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MT and
MA to consistently adopt and sustain sound financial and management practices to
further strengthen internal controls over the management and reporting of cash
accounts.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) provide sufficient budget and manpower
for the conduct of a physical inventory of the Municipality’s existing local roads, to
be led by the Municipal Engineer (ME), to establish accurate recording and
maintenance of relevant records; (b) require the MA and the MT to maintain and
regularly update the PPELCs and PCs, and to ensure the reconciliation of property
records; and (c) direct the MT to maintain the LRIRM and LRNPC, upon submission
by the ME of a report on the physical inventory of local roads, containing complete
descriptions and cost segregation of road components, as basis for recording in the
books of accounts by the MA.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned Municipal officials to:
(a) continuously ensure the prompt and full implementation of NGA-funded PPAs;
and (b) facilitate the return of any unexpended balances to the respective SAs, in
compliance with applicable COA regulations.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, instruct the (a) LSB
officials to submit the approved SEF budget to the MAO, MBO and MTO within the
prescribed period, for timely monitoring; and (b) MA to submit the quarterly and
annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) to the
DepEd, and ROs of the DILG and DBM to fully achieve and promote transparency
and accountability in the utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the LCE continuously improve and enhance the
Municipality’s development planning and project prioritization to ensure maximum
compliance with the prescribed criteria on the appropriation and utilization of the
20% DF, and to support the substantial implementation of planned PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MDRRM Office (MDRRMO)
Chief (a) to sustain the optimal utilization of the DRRMF for preparedness and
mitigation-related PPAs to achieve a disaster-resilient LGU; (b) and the MBO Officer
to strictly refrain from charging ineligible expenditures against the fund, in
adherence to existing laws and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE and the Legislative Office to actively
collaborate in the enactment of an updated and revised MRC to enhance the
Municipality’s fiscal capacity and service delivery to its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MHRMO Officer
to continuously comply with the applicable law, rules, and regulations in the
engagement, management, and payment of salaries for JO personnel.

We recommended that the LCE to instruct the GFPS to: (a) sustain gender
mainstreaming in local policymaking, planning, programming, budgeting,
implementation, monitoring, and evaluation, as mandated by PCW, DILG, DBM and
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; (b) ensure full and efficient implementation of GAD-related
PPAs within the allocated budget by ensuring its judicious utilization; and (c)
establish and maintain a centralized GAD database with reliable gender statistics and
sex-disaggregated data to strengthen planning, budgeting, programming and policy
formulation.

We recommended that the LCE continue to observe the withholding of taxes,
premiums, and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of Municipal officials
and employees, and timely remittance of the same to the BIR, GSIS, HDMF and
PhilHealth, in accordance with the provisions of RR No. 11-2018 and RA Nos. 8291,
9679 and 11223.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to be more diligent in
evaluating the supporting papers on claims to avoid suspensions or disallowances in
audit.

1.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require (a) the MA
to: (i) promptly recognize in the books all book reconciling items; and (ii) determine
the details of the unreconciled balance of ₱2,631.52 for appropriate adjustment in the
books; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer (MT) and the Municipal Budget Office
(MBO) Officer to closely monitor budgeted expenditures and revenues vis-a-vis
actual amounts to prevent the occurrence of cash deficits or overdrafts.

1.16. Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

2.22. We recommended that the LCE to instruct the (a) MA to: (i) trace the
discrepancies between the reported acquisition costs of the PPE accounts and the
lapsing schedule for depreciation to address the noted discrepancies; (ii) recognize in
the books the donated properties, including the vehicles received from the Supreme
Court, upon receipt of complete documentation to ensure accurate and complete
recognition of government assets; and (iii) ensure the maintenance of complete and
updated PPELCs for each class of property; (b) the General Services Office
(GSO)/Supply and Property Unit Officer to: (i) maintain and regularly update PCs
for all Municipal assets; (ii) coordinate with the MA and Inventory Committee (IC)
to facilitate the regular reconciliation of accounting and property records; (iii)
immediately secure and account for all unserviceable and abandoned vehicles to
prevent further damage, theft, or misuse; (c) IC to complete the physical inventory of
PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) and AT;
and (d) Disposal Committee to expedite the preparation of the IIRUP for all
unserviceable assets to facilitate their immediate disposal.

2.23. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): (a) strengthen
property management systems to ensure the proper safekeeping of government assets
and prevent undetected losses; and (b) facilitate the implementation of the “One-
Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006
to ensure the fair presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.

3.8. We recommended that the LCE require the MA, in coordination with other
concerned Municipal Offices to identify the original source agencies of the dormant
balances totaling ₱3,344,121.62 through all available means, including historical
records and coordination with relevant NGAs and LGUs. Should these efforts prove
unsuccessful, prepare and submit the necessary documentation to the Commission on
Audit (COA) to formally request for the write-off of untraceable fund balances in

the accumulation of discrepancies and ensure compliance with tax regulations.
