respective sources; (b) MA to properly classify the funds received from various NGAs
for implementation of specific programs or projects to the Due to NGAs account; and
(c) MA and OIC – MT to remit the balances of the prior years’ fund transfers to the
source agencies or the National Treasury as appropriate.

8.6. We recommended that the LCE require the OIC, MT and MA to ensure full
compliance with tax laws and regulations. Specifically, all taxes withheld from
compensation and supplier payments must be remitted within ten days after the end
of the month in which the withholding was made, as prescribed by existing BIR
regulations.

9.6. We recommended that the LCE require the OIC, MT and MA to: (a)
continuously comply with regulations on the withholding of premiums/contributions
and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of Municipality officials and
employees, and ensure timely remittance thereof together with the employer’s share
within the prescribed period; and (b) exert best efforts to determine and reconcile the
prior years’ unreconciled balance.

10.7. We recommended that the LCE require the OIC, MT and MA to: (a) ensure
consistent and timely remittance of employee and employer shares to PHIC; and (b)
coordinate with the HRMO to exert their best efforts in determining the details of the
prior years’ unremitted balance of ₱98,266.97, to facilitate its immediate remittance to PHIC.

11.6. We recommended that the LCE require the OIC, MT and MA to: (a) maintain
consistent and timely remittance of both employee and employer contributions,
including loan amortizations, within the prescribed period; and (b) coordinate with
the HRMO Chief to exert best efforts in determining the details of the prior years’
unremitted balance of ₱250,635.95 to facilitate its immediate remittance to HDMF.

12.17. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Assessor to: (i)
identify and appraise undeclared real properties; and (ii) reassess properties with
FMVs recorded as ₱1.00 to reflect accurate market values; (b) Municipal Engineer,
acting as the Building Official, to transmit the copies of issued permits and
certificates of registration to the Municipal Assessor within 30 days from issuance
thereof to facilitate timely assessments; (c) OIC, MT to: (i) expedite quarterly
computation of RPT shares for the component barangays to ensure the prompt
distribution of the proceeds which will support barangay operations; and (ii)
coordinate efforts with the Municipal Assessor in reconciling the records on RPUs
to facilitate the timely submission of the List of Delinquencies and accurate
recognition of RPT and SET receivables in the financial statements at the end of the
year; and (d) MA to observe the complete recording of receivables from RPT and
SET every end of the year.

13.14. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the (a) LCE require the OIC,
MT and BPLO Head to (i) coordinate efforts in resolving the identified
concerns/issues that caused the inaccurate assessment and collection of LBTs; (ii)
review all TOPs thoroughly to ensure that the tax base conforms to the gross
sales/receipts declared by the business taxpayers in their financial statements and BIR
returns; and (iii) conduct regular training for MTO and BPLO personnel on tax laws,
classifications, and e-BPLS operations; and (b) SB members, in coordination with the
OIC, MT (i) review and update existing revenue ordinances to ensure compliance
with RA No. 7160 and BLGF MC No. 001-2020; (ii) regularly monitor the
implementation of business tax administration policies; and (iii) recommend system
enhancements to the e-BPLS to minimize assessment errors.

14.15. We recommended that the LCE advise the MA (a) to obtain from the MHRMO
the balance of cumulated leave credits and record the corresponding Leave Benefits
Payable and Leave Benefits Expenses for the leaves of absence earned by the
Municipality’s employees; (b) in coordination with the MHRMO, comply with the
disclosure requirements under IPSASs 1, and 39 and provide adequate information on
employee benefits expenses and relevant policies on employee benefits in the NFS;
and (c) submit the requisite documentary requirements on payments for terminal
leave benefits to substantiate the authority and validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant (MA) to: (a) make the necessary adjusting journal entries for the
unrecorded reconciling items totaling ₱51,018.77 and for the reversion of stale checks
amounting to ₱7,002.91; and (b) ensure that all reconciling items identified in the
BRSs are promptly and accurately recognized in the books to reflect the true cash
position and related liabilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to: (a) reclassify the guaranty
deposits from “Financial Assets Available for Sale” account to “Financial Assets –
Others”; and (b) conduct a thorough review and investigation to determine the origin,
nature, and current status of the reported Investment in Stocks and Deposits on
Letter of Credits, and ensure proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements
(NFS).

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT to: (i) maintain SCs for all
inventory items; and (ii) prepare and submit the RIS and SSMI to the MAO within
the prescribed deadlines; and (b) MA to: (i) maintain and regularly update the SLCs
for all inventory items; (ii) consistently record inventory items under their respective
asset accounts; (iii) implement the PIM in accordance with the MNGAS for LGUs
and IPSAS 12, ensuring that supplies and materials are initially recorded as inventory
upon receipt and recognized as expense only upon issuance or consumption; and (iv)
in coordination with the RHU and other concerned Offices, submit a distribution list
or supporting documentation showing the actual recipients or usage of medicines to
the AT for verification and audit.

We also recommended that the LCE (a) create an Inventory Committee to
ensure compliance with prescribed procedures for inventory management, including
the conduct of regular physical inventory counts at least semi-annually; (b) allocate a
proper storage facility for supplies and materials to enable centralized receipt,
inspection, and safekeeping of inventory prior to issuance; and (c) designate and train
personnel specifically responsible for inventory control, documentation, and
reporting to ensure adherence to established inventory procedures and accountability
requirements.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to: (i) reclassify the
infrastructure-related disbursements charged under RME and adjust the affected
accounts accordingly to reflect the accurate PPE balance in the books; and (ii) ensure
prompt derecognition of disposed assets from the books to avoid overstatement of
account balances; (b) PADC to expedite the completion of the disposal process for all
unserviceable properties; (c) MA and MT to: (i) accelerate reconciliation efforts
between accounting records and results of physical inventory; and (ii) maintain
complete and updated PCs and PPELCs for effective monitoring and reconciliation
of PPEs, respectively; and (d) Inventory Committee to: (i) complete the annual
physical count of PPE to establish the accuracy and existence of PPE account
balances; and (ii) ensure all insurable assets are covered by appropriate insurance
with the GSIS to indemnify for the value of government properties lost through theft
or any unforeseen events.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to: (a) conduct a thorough
review of all dormant liabilities and submit a detailed validation report for each
liability account; (b) send written notices to identified claimants or concerned
agencies to confirm validity, request liquidation, or coordinate for reversion if no
claim is forthcoming; (c) initiate derecognition or reversion of accounts pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2023-008 duly supported with relevant documents; and (d)
reclassify amounts totaling ₱88,002.64 from Accounts Payable to Terminal Leave
Benefits Payable to ensure accurate financial reporting in accordance with applicable
accounting standards, and submit the journal entry vouchers (JEV), Trial Balance,
and GL showing the corrected account classification.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development (MPD) Officer to: (i) ensure that identified priority PPAs are
implemented within the year to maximize the utilization of public funds and improve
delivery of services to constituents; (ii) coordinate with implementing Offices to
ensure that appropriated funds are aligned with implementable and well-planned
PPAs; and (iii) in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO),
recommend necessary adjustments in budget execution to avoid idle funds and ensure
timely delivery of priority programs and services; (b) MA to: (i) perform a thorough
review of financial records to correct the misclassification of Advances to
Contractors, Guaranty/Security Payable, and PPE accounts, and ensure that the
appropriate accounts reflect accurate balances as at year-end; (ii) ensure that all
supporting documents are complete, valid, and in compliance with relevant laws and
regulations before payment to contractors is processed; and (iii) establish a systematic
process for filing and organizing supporting documents in DVs to ensure they are
easily accessible for audit; and ME to: (i) require concerned contractors to undertake
necessary repairs or corrective works during the defect liability period at no
additional cost to the Municipality; and (ii) strengthen project monitoring and quality
assurance procedures during construction to ensure compliance with technical
specifications and standards.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to: (i) identify and refund
all balances from prior years’ completed PPAs to the concerned NGAs; (ii) reclassify
misclassified entries to the appropriate accounts; (iii) ensure that all completed
infrastructure projects are properly and timely recognized as PPE ; (iv) ensure that
all supporting documents are complete, valid, and in compliance with relevant laws
and regulations before payment to contractors and suppliers is processed; and (v)
establish a systematic process for submission and organizing supporting documents
in DVs to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the submitted documents; and (b)
BAC Chairperson to ensure that all procurements under the alternative mode are
accompanied by a BAC resolution recommending the mode of procurement and a
declaration of the state of calamity, in case of emergency procurement.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) LDRRM Officer to: (i) prepare
a comprehensive and detailed LDRRMFIP which includes the specific PPAs,
timelines, and a separate section for PPAs funded by prior years’ unexpended
balances; and (ii) prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on a
monthly basis and submit the same to the MA for certification; (b) MA to: (i) submit
the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to the AT on or before the 15th
day following the end of each month; (ii) transfer the unexpended LDRRMF under
the account TL - DRRMF in the TF books at year-end; and (iii) ensure that the
necessary documents supporting payment claims are valid and complete before
payments are made; and (c) BAC to: (i) immediately submit missing procurement
documents on noted transactions; (ii) ensure that all procurement stages are
documented; and (iii) require suppliers to submit requisite documents such as
Mayor’s Permit, Tax Returns, and Omnibus Sworn Statement.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA, in coordination with the
Municipal Human Resource and Management (MHRM) Officer, to (a) strictly
enforce compliance with the remittance schedules set by the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG,
and PhilHealth; (b) prioritize the reconciliation with the GSIS and settlement of the
remaining unremitted balance of ₱879,359.41, and ensure that any resulting
deficiencies are addressed promptly; (c) maintain and regularly reconcile subsidiary
ledgers to monitor individual member accounts and ensure alignment between
withheld and remitted amounts; and (d) identify and address root causes of delays,
including payroll adjustments and documentation issues, to prevent future
discrepancies.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MT to: (i) coordinate with the
Local Finance Committee, initiate the review and updating of the LRC, and present the
revised LRC to the Sangguniang Bayan for deliberation and enactment; (ii) issue a
written certification of Real Property Tax receivables at the beginning of the year to
support the journal entry to be prepared by the MAO; (iii) coordinate with the MAO to
conduct a reconciliation of revenue accounts, and ensure that the amounts reflected in
the SRE are aligned with the MAO records; (iv) ensure the retention and proper
storage of all necessary documents required for business permit applications and
renewals; and (v) submit a copy of the proof of advance payment by the lessee for the
leased public market; and (b) take immediate legal actions to initiate contract
termination procedures and explore alternative arrangements to ensure the productive
use of the public market and safeguard the Municipality’s interest.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MHRM Officer to (a) develop and
implement a standardized recruitment and selection process for JOs, Casuals, and
COS personnel, with clear guidelines on qualifications, evaluation criteria, and
documentation; (b) ensure that the hiring of JOs, Casuals, and COS personnel is
based on necessity of services, qualifications and performance; (c) ensure that the
contracts of JOs, Casuals, and COS clearly outline their specific duties and
responsibilities; (d) conduct a comprehensive personnel needs assessment across all
Municipal Offices to align human resource deployment with actual operational
requirements; (e) review the ARs submitted by JOs, Casuals, and COS personnel and
ensure that outputs reported thereon are quantifiable and with details to substantiate
the rendition of service; and (f) enforce complete biometric logging, including during
lunch breaks, to ensure accurate attendance monitoring and timekeeping records.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the following officials to comply
with the aforementioned COA Circulars: (i) BAC Chairperson and Secretariat to
furnish the AT with copies of contracts, POs, and supporting documents within five
days from execution; (ii) MT to submit to the AT copies of delivery documents within
24 hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services; and (iii) MA to submit
complete sets DVs and ORs within 10 days after the end of each month; and (b)
require the aforementioned officials to implement a more efficient document
submission and review system to address the delays and improve internal controls to
ensure full compliance with the COA Circulars.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to (a)
coordinate with the MPD Officer in establishing a comprehensive GAD database for
a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; (b) ensure
close monitoring of the implementation of PPAs embodied in the GPB to achieve the
intended GAD objectives; and (c) conduct a thorough gender analysis using HGDG
tool to validate the attribution of the PPAs to GAD.

1.10. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the
(a) MT and MA to: (i) consistently adhere to the guidelines for reporting and
accounting of collections and deposits under existing regulations; (ii) continuously
collaborate and reconcile their respective records to promptly identify and rectify any
errors; and (b) MA to continuously comply with the timely preparation of BRS and
adjustment of any reconciling items.

2.17. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Inventory Committee
to: (i) conduct complete annual physical count of all PPE; (ii) use the correct format
of RPCPPE with complete details to facilitate reconciliation of the results with MAO
records; (iii) coordinate with the GSO and MEO in preparing and submitting the
complete inventory of all Local Roads, the corresponding Road Map and the
RPCLRN; and (iv) conduct an investigation to determine the whereabouts of the
unlocated missing PPE items during the physical inventory for appropriate action;
and (b) direct the MA to: (i) draw the necessary entries for the unrecognized PPE for
accurate presentation of the account in the financial statements; (ii) continue
coordinating with the MEO for the retrieval of the details and inspection of the
projects lodged in the CIP account for the reclassification to its proper PPE account,
if warranted; (iii) maintain complete PPELCs, including LRNLC; and (iv) provide
the required disclosure in the NFS for the Road Network account.

3.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to recognize the
accrued liability from earned leave benefits at year-end; and (b) HRMO Officer to
regularly furnish the MAO a summary or report on earned leave credits.

4.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to: (i) recognize the
appropriate asset, revenue, and expense accounts in the TF books upon receipt and
utilization of funds from other agencies arising from non-exchange transactions; and
(ii) restate the Comparative CSFPer of the ensuing year to reflect the recognition of
revenues and expenses.

5.8. We recommended and the LCE agreed to: (a) instruct all implementing
departments to closely monitor the implementation of PPAs to ensure their timely
execution and completion; (b) direct the MA to: (i) verify the completeness of
supporting documents before processing payments; and (ii) submit the lacking
documents to the AT for further review.

6.13. We recommended and the LCE agreed, (a) as the head of the Municipal
Development Council, to direct its members to: (i) continue adhering to the proper
allocation and utilization of the 20% DF; and (ii) ensure the implementation and
completion of all programmed PPAs within the set timeline to facilitate the prompt
realization of benefits for the intended beneficiaries; (b) direct the (i) Municipal
Engineer to submit the noted documentary deficiencies to the AT for further review;
and (ii) MA to verify the completeness of supporting documents before processing
payments.

7.19. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MDRRMC to: (i)
improve accuracy in budgeting by aligning PPA cost estimates with actual needs; and
(ii) reprogram and prioritize the use of unexpended DRRMF balances; and (b) MA
to (i) continue to adhere to the relevant rules and regulations on the utilization,
accounting, reporting and transfer of the unexpended balance to the TL-DRRMF
account; and (ii) ensure that the necessary documents are valid and complete before
payment is made to suppliers.

8.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to ensure the continuous (a) adherence
to the applicable rules and regulations on personnel management; and (b) submission of
the minimum documentary requirements set forth under Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

9.10. We reiterated the prior year recommendation that the LCE (a) exhaust all
possible means to enforce the collection of real property tax delinquencies, including
public awareness campaigns, and streamlining process, among others; and (b)
coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) members to review existing local policies and regulations, identify areas for
improvement. and implement reforms to strengthen local tax administration.
9.11. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MTO to provide
the MAO a copy of Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies as of year-end;
and (b) MAO to reconcile and make the necessary adjustments to the RPT Receivable
and SET Receivable accounts.

10.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the
amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/posted to ensure correctness of data and
avoid discrepancies; and (b) practice intact remittance of taxes withheld to BIR
within the prescribed period to avoid the incurring surcharges and penalties to be
charged against the erring employees.

11.12. We reiterated the prior year's recommendation, and the LCE agreed to direct
the MA to: (a) regularly conduct reconciliations of records with GSIS, HDMF, and
PHIC personnel to promptly address any unremitted premiums for Municipality
members; and (b) ensure the remittance of withheld amounts within the prescribed
period to avoid penalties that may be imposed by these agencies.

12.7. We recommended, and the LCE agreed, to require the GAD Focal Point Person
to: (a) continue complying with the budgetary requirement of allocating at least 5%
of the Municipality’s total budget appropriation for GAD PPAs; (b) prioritize the
implementation of the identified PPAs as per the GPB to promote a gender-responsive
society; and (c) fully utilize the GAD budget to achieve the objectives for which the
funds were appropriated.

1.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) designate
sufficient disbursing officers to manage the volume of programs and minimize the
noted lapses in the granting and liquidation of CAs; (b) require the AOs to: (i) strictly
observe the liquidation deadlines; (ii) ensure liquidations are supported with proper
and complete documentation; (c) refrain from granting CAs for infrastructure-
related projects; and (d) require the Municipal Accountant (MA) to: (i) refrain from
granting additional CAs without prior liquidation; and (ii) strictly adhere to the
existing rules and regulations in the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs.

2.12. We recommended that the LCE (a) direct the MA to adopt the Perpetual
Inventory Method for the recognition of inventories in the books and ensure the
maintenance of corresponding SLCs; (b) provide adequate storage space to enable
full control and monitoring of inventories; (c) direct the GSO to maintain stock cards,
consolidate RIS weekly, prepare the SSMI, and ensure reconciliation of records with
the MAO; (d) augment the manpower of the GSO to enable it to effectively perform
its duties and responsibilities; and (e) require the GSO to conduct regular physical
counts of inventories and prepare the RPCI for submission to COA and reconciliation
with accounting records.

3.18. We recommended that the LCE: (a) establish an Inventory Committee (IC)
responsible for conducting a physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and for
preparing the RPCPPE for CY 2024; (b) augment the personnel of the MAO and
GSO to ensure effective control, management, and maintenance of complete and
updated property records; (c) direct the GSO Chief to submit to the AT copies of CTs,
LTO registrations for motor vehicles, and AREs; (d) instruct the MA to adjust the
books by reclassifying semi-expendable properties incorrectly recognized as PPE; (e)
recognize in the books the 33 parcels of land with a total assessed value of
₱42,815,600.00; and (f) create a special committee to investigate the status of the 33
parcels of land covered by TDs under the Municipality’s name to facilitate their
titling.

4.13. We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA (a) to coordinate with the
MHRMO to obtain accurate balances of cumulated leave credits and recognize the
corresponding Leave Benefits Payable, Leave Benefits Expenses and Prior Period
Adjustment account for leave credits earned by Municipal employees in the current
and previous years; and (b) ensure that the NFS include adequate disclosure of
employee benefits expenses, relevant policies, and other relevant information in
compliance with IPSASs 1 and 39.

5.30. We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MT to: (i) prepare a stratified
schedule of PIL per tax period; (ii) conduct a regular examination of the books of
accounts and financial records of business establishments to verify the accuracy of
declared income; (iii) identify and reassess taxpayers with underdeclared gross
receipts, and tag them accordingly for monitoring; (iv) coordinate with the ME to
identify construction-related facilities operating within the Municipality that qualify
as project offices; and (v) enforce tax collection from external contractors per situs
rules; (b) BPLO to: (i) ensure submission and retention of complete SDs to permit
renewals; and (ii) implement a standard, notarized format for sworn declaration of
gross sales; and (c) MT and BPLO Head to devise measures to validate income
declaration, especially from businesses with direct transactions with the Municipality
and barangays.

6.17. We commended the LCE for appropriately providing a budget and
implementing the PPAs included in the AIP pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.
01. However, we recommended that the LCE (a) require the BAC to: (i) strictly adopt
competitive bidding in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects; and (ii)
ensure the timely submission of all procurement contracts and SDs to the AT; and (b)
require the MA and the MT to submit the requisite documents noted and ensure that
disbursements are supported with approved, complete, and properly filled-out
documentation.

7.12. We recommended that the (a) LCE instruct the HRMO Officer to: (i) require
the appearance of the 58 unverified COS personnel for validation; (ii) ensure that all
initial salary payments for COS personnel are supported by a certification stating
that the services cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel of the
Municipality; and (iii) ensure that ARs are prepared and submitted by the COS
personnel; and (b) Sangguniang Bayan Secretary submit a copy of the contract of
service for the engagement of consultancy to the AT.

8.11. We recommended that the LCE continue to be compliant with the (a) pertinent
provisions of RA No. 10121, on enhancing the capacities of the Municipality and its
constituents to effectively understand, prepare for, respond to, and recover from the
impacts of natural and man-made disasters; and (b) the relevant sections of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

9.13. We recommended that the LCE: (a) mobilize additional GAD resources, if
necessary, to ensure the full implementation of all programmed PPAs within the year;
and (b) direct the GFPS Secretariat to: (i) maintain and regularly update the GAD
database to effectively identify relevant gender issues and appropriate PPAs; and (ii)
ensure the collection and utilization of sufficient sex-disaggregated data to support
the preparation of GAD AR in compliance with the provisions of PCW-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

10.4. We recommended that the LCE direct the MA, MT, and HRMO Officer to:
(a) ensure the continuous and proper withholding of premiums, loan amortizations,
and other deductions, and improve the timely and full remittance of these amounts to
GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG, and other concerned agencies; (b) coordinate efforts to
identify and reconcile unremitted balances for immediate remittance to: (i) avoid
penalties and charges, which should be borne by the responsible Municipal officials;
and (ii) protect the benefits of affected Municipal employees; and (c) strengthen
coordination to improve record-keeping, ensure accurate transaction recording, and
facilitate the timely transmission of disbursement vouchers (DVs) for prompt and
accurate remittance.

11.4. We commended the efforts of the MA and the MT for their consistent
compliance with BIR regulations on the proper withholding and timely remittance of
taxes. We further recommended that the MA and MT exert their best efforts to
retrieve and examine previous years’ records to identify the details of the ₱133,289.74
in unremitted withheld taxes to facilitate its immediate remittance to the BIR.

12.6. We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the (a) BAC and
TWG members to submit to the AT copies of approved construction contracts and
POs together with their SDs within five working days from execution to facilitate
timely review and other necessary audit procedures; and (b) GSO to promptly notify
the AT and submit delivery receipts and SDs within 24 hours of accepting procured
projects to ensure proper monitoring and compliance.

13.6. We reiterated that the LCE direct the MA to: (a) require the concerned liable
individuals to settle the disallowed amounts in compliance with Section 7.1 of the
RRSA; (b) closely monitor the status of the appeal before the Commission Proper
(CP); and (c) recognize in the books the receivables arising from the disallowance
amounting to ₱484,500.00, in accordance with Section 7(b) of the RRSA.

1.20. We recommended that the LCE: (a) require the IC and the GSO, in
coordination with the MEO, to: (i) conduct a complete inventory of all PPE items,
including land, public infrastructure assets, and buildings; and (ii) update the Property Cards to
include complete acquisition details and descriptions of PPE items; (ii) institutionalize
periodic reconciliation of property records and the results of physical inventory of PPE
with the MAO’s PPE records; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant (MA) to: (i) prepare
adjusting entries to correct the accounting errors that resulted in the understatement of the
CIP account and overstatement of the corresponding PPE accounts and Accounts Payable
(AP) account; (ii) ensure that all infrastructure costs are initially recognized under the CIP
account and transferred to PPE only upon project completion and acceptance; (iii)
strengthen coordination with the MEO to secure timely and accurate status reports,
including percentage of completion and actual cost incurred as of year-end; (c) create a
Special Committee to: (i) gather all available ownership documents to facilitate titling of
parcels of land purportedly owned by the Municipality and allocate the necessary budget
for titling expenses; and (ii) request appraisal services from the Bureau of Internal Revenue
(BIR) or accredited appraisers to determine the fair value of donated properties to facilitate
recognition of their proper recognition of these in the books of accounts.

2.8. We recommend that the LCE require the MA, in coordination with other
concerned Municipal Offices to identify the source agencies of the ₱931,528.32
balances through all available means, including historical records and coordination
with relevant NGAs. If after exhausting all efforts and the sources remain
unidentifiable, prepare and submit the necessary documentation to the Commission
on Audit (COA) to formally request for the write-off of untraceable balances in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2023-008.

3.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MT and MA to: (a)
continuously comply with the prescribed guidelines on the preparation, reporting and
accounting of collections and deposits; and (b) coordinate with the Municipal
Information and Computer Technology Office (MICTO) or the Service Provider to
strengthen the E-GAPS by ensuring the inclusion of field collections and conducting
regular training for system users to fully utilize the features of E-GAPS and thereby
generate more reliable and accurate financial reports.

4.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and MT to ensure
strict and continuous compliance with the withholding of appropriate taxes and their
timely remittance within the prescribed period.

5.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA and the Human
Resource Management Office (HRMO) Chief to: (a) continue to withhold and remit
the required monthly contributions and loan payments to the GSIS; (b) trace and
reconcile the discrepancies between the GSIS billing and the actual payroll records to
facilitate the immediate remittance of ₱391,394.67 to the GSIS; and (c) henceforth,
ensure that employees' records in the GSIS system are promptly updated to avoid
discrepancies in the monthly GSIS billing vis-à-vis the actual amount withheld.

6.8. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the HRMO Chief and the
MA to: (a) continue to comply with regulations on the withholding and remitting
premiums and loan payments within the prescribed period; and (b) reassess and
improve the payroll processing and recording to identify areas for improvement to
minimize delays and reduce inconsistencies.

7.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the HRMO Chief to: (a)
promptly update PhilHealth regarding any changes or increases in the salaries of
officials and employees to ensure that the amounts withheld are properly aligned with
the amounts remitted; (b) verify and ensure the accuracy of the employee list uploaded
in the EPRS; and (c) cross-check the premium amounts due per employee, as reflected
in the SPA, against the actual amounts withheld per payroll to identify and resolve any
discrepancies.

8.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue the implementation of the
programmed PPAs as planned, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer (ME), to ensure the delivery of
intended socio-economic benefits to the public.

9.11. We recommended and the LCE agreed to: (a) ensure compliance with the
provisions of RA No. 10121 to achieve a disaster-resilient community continuously;
(b) require the MA to make the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the
TL – DRRMF as of year-end; and (c) strengthen internal controls over the encoding
and classification of expenses in financial reports by conducting regular reviews of
financial reports, implementing validation procedures prior to report submission, and
providing refresher training to personnel responsible for financial data entry to
minimize the risk of similar errors in the future.

10.7. We reiterated our recommendation and the LCE agreed to require the GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS) to ensure continued: (a) compliance with the budgetary
requirement of allocating at least 5% of the Municipality’s total appropriations for
GAD PPAs, as well as the substantial implementation of said PPAs; (b) preparation
of the GPB, duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG; and
(c) utilization of appropriate GAD tools to assess the efficiency and effectiveness of
implemented GAD PPAs.

11.4. We recommended and the LCE, as Chairperson of the LSB, agreed to instruct
the LSB Secretary to: (a) submit copies of the quarterly and annual SEF utilization
reports to the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG using SEF Budget
Accountability Form No. I; and (b) strictly adhere to the posting deadlines for said
reports in accordance with the Full Disclosure Policy.

1.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require: (a) the MA to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the AT to facilitate timely review and verification of transactions; (b) the MT to provide a copy of the CRAAF monthly to the AT; and (c) both the MA and MT to continuously implement and maintain strong internal controls over cash management, in strict compliance with existing rules and regulations.

2.11. We recommended that the LCE direct (a) the MTO to: (i) instruct the RPTIS developer to enhance the system to ensure that the generated CLRPTs includes only RPT and SET collectibles for the year; or, alternatively, provide the MAO with a reliable and accurate manually prepared CLRPTs, reflecting accurate collectibles for the year, to ensure the fair presentation of RPT and SET receivable balances at year-end; and (b) the MA to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recognition of RPT and SET Receivables, in accordance with the aforementioned provisions.

3.4. We recommended that the LCE (a) direct the SO to: (i) prepare and submit the SSMI, supported by RIS, to the MAO regularly for timely recording of issued inventories; (ii) conduct physical count of inventories every semester and submit the RPCI to the MAO for reconciliation and AT for reference in audit; and (iii) notify the AT of all inventory deliveries within 24-hours from receipt to facilitate timely inspection; (b) instruct the MA to prepare adjusting entries to recognize the cost of issued inventories; and (c) allocate sufficient resources and manpower to support the reconciliation and physical inventory counting.

4.23. We recommended that the LCE (a) temporarily reassign or relieve the members of the IC from their regular workload to allow them to prioritize and complete the annual physical count of PPEs and subsequently prepare the RPCPPE within three months or less; (b) require the MT to: (i) exert all necessary efforts to facilitate the speedy titling of the remaining four parcels of land under the name of the Municipality; (ii) prepare and maintain PCs for each class of PPE; and (iii) ensure that all serviceable MVs of the Municipality have valid LTO registration; (c) require the ADC to: (i) notify the AT of disposal proceedings in a timely manner; and (ii) facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 2024-01 dated January 30, 2024.

5.6. We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to make the necessary adjustments to the CIP account to accurately report the cost of on-going projects as of year-end; (b) MA and ME to closely coordinate and monitor the status of project completion to ensure proper recognition of the CIP account in the books; and (c) ME to require contractors to submit SWA or progress billing at year-end to serve as basis for recording the cost of completed portion of the projects in the books.

6.14. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MSWDO Officer to submit the approved project proposal together with the Specific and General MOA to the AT within five working days from its execution to facilitate timely review of the transactions; and (b) BAC Chairperson and MA to: (i) determine and address the cause of the deficiencies pertaining to supporting documents; and (ii) submit the requisite documents on the noted transactions; and (c) responsible official to ensure that all PPAs are implemented within the specified timeline

7.3. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) BPLO to (i) retain a copy of the sworn declaration of gross sales or receipts from operations submitted by each business taxpayer upon registration and renewal; and (ii) coordinate with the MAO in obtaining the year-end alphalist for verifying and computing business taxes, particularly for establishments with contracts with the Municipality; (b) BPLO and MTO to (i) ensure that all business establishments have paid the required business taxes and issue demand letters to those that failed to renew their business permits; and (ii) strictly enforce compliance with the provisions of the LRC in the assessment and collection of business taxes to promote fairness, accuracy, and transparency in tax administration.

8.15. We recommended that the LCE require the require the BAC Chairperson and Technical Working Group (TWG) members to (a) ensure strict compliance with the pertinent provisions of the Procurement Law, specifically on the review of bid documents and the prescribed periods for each procurement phase, to enhance transparency and efficiency in the procurement process; and (b) submit the lacking bidding documents to the AT.

9.19. We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MDRRMO Chief to (i) ensure that expenditures charged against the LDRRMF remain within the approved appropriation, otherwise, prepare a supplemental budget; (ii) prioritize the implementation of disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs as planned; and (iii) submit the declaration of state of calamity to support the use of QRF; (b) BAC to (i) ensure that public bidding remains the method of procurement unless the use of alternative methods is properly justified and documented; and (ii) submit all missing documents required under alternative mode of procurement outlined in “Annex H” of the Consolidated Guidelines for the Lease of Privately Owned Real Property and Venue; and (c) MA to ensure that all required supporting documents are valid and complete prior to the processing of payments.

10.10. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the immediate supervisor of consultants to ensure that the accomplishments and outputs stated in the ARs are reviewed, validated against actual performance and duly approved prior to the processing of payment; and (b) require the BAC to (i) submit the PhilGEPS registration of the consultants to the AT for further audit; and (ii) subject the procurement of consultancy services to public bidding unless the conditions for the use of the alternative mode of procurement are met.

11.4. We recommended that the LCE direct the MAO and MHRMO to continuously comply with the pertinent laws on the timely withholding and remittance of premiums, contributions, and loan repayments to GSIS, HDMF, and PHIC.

12.4. We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to continue complying with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes from government money payments, to ensure that the government is not deprived of taxes that can be used for the implementation of various programs and projects.

13.7. We recommended that the LCE require the GFPS to exert effort in mobilizing its proponent offices to provide the necessary data to fully utilize its GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation.

14.5. We recommended that the LCE (a) strictly enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances considering that the prescribed periods of settlement had already lapsed; and (b) require the Municipal officials concerned to ensure that succeeding transactions comply with the existing regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct (a) the CA to (i) submit to AT the BRSs, including the bank statements within ten (10) days after the end of each month; (ii) revert the unclaimed, stale and cancelled checks to the CIB account at the end of the year, and recognize the appropriate payable/liability accounts without physical cancellation of checks; and (b) the CA and City Treasurer (CT) to: (i) reconcile their cash records to determine the details of their unreconciled balance; and (ii) exert effort to retrieve the details/records of all cash balances of dormant accounts as basis for the subsequent management action.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) advise the (a) CT to (i) ensure that all unreleased checks as at year-end are reported to the CA for appropriate adjustment in the books; and (ii) require the PC custodian to liquidate the PC advance at year-end; and (b) CA to immediately revert all unreleased checks to the CIB account as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE continue to strictly adhere to the requirements for granting cash advances and submitting their liquidation to ICFAU, as provided in COA–DBM–DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs - DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a) CA and CGSO Officer, in coordination with the IC, to reconcile the difference of ₱16,419,285.60 between their records and the RPCPPE through (i) proper identification of unserviceable PPE; (ii) determination of the appraisal value of PPE acquired by the City through donation; and (iii) appropriate adjustment in the CAO and CGSO records of donated PPE to component barangays, among others; (b) CA to (i) restore in the books the carrying values of unserviceable PPEs that were outrightly derecognized in the books pending the completion of the IIRUP process, and preparation of the Appraisal Report by the Appraisal and Disposal Committee, to properly recognize any gain or loss in their disposal; and (ii) closely verify the nature of every transaction for correct recognition in the books; and (c) City’s Appraisal Committee to determine the appraisal value of PPE acquired by the City through donation for their correct valuation in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CGSO Officer to (i) insure all property, assets and interests with the GIF-GSIS; and (ii) prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) and facilitate the submission of the updated list of insurable properties to the GSIS, following the guidelines under Section 5.1 of COA Circular No 2018-002 to ensure necessary insurance coverage can be obtained through the GIF; and (b) Budget Committee to include insurance expenses for all insurable properties of the City in the budget.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) members of the CDRRMC to submit justification on allowing the use of the LDRRMF for the purchase of pet identification chips emphasizing, among others, the relevance and direct impact thereof to disaster rehabilitation and recovery under which it was programmed; (b) CGSO and CDRRMO to fast track the provision of conducive storage area for stockpile and demand from the supplier the full delivery of the procured items based on contracted specifications, without prejudice to the imposition of the corresponding penalties on the faulty delivery; and (c) CA to (i) determine and recognize in the books the balance of undistributed welfare goods as at the year-end pursuant to the accounting guidelines and RCA for LGUs; and (ii) submit the lacking documents on the noted procurements and ensure that the succeeding disbursements are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) the MA to (i) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all required bidding documents are submitted and attached to disbursement vouchers before payment; and (ii) deduct the corresponding retention money from every progress payment, pursuant to Section 6.1 of Annex “H” of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) BAC (i) to issue a resolution recommending the blacklisting of the supplier with questionable illegibility documents from participating in the public bidding process, without prejudice to the imposition of additional administrative, civil or criminal sanctions in accordance with Section 69 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and the provisions of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Resolution No. 09-2004, dated 20 August 2004 as amended by the GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017 dated December 21, 2017; and (ii) to ensure strict compliance with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, specifically on the review of bid documents and the eligibility screening of participating bidders, in collaboration with the Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG), to promote good governance in the procurement process.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA, the Chief of HRMO, and the MT to coordinate their efforts to ascertain the details of the previous years’ discrepancies and unreconciled balances of the Due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG accounts, through representation with the agencies and verification of previous years’ records, for appropriate action; (b) HRMO to promptly provide the GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG with updated information on the employment status of officials and employees and newly-hired personnel to ensure that the amounts to be withheld correspond with the billing statement of the GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG; and (c) HRMO and MT to establish coordination and reconciliation of their records.

observations.reason: Management has ensured that succeeding transactions will be properly monitored to ensure compliance with existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MA and MT to (i) continue the practice of periodic reconciliation of their respective cash records to provide consistent cash data/information to Management and other intended users; and (ii) remain compliant with the applicable guidelines on the intact and regular deposit of collections; and (b) MA to continue the practice of regular preparation of BRSs and adjustments/corrections in the GL, if any.

We recommended that the LCE require the concerned Municipal officials to: (a) continuously ensure the prompt and full implementation of NGA-funded PPAs; and (b) facilitate the return of any unexpended balances to the respective SAs, in compliance with applicable COA regulations.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) BAC and Technical Working Group (TWG) members to educate themselves with the requirements of public bidding and other related regulations for a transparent and efficient procurement process; and (b) MA and BAC Secretariat to (i) submit all the requisite bidding and related documents and submit a written explanation for the deficiencies; and (ii) verify the completeness of supporting documents before payment is made to suppliers/contractors.
We also recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC, continue to improve and enhance Municipal development planning and project prioritization to ensure that its manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is invariably compliant with the prescribed criteria.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the ME to strictly comply with the posting of signboards/tarpaulins at the project site of on-going infrastructure projects by ensuring that the cost of signboards/tarpaulins are included in the POWs.

We commended Management for the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the year to the BIR. We also recommended Management to continuously practice the withholding of appropriate taxes and remittance thereof in conformity with BIR RMC Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR RR No. 2-98.

We commended Management for its continuous compliance with existing laws on proper deduction and remittance of contributions of its employees and personnel in accordance with the provisions of RA Nos. 8291, 9679 and 11223, and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) ensure the MA and MT’s continuous compliance with the withholding and remittance of appropriate taxes within the prescribed period; and (b) require the MA to (i) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the Due to BIR account balance as at year-end for appropriate action; and (ii) record transactions in the books as they occur.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to update the GSIS records with the status of the Municipal employees to avoid the under and over-remittances of premiums and loan repayments withheld from employees’ compensation; and (b) MA to (i) maintain subsidiary records to support the general ledger accounts balance; (ii) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the Due to GSIS account balance at year-end for appropriate action; and (iii) record transactions in the books as they occur.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to trace the difference in the Due to LGUs account of ₱2,189.90 between the general ledger and financial statements; (b) HRMO Officer and MA to check the detailed list provided by Pag-IBIG vis-à-vis the payroll to prevent the under and over-remittances of premiums and loan payments to Pag-IBIG.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) HRMO to (i) verify the employee list uploaded in the EPRS to ensure that the list is correct and up to date to avoid under and over-remittances of PhilHealth premiums; and (ii) counter check the amounts of premiums due per employee on the generated SPA vis-à-vis the withheld per payroll to trace discrepancies; and (b) the MA (i) to maintain subsidiary records of Due to PhilHealth account for proper monitoring of account balance; and (ii) to ensure that payments are recorded in the books based on actual payment date.

We recommended that the LCE (a) institute appropriate actions towards officers and employees with long-overdue CAs such as: (i) requiring the refund/liquidation of the CAs; (ii) withholding the payment of salaries of the employees concerned; (iii) desisting from the practice of granting CAs to officials/employees who are not designated to perform disbursing function; and (iv) instituting other legal remedies and sanctions as applicable; and (b) require the MA to (i) monitor strict compliance with the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of CAs pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) strictly adhere to the proper classification of accounts according to RCALGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (iii) submit a satisfactory written explanation for non-withholding from the salaries and retirement benefits of the CAs of AOs who had been separated and retired from government service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the (a) members of the SWMB to prepare and submit to the NSWMC the required annual progress report and accomplishment; and (b) MENRO to (i) maintain a comprehensive SWM reports and data to determine the effectiveness of the Municipality’s PPAs in accordance with the goals and objectives of RA No. 9003; and (ii) provide financial and technical support for the maintenance of barangay MRFs to ensure their full operation and functionality, in accordance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the LFC to install an effective collection strategy on those projected revenues which were not realized to sustain its collection performance and generate more funds for the Municipality and those Departments involved in generating revenues to continuously strive to attain the targets, analyze the reasons for the shortfall in collections, and propose alternative measures to improve the collection of projections.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA, the Human Resources and Management Officer, and the MT to continuously comply with the regulations on the withholding of taxes from income payments, premiums/contributions, and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of Municipal officials and employees, and remittance thereof together with the employer share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to (a) require the immediate refund of the remaining balance of disallowances with no pending appeal at the Commission Proper; and (b) ensure that succeeding transactions comply with the existing laws and regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We also recommended that the LCE (a) require the LFC members to determine the feasibility of placing funds into HYSA and submit a certification that these funds could be considered idle funds, based on its definition under COA Circular No. 912-382; (b) submit authority from the SB allowing the LCE to place said funds into HYSA; and (c) coordinate with Local School Board (LSB) for the termination of the time deposit under the SEF, to make it available for its priority projects in accordance with the School Improvement Plan.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Development Council (MDC) members and other concerned officials to (i) continue to strictly adhere to the allocation of no less than 20% of the NTA to finance the developmental projects of the Municipality; (ii) ensure that all programmed PPAs are implementable during the year for early enjoyment of benefits by the constituents; (b) BAC members to consolidate the PPMPs of every office to create the Municipality’s APP in prescribed format to ensure that all procurements are included; and (c) MA to (i) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat for the submission of all the lacking supporting documents and written explanation for the deficiencies noted; and (ii) check the completeness of supporting documentation prior to payment.

We also recommended that the: (a) LCE require the (i) MBPLO to (i.a) obtain copies of Sworn Statements of Gross Receipts and/or ITR from business taxpayers upon renewal of their business permits and file them together with other relevant documents in their office for check and balance; and (i.b) require every business entity to submit their respective ITRs on or before April 30 of each year, as provided under pertinent the pertinent provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 831; and (b) SB members enact an ordinance adjusting the Municipality’s local tax rates pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the ME to strictly monitor compliance with the posting of signboards/tarpaulins at the project site of on-going infrastructure projects, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the GFPS to: (a) exert coordinated efforts with the implementing offices of the Municipality to adopt strategies to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs as planned and within the budgetary limits; (b) improve project planning, execution and monitoring by strengthening control policies in the utilization of the GAD Fund; and (c) submit a revised GPB to the DILG for its approval of any amendments or updates thereto.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE advise the MSWMB to (a) provide separate areas for segregation and recycling of solid wastes in the MRF and oversee that each function is performed in a systematic, efficient, and ecological manner; (b) conduct regular inspection of barangays’ MRFs and assess the availability of equipment needed for sorting, recovery, recycling, and composting; and (c) heighten the citizen’s consciousness and disseminate awareness about waste reduction, re-use, recycling, composting and livelihood programs that use diverted wastes to curb the high volume of solid wastes disposed of. We further recommended that the SB allot sufficient budget for the necessary improvements in the Central MRF to effectively carry out the sorting, recycling, and composting mechanisms therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Chief of NMH and the Municipal Health Officer to manage the health care wastes following the guidelines and requirements of the DOH Health Care Waste Management Manual to prevent the adverse impacts thereof to the patients, health care personnel, environment, and the general public. We also recommended that the SB allocate sufficient budget for health care waste management programs, particularly for the provision of appropriate containers and adequate disposal facilities for proper identification, segregation, storage and disposal of health care wastes.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) ME to (i) strictly follow the latest DPWH regulations on the computation of ABCs of infrastructure projects to establish proper bases of acceptable bid prices; and (ii) invoke the obligations of contractors and cause the repair works in the projects with observed deficiencies so as not to inflict further damage to the facilities pursuant to the warranty period prescribed in RA No. 9184; and (b) BAC to require the awarded contractor to post warranty securities in sufficient amounts and covering the warranty period prescribed for the different classifications of infrastructure projects.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the LCE require the (a) BAC to submit to the AT the copies of approved construction contracts and POs together with their SDs within five working days from the execution thereof to facilitate review and other necessary audit procedures thereon; and (b) deputized MGSO Chief to give prompt notice and submit to the AT the delivery receipts and SDs on the schedule of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of the purchased items.

We recommended that the LCE require the members of the GAD Focal Point System to (a) continuously review all GAD-related regulations to enhance their knowledge of the priority issues to be addressed through the Annual GAD Plan and Budget in a realistic and attainable manner within the budget year; (b) maintain GAD plan integration in their regular activities; and (c) require the concerned offices to adopt effective strategies to fully implement GAD PPAs to achieve the GAD-related targets.

observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 13 of CY 2024 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor advise the (a) OIC, CA and CT to continue their efficient and diligent efforts to monitor the correctness of the Cash and Cash Equivalent accounts and reconcile the CIB accounts with the bank book balances; (b) PCCs to fully settle/liquidate their accountabilities every end of the year in accordance with the prescribed regulation; and (c) OIC, CA to require the full liquidation of PC advances every end of the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC, CA to (a) make representation with the COA Central Office to request a copy of the credit advice and inquire on the status of liquidation of CFs granted to the former City Mayor; (b) require the City Mayor to settle his accountability if the said funds are found unliquidated; (c) require the AOs to settle/liquidate the cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to Sections 5.8 and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and (d) request an approval from COA for the writing-off of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: There is an ongoing review of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) OIC, City Health Officer to furnish the CAO all documents and records pertaining to the receipt, distribution/consumption, return and transfer of COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies; (b) OIC, CA to (i) make written representations to various source agencies/entities to furnish the unit and total cost of all the vaccines and supplies received therefrom, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts once received; and (c) (ii) to scrutinize fully the completeness of documents supporting the DVs before affixing her signature thereto; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) strictly observe the posting and documentary requirements on alternative mode of procurement in the City’s official website; and (ii) require the suppliers to inspect the documents as to their completeness prior to submission of claims for payment.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor require the (a) OIC, CA and the CGSO Officer to reconcile the difference of ₱20,573,147.34 between the Accounting records and results from physical inventory of PPE; (b) members of the Inventory Committee to (i) set an appropriate schedule to complete the physical inventory-taking of PPE and prepare a complete RPCPPE; and (ii) dispose the unserviceable PPE and derecognize in the books the non-existing/missing PPEs in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. We further recommended that the City Mayor (c) require the OIC, CA to revert to the CIP-Building and Structure account the reclassification of the completed infrastructure projects totaling ₱200,949,123.20 from the Building and Structure account; (d) require the Inventory Committee, through its Chairperson, to request additional manpower by hiring Job Order and/or contractual personnel to complete the conduct of physical count on time; and (e) require the CGSO Officer, OIC, CA and other officials concerned to pursue the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: The concerned officials conducted validation and thorough investigation on the whereabouts of property and equipment labeled as “NO REPORT” in the RPCPPE. Most of the equipment were already accounted as of July 13, 2023. Reiterated under Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report. There is an ongoing review of the submitted documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT to continuously exert efforts in generating revenues from local sources and enforcing legal remedies to collect the unpaid prior years' taxes; (b) OIC, CA to recognize the RPT and SET due and collectible for the year 2023 by setting up the respective receivable accounts in the books; and (c) OIC, City Assessor to make constant follow ups with the concerned departments/offices to fast track the enactment of the Revised Schedule of Fair Market Values and Real Property Assessments to determine the rightful amounts of RPT and SET due and collectible by the government.
observations.reason: RPT Receivables were set up under JEV Nos. 100-22-01-0022 dated 01/31/2022 in the amount of ₱183,506,000.15 and 100-23-01-0020 dated 1/31/2023 in the amount of ₱190,831,597.74 respectively. Reiterated under Observation No. 6, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor (a) spearhead the LDC in the conduct of in-depth planning and assessments prior to approval of the proposed development projects to determine whether or not these projects are procurement-and-implementation-ready for the ensuing fiscal year; and (b) require the Local Project Monitoring Committee to (i) submit justification/s on the delayed completion of the TB-Satellite Treatment Center and River Park Tourism Development projects without relevant approved time extensions; (ii) make constant follow-ups with the Environmental Management Bureau and the Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development for the immediate issuance of the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC), issuance of the development permit, and other requirements for the Establishment/Construction of Crematorium and Columbarium; and (iii) monitor the implementation of development PPAs within the agreed contract duration for timely realization of project benefits.
observations.reason: A justification was submitted in answer to the Audit Memorandum. Follow-ups and coordination with the regulatory agencies for the permits and complete requirements are ongoing. Reiterated under Observation No. 8, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) City Planning and Development Coordinator (CPDC) to formulate an Annual Investment Program (AIP) using the prescribed format and indicate the details, major final and immediate outputs of each PPA to provide adequate performance measurements that will facilitate project evaluation; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to ascertain that the documentary requirements are fully complied with prior to awarding of contracts; and (c) OIC, CA to ascertain the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prior to payment.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) require the OIC, CA to deduct the correct amounts of retention money against the remaining claims of the contractors to safeguard the government against liabilities arising from uncorrected and undiscovered project defects; (b) direct the City Engineer to (i) integrate CPES functions in his office to implement the CPES guidelines for all infrastructure projects and evaluate the contractors' performance prior to the issuance of Certificates of Project Completion; and (ii) constantly monitor the contractors' compliance with the posting of appropriate project signboards and/or public notices in the project sites throughout the duration of the projects to promote a transparent operations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) City Engineer to undertake and prepare detailed engineering activities prior to the implementation of infrastructure projects to prevent the occurrence of engineering disasters and hazards; and (b) personnel from the Information and Technology Office (ITO) to post the required quarterly reports on the City's website within twenty (20) calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Officer in Charge, City Human Resource Management Officer to (a) regularly evaluate each department's manpower and operational requirements and ensure that only the positions ancillary to the mandates of the office are filled up to avoid redundant functions and duties; (b) consider the qualified job order (JO) and contract of service (COS) workers for appointment to vacant plantilla positions in light of their actual performance and accomplishments; (c) submit to the Audit Team (i) the appointment papers and Statements of Actual Duties and Functions (SADF) of the identified JO workers; and (ii) an accurate listing of all JO, COS, and contractual personnel as at end of CY 2022; (d) refrain from designating individual COS workers to positions exercising control or supervision over regular career employees to promote accountability over government transactions; (e) seek reference from those that are comparable positions in the government when determining the amounts of fees for COS workers.
observations.reason: Ongoing review of each office’s organizational structure based on legal mandate, scope of functions, and manpower needs. 13 qualified JO workers were appointed to various plantilla positions. Reiterated under Observation No. 14, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the members of the GAD Focal Point System to (a) continuously review all GAD-related regulations to enhance their knowledge on the priority issues to be addressed through the Annual GAD Plan and Budget in a realistic and attainable manner within the budget year; and (b) require the concerned offices to adopt effective strategies to fully implement GAD PPAs to achieve the GAD-related targets.
We reiterated our audit recommendation that the City Mayor require the persons liable to immediately settle the remaining balance of disallowances amounting to ₱5,457,729.62.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 16, Part II of this Report.
