observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Person and Monitoring and Evaluation Team to (a) ensure full implementation of the planned PPAs as per GPB to achieve the desired results; (b) submit the complete set of the GAD AR within the prescribed period; and (c) evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The GPB now included PPAs relevant to gender issues. GAD funds appropriated and some activities implemented. The GAD AR was prepared though submitted late; Training and seminars conducted; monitoring and evaluation to be strengthened.
observations.reason: Non-functional Monitoring and Evaluation Team; delayed submission of GAD AR and lacking supporting documents; evaluation system not yet fully in place

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) (a) (i)Not Implemented
require the MA to: (i) trace the noted
discrepancies in the CLT account and
make the necessary adjustments in the
books to address the issue on the
fairness of presentation in the (ii)Not Implemented
financial statements; (ii) coordinate
with the MT for regular reconciliation
of their respective records for the
early detection and correction of
errors; (iii) prepare CRJ to record the (iii)Not Implemented
RCD and ORs submitted by the
MTO; and (iv) observe the timely (iv)Implemented
submission of bank reconciliation
reports to the AT; (b) require the MT (b) (i) Not Implemented
to: (i) deposit intact all collections
with the authorized government
depository bank (AGDB) daily or not (ii) Not Implemented
later than the next banking day; (ii)
require all AOs to prepare RCDs and (iii) Implemented
RAAF on a daily basis; (iii) ensure
that the cashbook for CLT is kept (iv) Implemented
updated and accurate; (iv) maintain
cashbooks for CIB and CAs; (v) (v)Implemented
desist from the practice of
encashment of checks from (vi) Implemented
collections; (vi) ensure that controls
and procedures are followed to
account for the requisition, receipt
and distribution of accountable forms; (vii) Not Implemented
(vii) require the LO to exert best
efforts to submit complete and
accurate CRAAF; and (viii) file the (viii)Not Implemented
appropriate fidelity bond of all AOs
pursuant to the Bureau of Treasury (c) Implemented
Circular No. 02-2009; and (c) ensure
that Municipal Officers and personnel
concerned observe and strictly adhere
to existing laws and regulations
governing management and
accounting of collections, deposits
and accountable forms.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the (a) MA to (i)
make the necessary adjustments in the
books to properly recognize
inventories on-hand at year-end and
recognize as expense the distributed
stocks accordingly; and (ii) maintain
SLCs; (b) MT to (i) prepare and
maintain SCs and SSMI for control
and monitoring of inventories; and (ii)
conduct physical count of inventories
and submit the results thereof to the
MAO and AT for reference in audit;
and (c) MT and MA to (i) reconcile
inventory records; and (ii) establish
an Inventory Management process
indicating the required documents to
be prepared upon receipt/delivery,
issuance, and at month-end, including
the deadline of submission of reports,
and to whom the reports are to be
submitted as prescribed by the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to
ensure accuracy of records, promote
operational efficiency, and encourage
adherence to policies, rules and
regulations.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to (a) require the MA to (i)
take immediate steps in accounting
for PPE accounts and derecognition
of semi-expendable, unserviceable
and non-existing/missing PPE; (ii)
reconcile their records with the MTO
and take up the differences based on
the guidelines prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2020-006 to address the
issue on the fairness of presentation of
the financial statements; and (iii)
maintain complete PPELCs for
monitoring of PPEs; (b) MT to (i)
maintain PCs for each class of PPE;
and (ii) include in the remarks column
of RPCPPE the conditions and the
relevant information for all PPE sub-
accounts group namely Land, Land
Improvements, Infrastructure Assets,
Buildings and Other Structures,
Machinery and Equipment,
Transportation Equipment, Furniture,
Furniture, and Books, et. al.; and (c)
Inventory Committee to (i) conduct
physical count of PPE at least once a
year to establish the accuracy and
existence of PPEs in accordance with
the approved PIP; and (ii) collaborate
with the MAO and MTO to ensure
that all PPEs included in the ICF are
reconciled in their respective PPELC
and PCs.

We recommended and LCE as head
of the MDC, agreed to (a) continue to
improve and enhance the
Municipality’s development planning
and project prioritization to ensure
that its manner of appropriation and
utilization of the 20% DF is
invariably compliant with the
prescribed criteria thereon; (b) address
the issue on delayed completion of
projects identified under the 20% DF;
and (c) require the ME to aim full
implementation of PPAs at the set time
to ensure the continuity, consistency,
and strength of the Municipality’s
development efforts towards the
attainment of target over the long run.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to (a) prioritize the placement
of full-time MDRRM Officer and
technical staff of the MDRRMO
involved in administration and
training, operations and warning, and
research and planning; (b) require the
MBO and MAO to (i) reconcile the
discrepancies in the budgeted amount
for DRRMF and its utilization; and
(ii) ensure the correctness of the
monthly report on the sources and
utilization of the DRRMF; and (c)
MDRRM Officer to fully implement
PPAs focused on disaster prevention,
mitigation, and preparedness at the set
time to avoid losses from hazards as
well as to reduce the intensity of
future disasters.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the (a) MBO to
ensure that changes in the annual
budget or AIP shall be supported with
approved SB resolution; (b) ME/BAC
Chairperson to (i) determine and
address the cause of the deficiencies
pertaining to the supporting
documents; (ii) ensure that payments
to Contractors are supported with
complete bidding documents,
eligibility requirements of
Contractors, as built-plans, sufficient
and properly labeled progress photos,
construction logbooks, photos of
project signboards, among others;
(iii) require the prescribed validity
period of performance security until
issuance by the Procuring Entity of
the Certificate of Final Acceptance
pursuant to Section 39 of the RIRR of
RA No. 9184; (iv) issue variation or
change order to cover increase or
decrease in quantities as a result of
disparity between the preconstruction
plans and construction drawings after
the award of the contract; (v) for
projects with discrepancies in the
actual measurement, submit as-built
plans to reflect the changes made in
the original contract; and (vi) submit
satisfactory explanation and supporting
documents to justify the payment made
for the reconstruction of the Slope
Protection at Sapang Bulak Elementary
School; and (c) MA to ensure that
supporting documents are sufficient and
valid prior to payment.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the (a) MA in
coordination with the ME/BAC
Chairperson, to submit the documents
with noted deficiencies to the AT; (b)
MENRO to coordinate with the
DOST for the implementation of the
proposed livelihood projects; and (c)
ME to coordinate with the
Contractors concerned to cause the
repair of the project with structural
defects noted with no additional cost
to the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the MA to (a)
prepare and keep a separate
subsidiary ledger for each fund
transfer to be able to track
information at a very detailed level at
any given time; and (b) if warranted,
file the request for authority to write-
off dormant fund transfers duly
supported with relevant documents;
and (c) prepare SL for each fund
transfers and conduct regular and
periodic verification, analysis, and
validation of the existence of fund
transfers to source NGAs, GOCCs,
and other agencies.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the MA to (a)
review the amounts of taxes withheld
and remitted/posted to ensure
correctness of data and avoid
discrepancies pursuant to BIR No. 2-
98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended
by BIR RR No. 11-2018 and BIR
RMC No. 23-2012; (b) submit legal
bases on withholding and remittance
of taxes on reimbursements of
expenses claimed by Municipal
employees and on payables without
corresponding invoice as proof of
payment; and (c) discontinue the
practice of reimbursements for the
purchase of goods and services as far
as reasonably practicable so that taxes
could be withheld for every
transaction and to avoid fines,
penalties and other sanctions for non-
compliance with rules and regulations
set forth by the BIR.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to identify the problems that
face real property tax collection of the
Municipality and enforce the
procedures and remedies in
addressing tax delinquencies as
provided by Section 174 of RA No.
7160 to improve revenue collection.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the HRMO to (a)
facilitate the submission of properly
accomplished DTRs and
accomplishment reports by the JO
personnel to substantiate payment of
their wages; and (b) require the noted
35 JOs to sign their contract of
services for sound management and to
make the contracts legally binding in
conformity with Section 4 of PD No.
1445.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the GAD Focal
Point System to (a) prioritize the
implementation of the identified
PPAs as per GPB to achieve and
promote desirable gender responsive
society; (b) utilize fully the GAD
budget in order to attain the objective
for which the funds were
appropriated; (c) exert efforts to come
up with a database on sex-
disaggregated data or perform gender
analysis to ensure that allocation of
funds on GAD-related PPAs are not
based on incorrect assumptions and
stereotypes; (d) initiate the
capacitation of key members of the
GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to
guarantee the integration of gender
concerns in the design, management,
implementation, monitoring and
evaluation of the agency programs
and projects and to determine the
efficiency and effectiveness of the
LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and (e)
create a Monitoring and Evaluation
Team to evaluate the outcomes of the
GAD PPAs.
We recommended that the LCE require the MA to consistently prepare the BRS in a timely manner for a fair presentation of the CIB accounts in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.reason:
Similarly discussed in
items that require adjustment in the
Observation and
Recommendation No. 2,
Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) create an Inventory Committee to
(i) spearhead the conduct at least once a year the physical
inventory taking until the same is completed;
(ii) prepare a PIP containing, at the least, the specific
assignment/duties of the members, cut-off date and a
schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory
taking activities from start up to the target completion date of the
physical inventory; and
(iii) prepare RPCPPE based on the result of the physical
inventory;
(b) require the MA to
(i) take immediate steps in accounting for PPE accounts
and derecognition of unserviceable as well as non-
existing/missing PPE and reconcile their records with the
GSO, Municipal Assessor, and Inventory Committee, and take
up the differences based on the guidelines prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
(ii) maintain complete PPELCs for monitoring of PPEs; and
(c) require the GSO Officer to maintain PCs for each class of
PPE.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE as head of the MDC, agreed to
(a) continue to improve and enhance the Municipality’s development planning and
project prioritization to ensure that its manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20%
DF is invariably compliant with the prescribed criteria thereon;
(b) address the issue on timely completion of projects identified under the 20% DF; and
(c) require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to aim full implementation of PPAs at the set
time to ensure the continuity, consistency, and strength of the LGU’s development efforts
towards the attainment of targets over the long run.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the
(a) ME to
(i) determine and address the cause of the deficiencies pertaining to the supporting
documents;
(ii) submit the requisite documents for further evaluation/post-audit;
(iii) issue variation or change order to cover increase or decrease in quantities as a result
of disparity between the preconstruction plans and construction drawings after the award
of the contract;
(iv) for projects with discrepancies in the actual measurement, submit as-built plans to
reflect the changes made in the original contract; and
(v) coordinate with the Contractor to cause the repair of the portion of the project found
to be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality; and
(b) MA to ensure that supporting documents are sufficient and valid before payment to
contractor is made.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the
(a) MA and MBO to reconcile the discrepancies in the reported utilized amount of the
DRRMF for CY 2022;
(b) MDRRM Officer to formulate, develop, and implement PPAs with the objective of
preparing for and mitigating the risks in the event of disasters and calamities to optimally
utilize the 70% Prevention and Mitigation Fund of DRRMF; and
(c) BAC Chairperson to
(i) issue BAC Resolutions recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for
the LCE’s approval;
(ii) subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are met for the use of
alternative mode of procurements; and
(iii) require the submission of technical, legal and financial documents of the suppliers
pursuant to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) aim for the full utilization and implementation of the identified development projects
funded by NGAs for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; and
(b) require the MA to
(i) submit the documents with noted deficiencies; and
(ii) ensure that supporting documents are valid and complete prior to payment to
contractors and suppliers.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD
Focal Point System to
(a) exert efforts to come up with a database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender
analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs are not based on incorrect
assumptions and stereotypes;
(b) initiate the capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to
guarantee the integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation,
monitoring and evaluation of the agency programs and projects and to determine the
efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and
(c) create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) strictly enforce the settlement of the audit charges and disallowances considering that
the prescribed periods of settlement had already lapsed; and
(b) require the Municipal officials concerned to ensure that succeeding transactions
comply with the existing regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct (a) the CA to (i) submit to AT the BRSs, including the bank statements within ten (10) days after the end of each month; (ii) revert the unclaimed, stale and cancelled checks to the CIB account at the end of the year, and recognize the appropriate payable/liability accounts without physical cancellation of checks; and (b) the CA and City Treasurer (CT) to (i) reconcile their cash records to determine the details of their unreconciled balance; (ii) exert effort to retrieve the details/records of all cash balances of dormant accounts as basis for the subsequent management action.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) require the CA to (i) make necessary adjustments on the uncapitalized PPE repairs and maintenance, erroneous capitalization of regular maintenance for road networks, and misclassification of PPE accounts totaling ₱29,173,115.17; (ii) recognize in the books the completed infrastructure projects amounting to ₱22,392,246.10, as of December 31, 2023; (iii) maintain PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) to support the general ledger (GL) balances of PPE accounts and regularly reconcile balances with the GSO property records; and (iv) provide the correct depreciation expense on PPE purchased prior to CY 2021; and (b) require the GSO Head to (i) present the 73 units of motor vehicles to the AT for validation; (ii) determine vehicles that were not yet registered with the LTO and ensure their registration to avoid possible fines and penalties; (iii) instruct the end-users of each vehicle to always preserve and keep copies of OR/CR in the vehicles for any surprise inspection by authorities; (iv) secure the deed of donation from the DOH and a private entity for the donated PPE and request from the City’s Appraisal Committee to render an appraisal report on donated PPE with unknown costs to facilitate their recognition in the books; (v) strictly implement that government vehicles bear government plate and official seal logo of the City with markings “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”; (vi) record the value of parcels of land according to their acquisition cost for appropriate reconciliation with the CAO records; (vii) conduct annual inventory of local roads under the City’s jurisdiction and prepare their corresponding Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and Local Road Network Property Cards; (viii) include in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) the e-trikes acquired through donation from the DOE; and (ix) reconcile the results of physical inventory of PPE with the CAO records; and (c) create a Special Inventory Committee to undertake the “One-Time” cleansing of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct (a) the CA to recalculate the funds allowed to be utilized for the payment of BAC honoraria; (b) the CT to cause the transfer of the unutilized funds from the sale of bid documents to GF at the end of the year; and (c) the CT and BAC members to refrain from charging BAC expenses other than BAC honoraria and overtime pay from the sale of bid documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the (a) CT and CA to (i) limit the grant of cash advance for CF to three (3) months’ requirements; and (ii) release the cash advance at the beginning of the project implementation period; (b) require the SDO to furnish the CAO, and the AT, with a certified copy of the TL on the previous CA’s LR duly stamped "received" by ICFAU; (c) SDO to provide the AT a copy of the PNP chief certification on cash advance for CF, for verification; (d) require the CAO to issue a demand letter to the SDO in case of his failure to fully liquidate his cash advance (i) within 30 days after every quarter, or from the approved target date of completion of the project/activity, or after the cash advance had been fully utilized, whichever comes first; and (ii) at the end of the year; and (f) the CAO invariably observe the proper use of account title “Prior Period Adjustments” in the recognition of expenses pertaining to prior period.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) recognize and take action on the need to revise and update the City’s RC through an Ordinance to generate more income for its development projects and programs; (b) instruct the LFC to (i) realistically set target incomes based on historical growth rates and the City’s capability; and (ii) appropriate/set budgets within reasonable or attainable estimates; and (c) direct the CT to intensify revenue collection efforts to meet the budgetary requirements for financing the City’s PPAs.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the BPLO Chief to (i) further identify and investigate any errors in the BPS-generated reports and report the issues to the service provider for appropriate action; (ii) regularly reconcile records with the CAO and CTO; (iii) investigate the aforementioned suppliers and contractors with underdeclared sales or receipts, subject them to examination of books of accounts to ensure the correctness of taxes, fees, and charges to be collected; (iv) regularly examine the books of accounts of business establishments to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales or receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid pursuant to Section 171 of RA No. 7160; (v) exert effort in learning the formulation and implementation of PILAA and submit a representation to the SP to institutionalize its use; (vi) require the taxpayers to submit a complete documentation of the financial records and ensure that these records form part of their permanent files for easy verification; and (vii) prescribe a standard format of Sworn Statement of Gross Sales or Receipts, when applicable, and require the business taxpayers to have it duly notarized upon submission; (b) strictly require the BPLO Chief and the CT to devise measures to validate the declared gross sales or receipts of establishments applying for business permits especially those who are having direct transactions with the City and its barangays; and (c) require the CAO to maintain SLs for the business taxpayers, and reconcile differences with the BPLO.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) continuously appropriate 20% of the City’s annual NTA for development projects in compliance with the mandate of the RA 7160 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; (b) require the CPDO Chief to improve the performance of the City in terms of project delivery through regular monitoring of project implementation and completion of programmed PPAs during the budget year; (c) require the BAC and CE to (i) carefully plan and schedule all procurement activities to ensure timely implementation and completion of development projects; (ii) monitor the submission of PPMPs by various Offices to facilitate the preparation and submission of the APP to the AT and other regulatory agencies; (iii) comply with the posting and reporting requirements in the implementation of projects; (iv) observe the earliest possible time in the conduct of public bidding, attendance of observers, and timely submission of contracts/PO and IAR within the prescribed period; and (v) submit the EO for the creation of Technical Inspection and Acceptance Committee for the delivery of goods and infrastructure assets; (d) instruct the BAC to prioritize BAC assignments over all other duties and responsibilities as required by the law (Jury Duty); and (e) designate new members of BAC, otherwise, renew the current members in good standing (if warranted) for every expiration of its office term.
We also recommended that the (a) CE (i) perform detailed engineering, surveys and designs on infrastructure projects in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the City Mayor prior to conduct of bidding; (ii) submit the copy of construction logbook and photographic records with imprinted dates as prescribed under DOLE D.O No. 13 s. of 1998; (iii) issue the Certificate of Acceptance after the one-year defects liability period and upon submission of the warranty certificate by the contractor; and (iv) require the project contractors to post detailed information in the project signboards and inform the AT when the project signboards are installed/posted; (b) CA and CPDO Chief ensure timely submission of quarterly utilization reports as required by DBM-DOF- DILG JMC No. 1; and (c) CA ensure that payments for infrastructure projects are supported with sufficient and appropriate documentation in accordance with the pertinent rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CAO to (a) maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for each beneficiary barangay; and (b) prepare a JEV and GL to facilitate tracing of the journal entries that make up the balances forwarded in the financial statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned City officials to (a) reconstitute the SWMB to include a representative from NGOs whose principal purpose is to promote recycling and the protection of air and water quality, a representative from the recycling industry and the manufacturing or packaging industry, and a representative from each concerned government agency possessing relevant technical and marketing expertise as may be determined by the Board; (b) strictly implement the no segregation, no collection policy to protect the health of garbage collectors and segregators from contaminated wastes; (c) invest more in facilities and consider fixing the unserviceable equipment to promote agriculture, composting, and recycling; decrease hauling cost; and lessen the amount of residual waste transferred to the sanitary landfill; (d) avoid delaying the purchase of essential hauling services for unjustifiable reasons which could endanger the environment and public health; (e) closely monitor and maintain a record of hauling activities to ascertain that the amount billed matches the actual waste collected; and (f) determine the precise method for calculating the volume or weight of wastes hauled.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) instruct the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) Chief and CA to (i) discontinue the practice of granting TA to officers/employees with assigned government vehicles; (ii) provide the necessary supporting documents in the payment of RATA; and (iii) strictly adhere to laws, rules and regulations on the granting and submission of necessary documents for RATA payments; (b) require the concerned officials/employees assigned with government vehicles to refund any excess payments of TA to which they are no longer entitled; and (c) cause the CA to (i) submit the paid DVs for the months April to December 2023; and (ii) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to the AT to avoid delays in the conduct of verification and post-audit activities.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GFPS-TWG to (a) aim for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing the GAD Fund so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries; and (b) continuously exert efforts to improve and update the database on sex-disaggregated data or perform gender analysis to ensure that allocation of funds on GAD-related PPAs are not based on incorrect assumptions and stereotypes.
We also recommended that the CA record the necessary adjustments in the Due to BIR and Other Payables account amounting to ₱66,119.54 to reflect the correct account balances.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the CA, CT and the authorized officer from the HRMO to (a) continuously observe the regulations and laws governing the withholding of premium contributions, loan amortizations, etc. and accordingly improve the remittances of the same to the respective government agencies concerned, the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; and (b) establish coordination to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of DVs to the Offices concerned to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) cause the CA and CT to (i) submit to the AT the 5,903 paid DVs in CY 2023 and 1,007 DVs for CYs 2021-2022 DVs and their SDs; and (ii) strictly comply with the timely rendition of accounts to the AT so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; (b) direct the CT to provide the AT with a (i) list of DVs with unclaimed checks as of year-end of 2023, and as of February 15, 2024; and (ii) report of cancelled and replaced checks for reference in audit; (c) require the CA to meet the due dates for submitting required financial reports, such as Monthly Reports, Financial Statements, and BRS, to the AT; (d) instruct all accountable officers to submit financial documents and reports for each month to the CAO on time; and (e) impose sanctions against the accountable officers who continuously fail to abide by the timely submission of documents and reports.

We recommended that the City Mayor furnish the AT with a status report on the actions undertaken on prior years’ audit observations and recommendations to keep them abreast of the actions taken by the concerned City officials relative thereto pursuant to the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended and the City Mayor concurred to require the (a) persons liable to cause the settlement of the suspensions and disallowances deemed final and executory; and (b) the City Accountant to (i) record in the books of accounts the issuance and settlements of the suspensions, disallowances and charges that have become final and executory as contained in the NFD; and (ii) maintain and properly update the subsidiary ledgers/records for each official/employee determined to be liable/responsible for the amount disallowed/charged/suspended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the CT to (a)
ensure that cash-handling AOs are sufficiently bonded and that cash advances
granted to DOs are limited to their maximum accountability; (b) deposit all
collections intact with the City’s authorized government depository bank (AGDB)
daily, or at the least, the next banking day, in accordance with Section 32 of MNGAS
for LGUs; and (c) require all field collectors to prepare and submit their individual
RAAF at the end of each month to facilitate preparation and submission of CRAAF
by the CT to the AT.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA and CT to (i) exhaust all
efforts in tracking the RCDs, validated deposit slips, and other relevant documents
related to the reported bank reconciling items, as reflected in the BRS since CY 2013;
and (ii) have a formal communication with the bank manager to trace the reported
bank reconciling items in their System for appropriate action; and (b) CA to make
the necessary adjustment in the books for all unreleased checks at the end of the year,
supported with the Report of Unreleased Checks certified by the CT.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CGSO Officer to (i) conduct a
physical count of inventories and report the results to the CAO using the RPCI, on a
semestral basis for reconciliation of property records with the CAO; (ii) limit the
procurement of inventories equivalent to two-month to prevent excess issuance to
end-users; and (iii) prepare the SSMI together with the RIS and submit them to the
CAO for recording of issued inventories in the books; (b) CA and CGSO Officer to
maintain SLCs and SCs, respectively, to support the general ledger account balances;
(c) CDRRMO Chief to strengthen internal controls on re-ordering welfare goods for
stock to prevent prolonged storage in stockroom leading to near-expiry welfare
goods; and (d) CA to follow the PIM in the recording and issuance of inventories.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) IC to (i) accelerate joint efforts
to reconcile discrepancies between accounting records and results of physical
inventory; (ii) maintain complete PCs and PPELCs for monitoring of PPEs; (iii)
conduct physical count of PPE at least once a year to establish the accuracy and
existence of PPE account balances; and (iv) establish the final appraisal value of the
properties for disposal to determine the minimum selling price, ensuring fair
compensation for the government; (b) CA to review and analyze the cost of completed
projects still recorded in the CIP account and reclassify them to their appropriate
PPE account supported with Certificate of Completion; and (c) CGSO Officer to (i)
renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the City with the LTO to
avoid unnecessary expenses from payment of damages and penalties in case of
vehicular accidents; and (ii) maintain a permanent file of the OR-CR of all motor
vehicles owned by the City, and henceforth maintain an archival system to ensure
safety and protection of all public documents and records.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CUPDO Officer to ensure that
future transfer of funds which are considered as realignment or augmentation be
done by way of appropriation ordinance and submitted to the Sangguniang
Panlungsod for review and approval; (b) CUPDO and CBO to strictly monitor the
status of Continuing Appropriations; (c) BAC, CE, and CA to submit the supporting
documents of transactions with noted documentary deficiencies; and (d) CE to
conduct extensive site investigation during the preparation of the detailed engineering
and establish the technical viability or success of the project to prevent delay in the
completion of PPAs.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CDRRMO Officer to prepare
and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 to reflect the QRF and PMF, as well as the list of projects
and activities charged to the unexpended CDRRMF of previous years; and (b) CA
and CT to perform reconciliation of the recorded Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account
with the CIB-DRRMF account and make the necessary adjustments on the identified
discrepancy to reflect the correct amount of unexpended CDRRMF in the financial
statements at year-end.

We recommended that the LCE require the CA to (a) review the amounts of
taxes withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace the noted discrepancy and
make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with BIR No.
2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-2018 and BIR RMC No.
23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to review the amounts of
GSIS contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace the
discrepancies, if any, and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) City
Human Resource and Management Office (CHRMO) Officer and CA to strictly
comply with RA No. 8291 on the withholding and full remittance of GSIS
contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the CA to (a) review the contributions
withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace discrepancies, if any, and make the
necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) CHRMO Officer and CA to strictly
comply with RA No. 9679 and its IRR on the withholding and full remittance of Pag-
IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the CA to (a) review the amounts of
PhilHealth contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace the
discrepancies, if any, and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b)
CHRMO Officer and CA to strictly comply with RA No. 7875, as amended by RA Nos.
9241 and 10606 and its RIRR on the withholding and full remittance of PhilHealth
contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to (i) instruct the CAO
personnel concerned to maintain SLs for Due to NGAs account; (ii) in coordination
with the BAC and CE, submit to the AT the legal, technical, and financial documents
of noted transactions to preclude them from being suspended in audit; (iii) ensure that
the documentary requirements for the procurement of infrastructure projects with
reference to RA No. 9184 are submitted and attached to DVs for timely verification and
audit of accounts; and (iv) coordinate with the City Officials concerned to submit the
liquidation report with supporting documents of the noted cash advances with
documentary deficiencies; and (b) CE to (i) aim for the full utilization and
implementation of the identified development projects under the trust fund for
optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; and (ii) perform detailed engineering
study to identify all potential issued in a project and address them immediately to
prevent delays and additional unnecessary cost.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CT and CA to reconcile their
respective books to resolve the noted discrepancy of ₱37,975,236.77 between the
recorded income from business taxes vis-à-vis the report submitted to the BLGF; and
(b) CT to (i) keep the subsidiary ledgers of business taxpayers updated to ensure that
all business establishments settle their business taxes yearly; (ii) submit a report on
delinquent business taxpayers and consider discussing with the software developer
any viable way to include the said report in the System; and (iii) enforce available
enforcement measures to promote taxpayer compliance with established tax
regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CENRO to (i) collaborate with
punong barangays to strictly enforce the “Yes Segregation, Yes Collection” policy by
establishing a team at the barangay level to monitor the waste management practices
of households and establishments and impose penalties accordingly; (ii) strictly
enforce and monitor the use of plastic bags and Styrofoam within the locality and
firmly impose fines and penalties against violators; (iii) acquire an appropriate
weighing scale for the Central MRF or contract an independent firm offering
weighing services to determine the accurate weight of wastes collected by the hauler,
and instruct the Central MRF personnel to maintain a logbook to record the wastes
collected by the hauler; (iv) provide personal protective equipment to waste collectors
and monitoring teams to ensure their health protection; (v) resume the provision of
separate garbage trucks for biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes and/or use
of separate schedules for specific types of wastes, to ensure awareness of residents and
establishments; (vi) operate the Central MRF in compliance with environmental
standards and best practices and ensure the effective and efficient utilization of
equipment and machineries; and (vii) conduct waste characterization to improve the
availability of information relevant to SWM planning decisions including data on
demographics, waste compositions and volumes, and the condition of existing
64
facilities and operations; and (b) require the CT and CA to establish a SWM Fund
from the sale of organic and recyclable materials, which shall be accounted for
separately from the General Fund to provide a stable funding source for recycling
and composting activities, and other operating expenses of the Central MRF.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Chairperson, CE, and CGSO Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of the above-mentioned
regulations on the timely furnishing of copies of contracts/POs to the AT.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to (i) submit the DVs that
had already been paid as of year-end and facilitate the submission thereof to the AT;
(ii) observe the submission of DVs within the first ten days of the ensuing month
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and (iii) ensure
that the DVs and supporting documents are signed immediately by the approving
officials to avoid unnecessary delays in the submission thereof to the AT; and (b)
CT to submit a list of unclaimed checks to the CA, copy furnished the AT, for proper
adjustment in the books.

We recommended that the LCE require the CA and CT to strictly comply with
the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports to avoid delays in
the verification and conduct of audit-related activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the CA and CT to ensure strict
adherence to applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing cash advances.
Additionally, we recommended that no additional cash advances will be allowed to
officials and employees until their previous cash advances have been fully liquidated.

We recommended that the LCE require the CHRMO to continue its
compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012 on the hiring and
payment of personnel under the JO and COS status.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to (a) strictly
adhere to COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely submission of the Annual GPB
and GAD AR to the AT; (b) coordinate with the CUPDO in establishing a fully
operational GAD database which will serve as the basis for gender-responsive
planning, programming and policy formation of the City; (c) ensure maximum
utilization of the GAD fund through implementation of GAD PPAs as planned; and
(d) ascertain that all PPAs included in the GPB are based on identified gender issues
and concerns and verify that expenses remain within budgetary limits.

We recommended that the LCE (a) strictly enforce the settlement of the audit
disallowances that were not appealed, as the prescribed periods for settlement have
already lapsed; and (b) require the (i) City Officials involved in the processing of
claims to be more diligent in evaluating the completeness of supporting documents;
and (ii) persons liable to immediately refund the disallowances once they have become
final and executory.

1.14. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) advise the (a) CT to (i) ensure that all unreleased checks as at year-
end are reported to the CA for appropriate adjustment in the books; and (ii) require the PC
custodian to liquidate the PC advance at year-end; and (b) CA to immediately revert all
unreleased checks to the CIB account as at year-end.

2.8. We recommended that the LCE continue to strictly adhere to the requirements for
granting cash advances and submitting their liquidation to ICFAU, as provided in COA–
DBM–DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs - DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January
8, 2015.

3.20. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE require the
(a) CA and CGSO Officer, in coordination with the IC, to reconcile the difference of
₱16,419,285.60 between their records and the RPCPPE through (i) proper
identification of unserviceable PPE; (ii) determination of the appraisal value of PPE
acquired by the City through donation; and (iii) appropriate adjustment in the CAO
and CGSO records of donated PPE to component barangays, among others; (b) CA to
(i) restore in the books the carrying values of unserviceable PPEs that were
outrightly derecognized in the books pending the completion of the IIRUP process,
and preparation of the Appraisal Report by the Appraisal and Disposal Committee,
to properly recognize any gain or loss in their disposal; and (ii) closely verify the
nature of every transaction for correct recognition in the books; and (c) City’s
Appraisal Committee to determine the appraisal value of PPE acquired by the City
through donation for their correct valuation in the books.

4.5. We recommended that the LCE require the CA to amortize the cost of computer
software items over their useful lives in accordance with the accounting guidelines in
IPSAS 31.

5.13. We recommended that the LCE (a) satisfactorily and fully implement the PPAs
funded by NGAs and to promptly return the unutilized balances to their respective
source agencies; and (b) instruct the CA and CT to remit to the source agencies the
balances of the prior years’ fund transfers.

6.20. We recommended that the (a) LCE require the CT and BPLO Head to (i)
immediately address the identified concerns/issues that caused the over-assessment of
LBTs and imposition of LBTs to newly opened business establishments to avoid
unjust revenue generation; and (ii) carefully review the TOPs and ensure that the tax
base conforms to the gross sales/receipts declared by the business taxpayers in their
financial statements; and (b) SP members enact a supplemental revenue ordinance to
adjust the current percentage being levied on local business taxpayers in accordance
with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and BLGF MC.

7.8. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CGSO Officer to (i) insure all
property, assets and interests with the GIF-GSIS; and (ii) prepare the Property
Inventory Form (PIF) and facilitate the submission of the updated list of insurable
properties to the GSIS, following the guidelines under Section 5.1 of COA Circular
No 2018-002 to ensure necessary insurance coverage can be obtained through the
GIF; and (b) Budget Committee to include insurance expenses for all insurable
properties of the City in the budget.

8.12. We recommended that the LCE advise the (a) LDC to (i) conduct
comprehensive planning and assessments prior to the approval of the proposed
development projects to determine if these are procurement-and-implementation-
ready for the budget year; and (ii) closely monitor the implementation of development
projects within the agreed contract duration to ensure timely realization of the
projects’ benefits; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee to observe the Guidelines in
the Preparation of the SPBDs and implement the prescribed formats of the required
forms as per GPPB Resolution No. 16-2020.

9.11. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) CA to consistently adhere to the
above-mentioned pertinent provisions of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-
002 to ensure the availability of DRRMF for the implementation of other disaster risk
reduction management activities of the City; and (b) CDRRMC members to
continuously exert their effort to identify cost-effective preparedness and risk
reduction projects to be funded by the DRRMF under the STF.

10.26. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) members of the CDRRMC to
submit justification on allowing the use of the LDRRMF for the purchase of pet
identification chips emphasizing, among others, the relevance and direct impact
thereof to disaster rehabilitation and recovery under which it was programmed; (b)
CGSO and CDRRMO to fast track the provision of conducive storage area for
stockpile and demand from the supplier the full delivery of the procured items based
on contracted specifications, without prejudice to the imposition of the
corresponding penalties on the faulty delivery; and (c) CA to (i) determine and
recognize in the books the balance of undistributed welfare goods as at the year-end
pursuant to the accounting guidelines and RCA for LGUs; and (ii) submit the lacking
documents on the noted procurements and ensure that the succeeding disbursements
are supported with complete documentation.

11.23. We recommended that the LCE (a) advise the City Solid Waste Management
Board (CSWMB) to (i) spread awareness about waste reduction, re-use, recycling,
composting and livelihood programs that use diverted waste; (ii) enforce the "No
Segregation, No Collection" policy, especially in noncompliant barangays and
households to reduce the amount of waste generated; (iii) conduct regular inspection
of the MRFs and assess the availability of equipment needed for sorting, recycling
and composting; (iv) coordinate with the City Health Officer (CHO) to train and
orient personnel engaged in SWM; (v) allocate budget to provide protective
equipment and gears for the safety of personnel who handle and transport the solid
wastes; and (vi) coordinate with the hauling service provider and secure a weighing
scale in the transfer station to determine the accurate volume of solid wastes
collected; and (b) create an Inspectorate Team to strengthen monitoring over
residual waste collection and disposal and oversee the implementation of a checking
system.

12.6. We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS)
members to consistently review all GAD-related regulations for enhanced planning
and implementation processes for GAD PPAs.

13.8. We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the LCE require the
CGSO Chief (a) and BAC members to submit the copies of approved construction
contracts and POs together with their SDs to the AT within five working days from
their execution to facilitate timely review of contracts; and (b) to notify the AT of
deliveries within 24 hours upon acceptance and furnish the AT with appertaining
delivery documents.

14.9. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the LCE require the OIC,
HRMO to (a) continuously evaluate each department's manpower and operational
requirements and ensure that only the positions ancillary to the mandates of the office
are filled up to avoid redundant functions and duties; (b) consider the qualified JO
workers for appointment to vacant plantilla positions in light of their actual
performance and accomplishments; (c) fast track the implementation of uniform
policies and guidelines on the recruitment and engagement of outsourced personnel
to systematize the hiring of individuals under JO bases considering the limited
resources of the government; and (d) review the ARs submitted by JO workers and
ensure that outputs reported thereon are quantifiable and verifiable to substantiate
the rendition of services vis-à-vis the accuracy and validity of claims for payroll.

15.3. We recommended that the LCE require the CT and CA to (a) continuously
comply with the regulations on the withholding of taxes from income payments,
premiums/contributions, and loan amortizations from the salaries and wages of City
officials and employees, and remittance thereof together with the employer share
within the prescribed period; and (b) exert their best efforts to determine the prior
years’ unreconciled balances.

16.5. We recommended that the LCE enforce the settlement of the remaining
disallowance by the persons liable, as it has become final and executory.

1.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the MT to unceasingly
comply with the remittance of collections regularly and intact to avoid the accumulation of
cash in her custody.

2.18 We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) IC to undertake an actual physical count of PPE and prepare a complete and reliable RPCPPE to substantiate the existence of PPE account balances; and (b) the MA (i) reclassify the costs for the dredging of the river to expense and/or prior year’s adjustment account; (ii) in coordination with the OIC-GSO and Municipal Engineer (ME), determine the specific details of the Land Improvements account to facilitate the provision of allowance for depreciation thereon; and (iii) strictly adhere to the proper classification of accounts according to RCALGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

3.5 We recommended that the LCE strengthen inventory controls by requiring the (a) MA to (i) record all acquisitions of inventories to the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize corresponding expense accounts upon receipt of SSMI supported with RIS from the SO; (ii) maintain SLCs to record the receipt and issuance of procured inventories; and (b) SO to (i) to receive all procured inventories from suppliers and request the Inspectorate Committee to inspect them and sign the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) upon inspection; and (ii) maintain SCs to record all inventories received from suppliers; (iii) prepare RIS to document the issuances to requisitioning offices and prepare weekly report of issuances through the SSMI; (iv) submit the SSMI supported with RIS to the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) for recording of issued inventories; and (v) conduct a semestral count of inventories, prepare the RPCI, and submit a copy to the MA and AT for reconciliation of records and reference in audit, respectively.

4.6 We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) exert vigorous efforts to work back on previous years’ records to identify the details/purpose of the amount of ₱47,290.94 for proper resolution; and (b) draw a JEV to correct the erroneous recording of the Due to LGUs account totaling ₱1,182,533.86.

5.14 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) the MA to (i) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all required bidding documents are submitted and attached to disbursement vouchers before payment; and (ii) deduct the corresponding retention money from every progress payment, pursuant to Section 6.1 of Annex “H” of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (b) BAC (i) to issue a resolution recommending the blacklisting of the supplier with questionable illegibility documents from participating in the public bidding process, without prejudice to the imposition of additional administrative, civil or criminal sanctions in accordance with Section 69 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and the provisions of Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) Resolution No. 09-2004, dated 20 August 2004 as amended by the GPPB Resolution No. 40-2017 dated December 21, 2017; and (ii) to ensure strict compliance with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, specifically on the review of bid documents and the eligibility screening of participating bidders, in collaboration with the Secretariat and Technical Working Group (TWG), to promote good governance in the procurement process.

6.24 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) BAC and TWG to (i) submit a satisfactory written explanation on their failure to conduct post-qualification to suppliers prior to award of contract; and (ii) issue a BAC Resolution recommending the blacklisting of the supplier for submitted questionable eligibility document if found with merit; (b) MA to (i) coordinate with the BAC on the submission of the complete and valid documents to be attached to the disbursement claims before payment; and (ii) strictly adhere to the proper classification of accounts under the RCALGUs under COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (c) LDRRMO to (i) assign a focal person to be in charge of the receipt, distribution, maintenance and submission of reports and registries for the issuance of relief goods; (ii) submit to the AT the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF every 15th day after the end of each month.

7.20 We recommended that the LCE (a) require the members of the LFC, in coordination with the SB, to initiate the updating of the Municipality’s LRC, pursuant to RA No. 7160; (b) direct the MT to (i) seek the assistance of barangays in conducting an inspection and verification of the statuses of the 139 businesses for appropriate action; (ii) provide the BPLO of the reports for the business tax collections for easy reconciliation and monitoring of the latter’s record; and (iii) examine the taxpayers’ books of accounts to confirm their declared gross receipts for accurate collections of business taxes; and (c) direct the MTO and BPLO to monitor their request to the the Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) to fast-track the grant of the system and the conduct of training for relevant Municipality’s employees on its use.

8.18 We recommended that the LCE (a) update the composition of the SWMB in accordance with Section 12 of RA No. 9003; and (b) require the members of the SWMB to prepare and submit to NSWMC the annual progress report and accomplishments required by the latter in its Resolution No. 1180, approving the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP; and (c) in coordination with the MENRO, provide financial and technical assistance for the maintenance of barangay MRFs and ensure their full operation and functionality, under Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

9.29 We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA, the Chief of HRMO, and the MT to coordinate their efforts to ascertain the details of the previous years’ discrepancies and unreconciled balances of the Due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG accounts, through representation with the agencies and verification of previous years’ records, for appropriate action; (b) HRMO to promptly provide the GSIS, PhilHealth, and PAG-IBIG with updated information on the employment status of officials and employees and newly-hired personnel to ensure that the amounts to be withheld correspond with the billing statement of the GSIS, PhilHealth, and PAG-IBIG; and (c) HRMO and MT to establish coordination and reconciliation of their records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MA and MT to (i) continue the practice of periodic reconciliation of their respective cash records to provide consistent cash data/information to Management and other intended users; and (ii) remain compliant with the applicable guidelines on the intact and regular deposit of collections; and (b) MA to continue the practice of regular preparation of BRSs and adjustments/corrections in the GL, if any.

We recommended that the LCE (a) create an inventory committee tasked with conducting the physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE and ensure that committee members are relieved from their regular duties to complete the undertaking and submit an RPCPPE to the MA and AT not later than January 31 of each year; (b) require the GSO/MT and MA to: (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer (ME) to facilitate compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 in keeping and maintaining records for the Road Networks account; and (ii) maintain complete and updated PCs and PPELCs, respectively, for the control and monitoring of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) expedite the retrieval of pertinent records from previous years fund transfers to facilitate the return of unexpended balances to the respective SAs; and (b) adjust the fund transfers received from various agencies totaling ₱2,715,111.44, in their appropriate accounts to ensure a fair presentation of the Due to NGAs, Due to GOCCs and Trust Liabilities accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) BAC and Technical Working Group (TWG) members to educate themselves with the requirements of public bidding and other related regulations for a transparent and efficient procurement process; and (b) MA and BAC Secretariat to (i) submit all the requisite bidding and related documents and submit a written explanation for the deficiencies; and (ii) verify the completeness of supporting documents before payment is made to suppliers/contractors.

We also recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC, continue to improve and enhance Municipal development planning and project prioritization to ensure that its manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is invariably compliant with the prescribed criteria.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the ME to strictly comply with the posting of signboards/tarpaulins at the project site of on-going infrastructure projects by ensuring that the cost of signboards/tarpaulins are included in the POWs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MDRRMO and the MBO Heads to refrain from charging ineligible expenditures against the LDRRMF; (b) MA and BAC Secretariat to submit explanation or justification on noted documentary deficiencies; and (c) MA to verify the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment to suppliers/contractors.

We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the MENRO Head to (i) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the SWMP of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of environmental laws; (ii) monitor the barangays without MRFs and provide technical/financial for them to establish their own MRFs; and (iii) review the Pambayang Kautusan Blg. 23 and identify the provisions that require strict implementation and revisions to ensure the attainment of the objectives for enacting the Ordinance; and (b) require the MBO to provide a budget for the rehabilitation of perimeter fence at the Municipality’s MRF.

We recommended that the (a) LCE require the MT to continuously exert her best effort to improve tax collection efficiency and (b) Sangguniang Bayan members to prioritize the enactment of revised MRC in accordance with Section 191, Book II of RA No. 7160.

We commended Management for the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the year to the BIR. We also recommended Management to continuously practice the withholding of appropriate taxes and remittance thereof in conformity with BIR RMC Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR RR No. 2-98.

We commended Management for its continuous compliance with existing laws on proper deduction and remittance of contributions of its employees and personnel in accordance with the provisions of RA Nos. 8291, 9679 and 11223, and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MPDO, along with the GFPS and Technical Working Group, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database to facilitate better and efficient formulation of PPAs to truly address GAD issues and concerned in the locality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct the (a) liable persons to refund the disallowance that became final and executory there being no appeal filed within the prescribed period; and (b) officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

1.15. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to (a) require
the MA to (i) recognize cash transactions in the books as they occur; (ii) prepare
adjusting entries to correct the noted accounting errors in the recording of year-end
collections and deposits, and the dishonored check; and (iii) strictly adhere to the
prescribed accounting entries for recording cash transactions and dishonored checks;
and (b) MT to cause the immediate submission of DVs for disbursements totaling
₱13,444.00 to the MAO for recording in the books; and (c) delegate the issuance of
checks to the MTO.

2.9. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MA to (i) consistently
apply the PIM for recording inventories; (ii) maintain SLCs to support the reported
balances of the Inventory accounts; and (iii) recognize in the books the issued
inventories based on the SSMI to be submitted by the MTO/SO; and (b) require the
MTO/SO to (i) maintain SCs to record all purchased inventories and issuances; (ii)
prepare SSMI supported with RIS to document the issued inventories and submit the
report to the MAO for recording in the books; and (iii) conduct a physical inventory
of inventories on a semestral basis and prepare a RPCI for submission to the MAO
and AT.

3.17. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to (i) trace the
discrepancies between the reported acquisition costs of the PPE accounts and the
lapsing schedule for depreciation to provide a reliable balances for depreciation
expenses and accumulated depreciation accounts; (ii) exhaust all efforts to retrieve
the records of the PPE accounts under the TF books to facilitate their transfer to GF
books and provide an allowance for their depreciation; (b) GSO to (i) render a
complete RPCPPE for reconciliation with the MAO’s property records; (ii) safekeep
the Municipality’s properties; (c) Disposal Committee to prepare the Inventory and
Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to account for all
unserviceable PPEs to facilitate their immediate disposal; and (d) MA and GSO
Officer to prepare and maintain their property records.
3.18. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to facilitate the “One-Time”
Cleansing of PPE accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for fair
presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.

4.4. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the Municipal Engineer
(ME) to prepare the Certificate of Inspection and Completion and Statement of Work
Accomplished for the completed projects and provide these to the MA to facilitate the
recording thereof in their corresponding PPE accounts; and (b) the MA to recognize
the immediate transfer of the completed projects to their respective PPE accounts
following Section 50 of the MNGAS-LGU Volume I.

5.10. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) exert her best
effort in identifying the source of funds of the unutilized balances totaling
₱3,343,987.77 to facilitate their return to the source agencies. Otherwise, request for
the write-off of these accounts, if warranted; and (b) reclassify the receipts totaling
₱1,801,427.85 to their appropriate accounts.

6.12. We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) to require the Municipal Planning
and Development Office (MPDO) Chief and implementing Offices to (i) implement
the development PPAs as planned and establish a clear order of priority in their
implementation; and (ii) to implement the intended development projects under the
continuing appropriations of the 20% DF; (b) in coordination with the Local
Development Council (LDC), ensure thar the programmed PPAs are procurement
and implementation ready for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents; and (c)
to require the MA to (i) submit the lacking documents on the aforementioned
disbursements; and (ii) verify the completeness of the supporting documents prior to
payment.

7.13. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the MA to (i) make the
necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the TL – DRRMF as of year-end; (ii)
return the unutilized balance of NDRRMF-FALGU to the National Treasury; (b) the
Municipal officials and personnel in charge for the distribution of welfare goods to
ensure that the distribution lists are completely supported with information necessary
for the validation of the AT; and (c) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to practice
public bidding for the purchases of welfare goods for stockpile.

8.5. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT to (i) submit the approved
estimates of petty expenses for one month as documentary requirement for the initial
set-up of the PCF; (ii) ensure that payments out of the PCF are in accordance with
the nature of expenses intended for the fund; and (iii) establish a separate cash
advance for financial assistance; and (b) MA (a) submit the lacking documents for
the payments of financial assistance; and (ii) verify the completeness of the submitted
requirements prior to payment.

9.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC Secretariat to
submit to the Audit Team within the prescribed period all advance copies of perfected
contracts, POs and pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents; (b)
BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG to strictly comply with the required
documentary requirements provided by the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the
procurement of projects through public bidding, including the proper mechanism on
the use of the PhilGEPS; (c) the Supply/ Inspection Officer responsible for accepting
deliveries to notify the AT of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for
verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment
pursuant to the above-stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001, 2009-002
and 95-006; and (d) submit written justification for the noted deficiencies and the
requisite documents mentioned above, for evaluation of the AT.

10.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) ensure the MA and MT’s
continuous compliance with the withholding and remittance of appropriate taxes
within the prescribed period; and (b) require the MA to (i) retrieve prior years’
records to determine the details of the Due to BIR account balance as at year-end for
appropriate action; and (ii) record transactions in the books as they occur.

11.8. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Human Resource
Management Officer (HRMO) to (i) update the GSIS records with the status of the
Municipal employees to avoid the under and over-remittances of premiums and loan
repayments withheld from employees’ compensation; and (b) MA to (i) maintain
subsidiary records to support the general ledger accounts balance; (ii) retrieve prior
years’ records to determine the details of the Due to GSIS account balance at year-
end for appropriate action; and (iii) record transactions in the books as they occur.

12.6. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to trace the
difference in the Due to LGUs account of ₱2,189.90 between the general ledger and
financial statements; (b) HRMO Officer and MA to check the detailed list provided
by Pag-IBIG vis-à-vis the payroll to prevent the under and over-remittances of
premiums and loan payments to Pag-IBIG.

13.7. We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) HRMO to (i) verify
the employee list uploaded in the EPRS to ensure that the list is correct and up to date
to avoid under and over-remittances of PhilHealth premiums; and (ii) counter check
the amounts of premiums due per employee on the generated SPA vis-à-vis the
withheld per payroll to trace discrepancies; and (b) the MA (i) to maintain subsidiary
records of Due to PhilHealth account for proper monitoring of account balance; and
(ii) to ensure that payments are recorded in the books based on actual payment date.

14.10. We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GAD Focal Point Person
and Monitoring and Evaluation Team to (a) ensure full implementation of the
planned PPAs as per GPB to achieve the desired results; (b) submit the complete set
of the GAD AR within the prescribed period; and (c) evaluate the outcomes of the
GAD PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) require the MA to:
(i) trace the noted discrepancies in the CLT account balance and make the necessary
adjustments in the books for a fair presentation of the account in the financial
statements; (ii) coordinate with the MT in reconciling regularly their respective
records for early detection and correction of errors; and (iii) prepare CRJ based on
RCD and ORs submitted by the MTO; (b) require the MT to (i) deposit intact all
collections with the Municipality’s authorized government depository bank (AGDB)
daily or not later than the next banking day; (ii) require all AOs to prepare RCDs
and RAAF on a daily basis; and (iii) post fidelity bond for the three designated
collecting officers pursuant to the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009; and (c)
ensure that Municipal officers and personnel concerned observe and strictly adhere
to existing laws and regulations governing government funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT to immediately submit the
99 DVs and their supporting documents to the MAO for recording in the books and
henceforth, ensure that all DVs with released checks are completely transmitted to
MAO; (b) MA to (i) record in the books stale checks aged more than six months; (ii)
prepare BRSs for the aforementioned eight bank accounts and regularly obtain bank
statements within five days after the end of each month to ensure timely preparation
and submission of BRSs; and (c) MT and MA to regularly reconcile their cash records
to immediately settle discrepancies, if any.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT to (i) submit the
disbursement vouchers for cash advances totaling ₱6,575,355.92 to the MAO for
recording in the books; and (ii) submit all DVs to MAO upon payment; (b) instruct
the MA to (i) reclassify the erroneous recording of expense as cash advance; (ii)
submit to AT the LRs that were already recorded in the books of accounts; and (iii)
ascertain the completeness of LRs before recording in the books of accounts; (c)
MT to cease the practice of granting cash advances for special purposes to officials not
designated as SDO nor bonded; and (d) direct the MA and MT to require the
accountable officers to attach the requisite supporting documents in their LRs.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MT to (i) accomplish completely
the RIS to support the issuance of inventories to end-users; (ii) prepare and maintain
SCs and SSMI for adequate asset monitoring and control, and submit a copy of the
SSMI to the MAO supported with accomplished RISs; and (iii) conduct a regular
physical count of inventories and submit the RPCI to the MAO for reconciliation, and
AT for reference in audit; (b) MA to prepare and maintain SLCs, and use the SSMI
as the principal document in the derecognition of inventories; and (c) MT and MA to
reconcile their inventory records as frequently as necessary to ensure the accuracy of
the Inventory account balances.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the IC to (i) prepare and timely
submit the PIP; and (ii) prioritize the completion of the physical count over their
regular duties to prepare RPCPPE based on the results of such count; (b) require the
MA to (i) prepare PPELCs and regularly update its records for adequate monitoring
of PPEs; and (ii) reconcile records with the Municipal Assessor, and in coordination
with the MTO, make representations with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) to
secure copies of TCTs under the name of the Municipality to establish ownership over
the recorded landholdings; and reconcile records with the MTO to establish the
existence and ownership of the 32 units of transportation equipment without OR-CR;
(c) establish a DC that will focus on undertaking the necessary procedures for the
disposal of the unserviceable assets of the Municipality; and (d) MTO to (i) maintain
Property Cards for each class of PPE and copies of MR for each AO to ensure proper
accountability over the Municipality-owned assets; and (ii) implement a system to
actively monitor and facilitate the timely renewal of motor vehicle registrations.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the LFC to seek legislative approval
for the updating of business tax rates; (b) the MA to properly classify business tax
collections in the books; (c) the BPLO Chief to ensure that all essential documents are
submitted by business taxpayers upon their registration and renewal of business
permit; and (e) the BPLO Chief and MT to levy and collect taxes in accordance with
the provisions of the Municipality’s RC.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) AOs to (i) strictly observe the
prescribed period in the liquidation of CAs; and (ii) immediately settle/liquidate the
cash advances for CFs; and (b) MA to refrain from granting multiple cash advances
to AOs to prevent the accumulation of their cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA to (i) make representations
with the identified source agencies to facilitate the return of the unexpended balances
from completed projects; (ii) demand the submission of MOA from implementing
departments and ensure that supporting documents are valid and complete before
payments are made; (b) instruct the implementing departments to execute the PPAs
within the time frame; and (c) require the BAC to submit the required documents on
the noted transactions and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016
RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the Municipal Development
Council (MDC), continue to improve and enhance the Municipality's development
planning and project prioritization for the 20% DF to guarantee its consistent
application of the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; (b) require the ME to
fully implement the PPAs within the time frame; (c) require the BAC Chairman to
submit the requisite documents in compliance with the provisions of 2016 RIRR of
RA No. 9184; and (d) require the MA to ensure that supporting documents are valid
and complete before payments are made.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the MDRRMO Chief to (i) optimize
the fund utilization to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of
disasters in the community; and (ii) specify in the LDRRMFIP the list of PPAs to be
charged against the previous years’ LDRRMF before implementation; (b) the MA
(ii) ensure that disbursements are duly supported with complete and proper
documentation before payment to establish their validity and propriety; and (ii)
adjust the Utilization Report for the TL-LDRRMF to come up with the correct year-
end balance; and (c) the MDRRMO Chief and MA to prepare and submit the
required reports within the prescribed period to ensure the availability of
information on the receipts and utilization of DRRM funds.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT in charge of accepting
deliveries of procured items, to comply strictly with the submission of delivery
documents to the AT within 24 hours upon acceptance thereof.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MT to provide the AT with a (i)
list of DVs with unclaimed checks as of year-end of 2023, and as of the present date;
and (ii) report of cancelled and replaced checks for reference in audit; and (b) MA to
(i) facilitate the immediate submission of DVs to the AT for post-audit; and (ii)
strictly comply with the timely submission of DVs to the AT to enable prompt verification,
post-audit of transactions, and timely communication of audit results.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairperson of the SWM Board, in
coordination with the MENRO and barangays (a) exert collaborative efforts in their
campaign towards safe and sanitary SWM by requiring business establishments to
put preference in using biodegradable packing materials; and (b) discourage littering
and open burning by imposing penalties against violators.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MAO, MTO and MHRMO to
continuously comply with the pertinent laws on the timely withholding and
remittance of premiums, contributions, and loan repayments to GSIS, HDMF, and
PHIC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to (a) continuously comply with
the pertinent laws on the timely and intact remittance of withheld taxes; (b) revisit
BIR 2-98, as amended, and other relevant BIR issuances on the appropriate
withholding of EWT, VAT, and Percentage Tax; (c) issue an Office Memorandum
reiterating the obligation of the Municipality, as well as the employees as its agent, to
withhold taxes on certain money payments including reimbursable expenses, and by
providing guidelines on proper withholding of taxes; and (d) as far as reasonably
practicable, discourage the purchase of goods and services on reimbursement basis.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the GFPS to (i) continue its faithful
compliance with the budgetary requirement for the GAD Fund; and (ii) exert effort
in mobilizing its proponent offices to accomplish all the planned PPAs and fully utilize
the GAD budget in the succeeding years; and (b) the MPDO to spearhead the setting
up, maintenance, and updating of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-
responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal
Accountant (MA) (a) to closely verify the accuracy of recording collections and
deposits in the books; and (b) and Municipal Treasurer (MT) to perform regular reconciliation of their cash records to promptly identify differences and effect adjustment in the books and/or cashbook.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) delegate capable
personnel of the MAO to prioritize the preparation of BRSs for all the Municipality’s
bank accounts, and promptly recognize the identified book reconciling items in the
books for fair presentation of CIB and other affected accounts’ balances; (b) regularly
submit copies of BRSs to the AT for immediate review; and (c) monitor the SL and GL balances regularly to prevent disagreement(s) in their balances.
