We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal officials in charge
of documentation of CF to strictly submit/attach the required documents to the cash
advance voucher before payment; and (b) MA to (i) submit the cash advance voucher
for CF and its attachments within seven days after the release of check; and (ii)
recognize the Advances to SDO account on cash advances for CF.

We recommended that the LCE (a) institute appropriate actions towards
officers and employees with long-overdue CAs such as: (i) requiring the
refund/liquidation of the CAs; (ii) withholding the payment of salaries of the
employees concerned; (iii) desisting from the practice of granting CAs to
officials/employees who are not designated to perform disbursing function; and (iv)
instituting other legal remedies and sanctions as applicable; and (b) require the MA
to (i) monitor strict compliance with the regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of CAs pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) strictly adhere to the
proper classification of accounts according to RCALGUs under COA Circular No.
2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (iii) submit a satisfactory written explanation
for non-withholding from the salaries and retirement benefits of the CAs of AOs who
had been separated and retired from government service.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) MA to (i) record all
acquisitions of inventories to their appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize
corresponding expense accounts upon receipt of SSMI supported with RIS from SO;
and (ii) maintain SLCs; and (b) SO to (i) conduct a semestral physical count of
inventories and submit the RPCI to the MA and AT for reference in audit; and (ii)
prepare and maintain SCs, RIS, and SSMI in compliance with applicable provisions
of MNGAS.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) AC to conduct an appraisal
of donated PPE in accordance with Item 6.2.12 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for
eventual recording in the books; (b) MA to (i) comply with the proper recognition of
CIP and AP accounts as per COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) prepare an adjusting
entry to derecognize from the CIP and AP accounts the cost of the MRFs not yet
started; and (iii) close the CIP account and transfer the appropriate amounts to the
relevant PPE account for completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MT
and MA to (i) continue the practice of periodic reconciliation of their respective cash
records to avoid discrepancies between their records; and (ii) exert coordinated efforts in
securing necessary documents or proof of transactions from the Land Bank of the
Philippines (LBP)-Meycauayan Branch to facilitate proper and timely recording of Debit
Memoranda (DM) and Credit Memoranda (CM) in the books of account; (b) MT to remain
compliant with the intact and regular deposit of collections; (c) MA to immediately
recognize in the books of account the identified book reconciling items.

We also recommended that the LCE (a) require the Local Finance Committee (LFC)
members to determine the feasibility of placing funds into HYSA and submit a
certification that these funds could be considered idle funds, based on its definition under
COA Circular No. 912-382; (b) submit authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB)
allowing the LCE to place said funds into HYSA; and (c) coordinate with Local School
Board (LSB) for the termination of the time deposit under the SEF, to make it available
for its priority projects in accordance with the School Improvement Plan.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the IC to (i) complete the physical
count of all PPE owned by the Municipality and cause the preparation and submission
of RPCPPE to the AT not later than January 31 of each year; (b) require the MA to (i)
reclassify the misclassified PPE items totaling ₱1,424,540.00 into their appropriate PPE
accounts including accumulated depreciation, if any; and (ii) recognize the depreciation
expense incurred on completed CIP projects in CY 2023 totaling ₱550,563.40; (c) require
the ME to furnish the MA with copies of certificate of project completion and inspection
for the latter to reclassify the CIP account to the appropriate infrastructure asset account;
(d) create a Disposal Committee (DC) tasked with preparing documents necessary for
the disposal of unserviceable PPE; (e) direct the inspectorate team to completely fill out
the IAR for procured PPE items; (f) require the MA and MT to maintain complete and
updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, per PPE class.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) to optimally utilized fund transfers from NGAs by
implementing the identified priority PPAs within the provisions of applicable rules and
regulations as well as related Memorandum of Agreement; (b) MA and MT to continue
the practice of refunding unused balances of transferred funds upon completion of
projects to the SA or the BTr, whichever is applicable; (c) MA to (i) draw a correcting entry
for the erroneous recording of fund transfers from PhilHealth and 20% Municipal share
from Fire Code Fees amounting ₱9,178,257.71, and ₱5,923,025.08, respectively, to Due
to GOOCs and Trust Liabilities account, respectively; (ii) account for the total 15% share
of the OBO from Building Permit Fees as of December 31, 2023, and transfer the amount
to the Trust Liabilities account; and (iii) furnish the AT with copies of Journal Entry
Vouchers and supporting documents, thereafter.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Development
Council (MDC) members and other concerned officials to (i) continue to strictly adhere to
the allocation of no less than 20% of the NTA to finance the developmental projects of
the Municipality; (ii) ensure that all programmed PPAs are implementable during the year
for early enjoyment of benefits by the constituents; (c) BAC members to consolidate the
PPMPs of every office to create the Municipality’s APP in prescribed format to ensure
that all procurements are included; and (d) MA to (i) coordinate with the BAC Secretariat
for the submission of all the lacking supporting documents and written explanation for the
deficiencies noted; and (ii) check the completeness of supporting documentation prior to
payment.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to (i) pursue compliance with the
required allocation of LDRRMF based on 5% of the total estimated revenue from regular
sources; (ii) ensure full implementation of all PPAs programmed under the 70% MF; (b)
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) and MDRRMO
to provide specific timetable for the implementation of PPAs incorporated in the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to
facilitate their timely and complete implementation; (c) MA and MT to transfer only the
unexpended balance of the current year’s 30% Quick Response Fund (QRF) and 70% MF-
MOOE to the STF under the TL-DRRMF every year-end; (ii) revert to the GF the
unexpended balance of the MF-CO in previous years for the implementation of its
intended project; and (d) ensure that disbursements made against the 5% LDRRMF are
supported with complete and valid supporting documentation.

We recommended that the LCE require the ESWMB to (a) clearly define
target outputs for SWM PPAs in terms of their quality or quantity to evaluate the
effectiveness of implemented PPAs; (b) review Municipal Ordinance No. 622 dated
October 21, 2007, for full enforcement of its provisions, and immediately submit a
proposal for the amendment of obsolete provisions of the Ordinance, if warranted; (c)
collaborate with the Municipal Public Information Office to post SWM accomplishments
in the Municipal and barangay bulletins, as well as other public places within the
Municipality; and (d) enhance education campaigns on SWM, particularly focusing on
waste diversion, to meet the required waste diversion rate as per the SWMP and RA No.
9003.

We commended Management for its successful tax campaigns, which have
resulted in the actualization of targeted income for business taxes.

We also recommended that the (a) LCE require the (i) Municipal Business Permit and
Licensing Office (MBPLO) to (i.a) obtain copies of Sworn Statements of Gross Receipts
and/or ITR from business taxpayers upon renewal of their business permits and file them
together with other relevant documents in their office for check and balance; and (i.b)
require every business entity to submit their respective ITRs on or before April 30 of each
year, as provided under pertinent the pertinent provisions of Municipal Ordinance No.
831; and (b) SB members enact an ordinance adjusting the Municipality’s local tax rates
pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the ME to strictly monitor
compliance with the posting of signboards/tarpaulins at the project site of on-going
infrastructure projects, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30,
2013.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the GFPS to (a)
exert coordinated efforts with the implementing offices of the Municipality to adopt
strategies to ensure full implementation of GAD PPAs as planned and within the
budgetary limits; (b) improve project planning, execution and monitoring by
strengthening control policies in the utilization of the GAD Fund; and (c) submit a
revised GPB to the DILG for its approval of any amendments or updates thereto.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MA to (a)
request compromise agreement with the BIR to settle the unsupported due to BIR in the
amount of ₱1,192,437.65, if warranted; (b) immediately remit the unremitted taxes
withheld in CY 2023 totaling ₱331,960.71 to the BIR; and (c) facilitate prompt and intact
remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid the incurrence of interest and surcharges.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MHRMO Officer to immediately
notify GSIS of any updates regarding the Municipality’s personnel status and
correct/update their records accordingly; (b) MA to exert best effort to retrieve records and
documents supporting the prior years’ unremitted premiums/contributions to GSIS; and
(c) MT and MA to ensure timely and intact remittance of premiums and loan payments to
prevent the occurrence of unremitted premiums.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MHRMO Officer to timely
coordinate with the Pag-IBIG of any updates on the Municipality’s personnel status and
correct/update their records accordingly; (b) MA exert best effort to retrieve records and
documents supporting the prior years’ unremitted premiums withheld from employees to
facilitate immediate remittance; and (c) MT and MA to ensure timely and intact remittance
of Pag-IBIG premiums and loan payments to prevent the occurrence of unremitted
premiums.

We recommended that the LCE direct the (a) MHRMO Office to immediately
notify PhilHealth of any updates of the Municipality’s personnel on the status and
correct/update their records accordingly; (b) MA to exert best effort to retrieve records
and documents supporting the unsubstantiated prior years’ balances to facilitate
immediate remittance; and (c) MT and MA to ensure timely and intact remittance of all
the accrued PhilHealth premiums to prevent the occurrence of unremitted premiums.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE instruct Municipal officials
involve in the processing of claims to exercise diligence in evaluating the supporting
papers of disbursement vouchers to prevent the issuance of audit notices.

1.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MA and
the OIC, MT to (i) recognize in their cash records the identified discrepancies affecting
the CLT account; and (ii) reconcile their cash records regularly to immediately address
discrepancies, if any, for an accurate reporting of cash account balances; (b) OIC, MT to
(i) immediately notify the payees on the availability of their checks at least one month
before they become stale; (ii) promptly cancel stale checks and report them to the MAO
for recording in the books; and (iii) provide the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) of
the list of unclaimed checks at year-end for adjustment in the books; and (c) and (c) MA to
(i) immediately recognize and adjust in the books book reconciling items; (ii) monitor
and age all outstanding checks in the bank reconciliation statements (BRSs) to determine
stale checks for appropriate reversion in the CIB account; (iii) revert all unreleased
checks to the CIB and appropriate liability accounts as of year-end; and (iv) serve demand
letter to the former PC custodian for her outstanding PC advance.

1.16. During the exit conference, the MA and OIC, MT committed to regularly reconcile
their records and make the necessary adjustments in their records in compliance with the
audit recommendations.

2.12. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA to (i) serve demand letters
to all defaulting AOs and enforce the immediate settlement/liquidation of their
outstanding CAs; (ii) coordinate with the OIC, MT to cause the withholding of the
payment of salaries due to the AOs who are still in service in settlement of their
unliquidated CAs; (iii) submit the requisite documents to support the payments of
honoraria to the BAC and TWG members; (iv) strictly implement the (iv.a)
submission of complete SDs on the payment of honoraria; and (iv.b) prompt
liquidation of CAs; and (b) require the MT to ensure that all AOs are bonded to
protect the interest of the Municipality.

2.13. The MA committed to extend efforts in sending demand letters and to strictly
monitor the liquidation of CAs. The AT was also informed that disbursements for BAC
honoraria are already made through CAs by a bonded AO.

3.12. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Budget Office (MBO)
Officer to strictly adhere to the required allocation for CF; and (b) MA, in coordination
with the SDO and the authorized representative from the Office of the LCE, to (i) submit
to the AT the certified true copies of the noted requisite documents from the grant of CAs
for CF totaling ₱5,850,000.00; and (ii) submit the CA vouchers, along with their covering
transmittal and SDs to the AT within seven days after the release of check.

3.13. In concurrence with the audit recommendations, the concerned Management officials
vowed to comply with the recommendations and adhere to the rules and regulations on the
grant and liquidation of cash advances for the CF.

4.6. We recommended that the LCE require the MA and OIC, MT to regularly
coordinate with the Office of the Provincial Accountant to request a monthly report
on the receivables of the Municipality to serve as basis in recording the Due from
LGUs – PGB and Due to LGU- PGB accounts.

4.7. Management commented that the MA and OIC, MT will coordinate with the
Provincial Accountant regarding the noted discrepancies.

5.8. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) apply the PIM for
recording welfare goods in the books and maintain SLCs to substantiate the reported
inventory account balance; and (ii) record in the books the year-end balance of
welfare goods; and (b) deputized GSO Chief to strengthen internal controls over
welfare goods by maintaining SCs to facilitate proper recording and monitoring
thereof.

5.9. The LCE and concerned Municipal officials agreed to implement the audit
recommendations.

6.12. We recommended that the LCE consider immediately utilizing the “One-Time
Cleansing” of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, which
would help resolve the perennial issues with PPE accounts, ensure accurate PPE
account balances, and safeguard the Municipality’s properties from misuse and theft.

6.13. The GSO chief and MA concurred with the audit recommendations and claimed that
they had initiated the procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

7.15. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) responsible offices/departments
to satisfactorily and fully implement the PPAs funded by NGAs; (b) MA (i) and MT to
promptly return the unutilized balances from completed projects to their respective SAs,
including the previous years’ balances; (ii) to properly classify the fund transfers from
various NGAs with specific PPAs under the Due to NGAs account; (iii) reclassify
erroneous classification of funds transfers from Other Payables to Due to NGAs
account; and (c) Municipal Social Welfare Development Office (MSWDO) Officer to
provide the MA with the liquidation reports duly received by the DSWD for recording
in the books.

7.16. The concerned Management officials pledged to coordinate with the SAs of the
prior years’ fund transfers and promptly implement the audit recommendations. Also, a
clearance from the DSWD Regional No. 3 was received by the Municipality regarding the
liquidation of the ₱1,971,000.00 balance of the Social Pension for Senior Citizens.

8.22. We recommended that the (a) LCE require the OIC, MT and OIC, BPLO to (i)
carefully review the TOPs and ensure that the tax base is adequately supported with
audited financial statements, income tax returns, or notarized declarations of gross
sales/receipts for accurate and reliable bases of LBT assessment and collection; and
(ii) immediately address the identified concerns/issues that caused the under-
assessment of LBTs to avoid inequitable taxation and foregone revenues; and (b) SB
members enact a supplemental revenue ordinance to stipulate the lacking statutory
percentages/rates to be levied on local business taxpayers in accordance with the
provisions of RA No. 7160 and BLGF MC.

8.23. We also recommended that the LCE direct the MA to record the subsidies and
shares from the national government under the appropriate account classifications in
accordance with the RCA for LGUs.

8.24. At the exit conference, the concerned Management officials concurred with and
vowed to implement the audit recommendations.

9.7. We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE require the (a) deputized
MGSO Chief to (i) insure all its property, assets and interests with the General
Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System; and (ii) to
prepare the Property Inventory Form (PIF) and facilitate the submission of the
updated list of insurable properties with the GSIS by following the guidelines under
Section 5.1 of COA Circular No 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 so that necessary
insurance coverage can be applied with the GIF; and (b) Budget Committee to
include in the budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the
Municipality.

9.8. In agreement with the audit recommendations, the deputized MGSO Chief informed
the AT that they have prepared the PIF and committed to follow up on the status of
application for insurable properties with the GSIS.

10.9. We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the Municipal Development Council
(MDC) pursue sustainable socio-economic development through adequate planning and
optimal utilization of the 20% DF for priority development PPAs that are procurement and
implementation-ready; and (b) direct the BAC to strictly observe the rules and
regulations on posting of the required performance and warranty securities to safeguard
the government against faulty performance of contractors and uncorrected product
defects.

10.10. The responsible Management officials acknowledged the observations and
committed to implement the audit recommendations progressively.

11.13. We recommended that the LCE (a) implement the programmed PPAs as
planned to realize project benefits and achieve a disaster-resilient community; and (b)
require the (i) Head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office
(MDRRMO) Officer to formulate the annual LDRRMFIP using the prescribed format
to provide adequate performance measurements and information to stakeholders; and
(ii) MA to fast track the reconciliation of MAO records for LDRRMF, and with the
MBO records and facilitate the transfer of the unexpended balances of the 30% QRF
and 70% PF-MOOE to the TF books to ensure availability of funds for DRRM
activities in the next five years.

11.14. At the exit conference, the Head of the MDRRMO acknowledged the deficiencies
and pledged compliance with the rules and regulations on the planning, utilization, and
reporting for LDRRMF. Meanwhile, the MA conveyed that the unexpended PF-MOOE
and QRF were transferred to the TF books in May 2024.

12.17. We recommended that the LCE advise the MSWMB to (a) provide separate
areas for segregation and recycling of solid wastes in the MRF and oversee that each
function is performed in a systematic, efficient, and ecological manner; and (b) conduct
regular inspection of barangays’ MRFs and assess the availability of equipment needed
for sorting, recovery, recycling, and composting; and (c) heighten the citizen’s
consciousness and disseminate awareness about waste reduction, re-use, recycling,
composting and livelihood programs that use diverted wastes to curb the high volume
of solid wastes disposed of.

12.18. We further recommended that the SB allot sufficient budget for the necessary
improvements in the Central MRF to effectively carry out the sorting, recycling, and
composting mechanisms therein.

12.19. Management recognized the shortfalls in the implementation of solid waste
management and committed to execute the audit recommendations.

13.8. We recommended that the LCE direct the Chief of NMH and the Municipal
Health Officer to manage the health care wastes following the guidelines and
requirements of the DOH Health Care Waste Management Manual to prevent the
adverse impacts thereof to the patients, health care personnel, environment, and the
general public.

13.9. We also recommended that the SB allocate sufficient budget for health care
waste management programs, particularly for the provision of appropriate
containers and adequate disposal facilities for proper identification, segregation,
storage and disposal of health care wastes.

13.10. According to the NMH representative, they are preparing the requirements for
engaging a third-party service provider for the treatment and disposal of health care
wastes. The concerned officials acknowledged the audit recommendations and pledged
compliance therewith.

14.12. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) ME to (i) strictly follow the
latest DPWH regulations on the computation of ABCs of infrastructure projects to
establish proper bases of acceptable bid prices; and (ii) invoke the obligations of
contractors and cause the repair works in the projects with observed deficiencies so
as not to inflict further damage to the facilities pursuant to the warranty period
prescribed in RA No. 9184; and (b) BAC to require the awarded contractor to post
warranty securities in sufficient amounts and covering the warranty period prescribed
for the different classifications of infrastructure projects.

14.13. In compliance with the audit recommendations, the ME and the BAC members
committed to observe the rules and regulations on infrastructure projects.

15.10. We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the LCE require
the (a) BAC to submit to the AT the copies of approved construction contracts and
POs together with their SDs within five working days from the execution thereof to
facilitate review and other necessary audit procedures thereon; and (b) deputized
MGSO Chief to give prompt notice and submit to the AT the delivery receipts and
SDs on the schedule of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of the purchased
items.

15.11. Having reminded of the relevant rules and regulations, the responsible BAC and
MGSO personnel committed to promptly submit the approved contracts, POs, and
delivery documents to prevent the recurrence of the noted deficiencies.

16.9. We recommended that the LCE require the members of the GAD Focal Point
System to (a) continuously review all GAD-related regulations to enhance their
knowledge of the priority issues to be addressed through the Annual GAD Plan and
Budget in a realistic and attainable manner within the budget year; (b) maintain GAD
plan integration in their regular activities; and (c) require the concerned offices to
adopt effective strategies to fully implement GAD PPAs to achieve the GAD-related
targets.

16.10. At the exit conference, the members of the GFPS committed to extend efforts in
implementing the annual GPB in compliance with the audit recommendations.

17.16. We recommended that the LCE require the OIC, MT and MA to (a) strictly
comply with the law and regulations on the withholding of taxes from income
payments from the salaries and wages of Municipal officials and employees and the
goods and services procured and remitted within 10 days following the end of the
month the withholding was made; (b) continuously comply with the regulations on
the withholding of premiums/contributions, and loan amortizations from the salaries
and wages of Municipality officials and employees, and remittance thereof together
with the employer share within the prescribed period; and (b) exert their best efforts
to determine the prior years’ unreconciled balances.

17.17. During the exit conference, the OIC, MT and MA pledged to comply with the audit
recommendations and to coordinate efforts to trace and remit the prior years’ balances.

18.5. We recommended that the LCE enforce the settlement of the remaining
disallowances by the persons liable amounting to ₱7,357,934.92.

18.6. In concurrence with the audit recommendations, Management vowed to coordinate
with the former and current officials for immediate settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MT to deposit collections regularly and intact; and (b) MT and MA to continuously comply with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting of collections and deposits pursuant to above-mentioned regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to (i) apply the PIM in the recording of inventories upon purchase; (ii) make the necessary adjustment(s) in the books to properly recognize inventories on-hand at year-end and recognize as expense the distributed stocks accordingly; and (iii) maintain SLCs; (b) SO to (i) prepare and maintain SCs for control and monitoring of inventories; (ii) prepare and submit the SSMI to the MAO to facilitate the recording of issued inventories for the month; and (iii) conduct physical count of inventories and submit the results thereof to the MAO and AT for reference in audit; and (c) MT and MA to (i) reconcile inventory records; and (ii) establish an Inventory Management process indicating the required documents to be prepared upon receipt/delivery, issuance, and at month-end, including the deadline for submission of reports, and to whom the reports are to be submitted as prescribed by the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to ensure accuracy of records, promote operational efficiency, and encourage adherence to policies, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA and MT to maintain complete PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for monitoring, control and reconciliation of PPE records; (b) IC to (i) complete the physical inventory taking of PPE in accordance with the PIP to establish the existence and reliability of PPE account balances; (ii) collaborate with the MAO and MTO to ensure that all PPEs included in the ICF are reconciled with their respective PPE records; (iii) render a complete RPCPPE with the conditions and relevant information on PPE; and (iv) ensure that all insurable assets have insurance coverage with the GSIS to indemnify for the value of government properties lost through theft or any unforeseen events; (c) DAC to submit the relevant documents pertaining to the disposed PPE with total acquisition cost of ₱8,476,469.17; and (d) MA to recognize in the books the costs of the partially-completed projects as of year-end and henceforth require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to regularly submit to the MAO a copy of the status report of on-going projects for appropriate recognition in the books.

We recommended that the LCE, as head of the MDC, (a) continue to improve and enhance the Municipality’s development planning and project prioritization to ensure that its manner of appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF is invariably compliant with the prescribed criteria; (b) address the issue on timely completion of the programmed development projects; and (c) aim full implementation of PPAs at the set time to ensure the continuity, consistency, and strength of the LGU’s development efforts towards the attainment of targets over the long run.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) ME/BAC Chairperson and MA to undertake the following actions: (i) identify and address the root causes of the deficiencies related to the supporting documents; and (ii) submit the necessary documents for the transactions with deficiencies; (b) ME (i) to coordinate with the contractors concerned for the installation of project signboards at designated sites and provide evidence of their installation; (ii) to adhere to DPWH guidelines in determining the Approved Budget for the Contracts (ABCs) to prevent undue inflation of project cost; and (iii) as BAC Chairperson, subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are met for the use of alternative procurement methods; and (c) MA to ensure that (i) advance payments equivalent to 15% of the contract price are substantiated with the required documentation; and (ii) supporting documents are sufficient and valid before any payments are made.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MDRRMO Officer to (i) submit the updated MDRRMC and MDRRMO organizational structure to the AT; (ii) implement fully PPAs for disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness at the set time to avoid losses from hazards as well as to reduce the intensity of future disasters; (iii) prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, reflecting the allocation of 30% for QRF and 70% for Mitigation Fund, including a list of projects and activities charged to unexpended LDRRMF from previous years; (iv) strictly adhere to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 regarding the release and use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and (v) ensure that the MDRRMF shall be used solely to support disaster risk management activities; (b) MBO to (i) prepare a separate RAOs for MDRRMF; and (ii) ensure that changes in the annual budget or AIP shall be supported with approved SB resolution; (c) BAC Chairperson/Secretariat to (i) post in the PhilGEPS the RFQ and Award for the procurements covered by the alternative mode of procurement – Negotiated Procurement – SVP and Emergency Cases; and (ii) issue BAC Resolutions recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for approval of the LCE; (d) MA and BAC Chairperson to submit the requisite documentary requirements which include list of recipients and pictures of actual distribution of welfare goods; proof of posting of RFQ in the PhilGEPS website, website of the Municipality, if available, and at any conspicuous place reserved for this purpose in the premises of the Municipality; and (e) MA to (i) transfer the unexpended LDRRMF under the account TL - DRRMF in the Trust Fund books at year-end; (ii) submit on a monthly basis the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; and (iii) ensure that the necessary documents supporting payment claims are valid and complete before payments are made.

We recommended that LCE (a) expedite the full utilization and implementation of the identified development projects under the trust fund for optimum attainment of the intent of the fund; (b) require the MA (i) in coordination with the ME, BAC Chairperson, and MSWDO to submit the documents with noted deficiencies to the AT; (ii) exert efforts in identifying the source of funds for the unused balances amounting to ₱3,260,092.48; (iii) if warranted, file the request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers duly supported with relevant documents; and (iv) conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of fund transfers to source NGAs, and other government and non-government agencies; (c) require the BAC Chairperson to (i) post clarifications, modifications, changes, or amendments to the Bidding Documents through the issuance of the corresponding Supplemental/Bid Bulletin in the PhilGEPS website; (ii) cause the posting of RFQs in the PhilGEPS website in accordance with Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; (iii) cause the preparation of BAC Resolutions recommending to the HOPE the mode of procurement and the award of contract in favor of the supplier or contractor with the Single or Lowest Calculated and Responsive Quotation; and (iv) subject all procurements to public bidding unless the conditions are met for the use of alternative procurement methods.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MT and MA to reconcile their respective books to correct the noted discrepancy of ₱211,063.21 on the recorded income from business taxes; and (b) MT and BPLO Officer to (i) continue to comply with the prescribed government laws and procedures in the systematic processing of application for new and renewal of business permits; and (ii) enforce procedures and available remedies to promote taxpayer compliance and in addressing tax delinquencies as provided in Section 6.B.02, Article B of the MRC.

We recommended that the LCE require the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the SB to (a) request the presence of the Lessee for the discussion of workable solutions to address the issues that hamper the operation of the public market and shopping mall; (b) undertake a full review of the MOA with Springfont Realty Corporation to identify indications of default and assert viable options for the continuance or cancellation of the lease agreement and take back the leased asset in accordance with applicable laws; and (c) investigate the alleged payment of ₱1,800,000.00 by the Lessee to the Municipality for the advance rental.

We recommended that the LCE (a) coordinate with the SB for the rationalization of the MOA including options for its amendment or modification and payments of royalty fees to the PFDA for the past 20 years; (b) require the MT to trace their records from CY 2004 to present to obtain information on the income from fish port operation and submit the same to PFDA; and (c) require the MA to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for a liability account showing the unpaid retainer’s fee to the PFDA.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) closely monitor the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted posted in the ledger to trace discrepancies, if any and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (b) strictly comply with BIR No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-2018 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Human Resource and Management Office (HRMO) Officer to (i) update the individual record of GSIS members for proper monitoring; (ii) coordinate with the GSIS to resolve the discrepancies between the premiums and contributions and loan payments deducted with the amount remitted to GSIS; and (iii)) submit updated records on time and reconcile with the record of GSIS; (b) MA to review the contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace discrepancies, if any and make the necessary adjustments if warranted; and (c) HRMO Officer and MA to strictly comply with RA No. 8291 on the withholding and full remittance of GSIS contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) HRMO Officer to (i) maintain individual records of employees for proper monitoring; (ii) coordinate with the Pag-IBIG to resolve the discrepancies between the premiums and contributions; and (iii) submit the records of new members on time to avoid recurrence of discrepancies; (b) MA to review the contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace discrepancies, if any and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (c) HRMO Officer and MA to strictly comply with RA No. 9679 and its IRR on the withholding and full remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) HRMO Officer to submit the records of new members on time to avoid recurrence of discrepancies; (b) MA to review the contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the ledger to trace discrepancies, if any and make the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (c) HRMO Officer and MA to strictly comply with RA No. 7875, as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606 and its RIRR on the withholding and full remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO Officer to (a) ensure that payrolls are duly supported with the biometric system-generated DTRs and manual DTRs for personnel assigned outside the Municipal building; (b) attach a certification stating that the activities/services of COS personnel cannot be provided by regular personnel to payments of first claims of salaries; (c) require the COS and JO personnel to submit Accomplishment Report on every payroll period; and (d) require the use of locator slips for activities/events/errands that would require leaving or going out of the workplace during office hours within the day.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) BAC Chairperson and other Municipal Officials concerned to (i) strictly adhere to the procedural guidelines in the implementation of infrastructure projects through Pakyaw Contracting as provided under Appendix 1 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (ii) submit a written explanation for the unprepared technical and bidding documents on the implemented infrastructure projects through Pakyaw Contracting; and (b) MA to ensure that the documentary requirements for the procurement of infrastructure projects through Pakyaw Contracting are submitted and attached to DVs for timely verification and audit of accounts.

We recommended that the LCE (a) undertake innovative strategies that are more responsive to the needs of the community and develop mechanisms to motivate waste generators such as households, market stall owners, commercial and industrial establishments on proper handling, segregating, collection, and disposal of wastes; (b) set-up and operate a barangay or subdivision-based MRF model to showcase proper handling and disposal of wastes; (c) train waste collectors on best methods of solid waste collection and handling; and (d) operate the Municipal MRF in compliance with environmental standards and best practices.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA to (a) submit the financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and (b) make the necessary adjustment in the books for all unreleased checks at the end of the year, supported with the Report of Unreleased Checks certified by the MT.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Chairperson to strictly comply with the provisions of the above-mentioned COA Circulars on timely furnishing of copies of contracts/POs and notification to the AT of the scheduled delivery of goods for random verification.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to (a) submit the details of the GAD AR and supporting documents to the AT for further audit of the expenses charged against GAD fund; (b) initiate the capacitation of key members of the GFPS on the use of HGDG tool to guarantee the integration of gender concerns in the design, management, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the programs and projects and to determine the efficiency and effectiveness of the LGU’s mainstreaming efforts; and (c) create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team to evaluate the outcomes of the GAD PPAs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer (MT) to provide the MAO with a copy of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks at year-end; and (b) MA to (i) recognize in the books the five confirmed bank accounts to ensure the presentation of accurate and reliable financial statements; (ii) continuously comply with the timely preparation of BRSs and adjustment of reconciling items in the books; and (iii) revert the unreleased checks at year-end to the CIB-LCCA account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MT and MA to (a) consistently adhere to the reporting and accounting guidelines for collections and deposits pursuant to existing regulations; and (b) collaborate and reconcile their respective records to promptly identify and rectify errors, if any.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MA to (i) consistently record inventories and semi-expendable properties in their respective asset accounts; and (ii) ensure that the derecognition of inventories and semi-expendable properties is supported with sufficient supporting documents; and (iii) make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the two equipment initially recorded as expenses; and (b) GSO to (i) prepare and submit SSMI to the MAO; (ii) issue ICS for every issuance of semi-expendable properties to establish accountability; (iii) maintain RegSPI for proper monitoring of semi-expendable properties; and (iv) use the prescribed format of RPCI for submission to the MAO and AT semi-annually.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) MA to coordinate with the ME to retrieve the project details from the CIP accounts for reclassification into appropriate PPE accounts, if warranted; (b) GSO Head to (i) utilize the prescribed reports format such as RPCPPE, RPCSP, and RLRN to accurately document the results of physical counts of PPE; and (ii) cause the completion of the prepared IIRUP to facilitate the disposal process of unserviceable PPE; and (c) MT to update the registration with LTO and insurance with GSIS for all motor vehicles owned by the Municipality.

We also recommended and the LCE agreed to establish a Titling Committee, responsible with the validation of parcels of lands owned by the Municipality and processing the titling of the Municipality’s legally-owned parcels of land.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and adjust their balances accordingly.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue to satisfactorily implement the PPAs funded by NGAs and promptly return the unutilized balances to their respective source agencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the ME to (i) immediately submit a written satisfactory explanation supported with complete documentation and photos on the noted inconsistencies on the projects “Supply and Installation of Solar Lights” and “Construction of Additional Niches”; (ii) require the contractors concerned to immediately repair the noted major cracks on concrete and asphalted pavements; and (iii) compel the contractors to install the required signboard at the project site showing its specifications for the purpose of transparency; (b) require the ME, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat, and MA to (i) submit the lacking documents to the AT for further audit and (ii) henceforth, attach all required documentary requirements to the DVs prior to processing of payment; (c) require the BAC Secretariat to post the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS; and (d) require the MA to withhold retention money from all infrastructure projects as required by the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) MA to (i) transfer the unexpended DRRMF under the account “Trust Liability - DRRM” in the Trust Fund books at year-end; (ii) submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF monthly; (iii) submit the noted deficient supporting documents and henceforth, ensure that the necessary documents are valid and complete before payment is made to suppliers; and (iv) submit immediately the eight DVs totaling ₱11,531,070.00 to the AT for post-audit; and (b) MDRRMO to submit the revised MDRRMFIP for the inclusion of the project “Repairs and Maintenance of Evacuation Center”.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) provide resources to fully operate the Central MRF which would reduce the residual waste being hauled and collected by the service provider; (b) strictly enforce the sanctions on violators of the ordinances/laws/regulations pertaining to solid waste management; and (c) further intensify and involve the barangay governments in monitoring the compliance of their constituents with environmental laws, rules and ordinances.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MENRO Officer to (a) require the maintenance of a permanent record/logbook at the Central MRF for monitoring; (b) review and strengthen their internal control measures to ensure verification of supplier’s billings against records maintained by the respective offices; and (c) distribute immediately the garbage collection motorcycles to immediately serve their purpose.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) exhaust all possible actions to enforce the collection of real property tax delinquencies, which may include conducting public awareness campaigns and streamlining process, among others; and (b) coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) members to review the existing local policies and regulations, identify areas for improvement, and implement reforms to strengthen local tax administration.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) review the amounts of taxes withheld and remitted/posted to ensure correctness of data and avoid discrepancies; (b) immediately remit the unremitted taxes withheld in the amount of ₱915,747.06 to the BIR; and (c) practice intact remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR within the prescribed period to avoid incurring surcharges and penalties to be charged against the erring employees.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to (a) conduct regular reconciliation of records with the GSIS, HDMF and PHIC personnel to immediately address the employees’ unremitted premiums; and (b) remit within the prescribed period the amount withheld to avoid the payment of penalties that may be imposed by these agencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the GFPG and its TWG to execute fully its functions and ensure the implementation of HGDG to fully comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Circular No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01; and (b) consider the creation of GAD Office to maintain a focused, concentrated, and coordinated response in the administration, planning, implementation, and reporting of GAD related PPAs.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE (a) require the IT to complete
the physical count of Municipality-owned properties to enable the submission of a
complete RPCPPE within the prescribed deadline, and to allow the reconciliation of
the physical inventory results with the MAO and GSO records; (b) require the
Municipal Accountant (MA) and GSO Officer to strictly comply with the required
maintenance of PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE to record all
acquisitions, adjustments, and disposals thereof; (c) create a Special Committee to
facilitate the titling of the 33 parcels of land under the name of the Municipality; and
(d) require the MA to recognize in the books the 33 parcels of land with an assessed
value of ₱42,815,600.00.

1.3 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the
(a) Municipal Treasurer (MT) and Municipal Accountant (MA) to (i) execute full
automation of collections to minimize manual interventions, reduce errors, and
enhance efficiency and transparency in the reporting of collections; and (ii) regularly
reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors; and
(b) MT to direct the LO to closely verify the RCDs submitted by AOs particularly to
ensure that all issued accountable forms are reported;

2.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) General Services
Office (GSO) Officer to facilitate the registration and transfer of ownership of 11
motor vehicles in the name of the Municipality; and (b) MA to (i) record in the books
the unrecorded transportation equipment funded by TF; and (ii) in coordination with
GSO Officer to submit the lacking requisite documents on the purchase of a parcel of
land.

3.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal officials
concerned to strictly adhere to the regulations provided in COA-DBM-DILG-
Governance Commission for GOCCs-DND JC No. 2015-01 to avoid the recurrence of
similar deficiencies.

4.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA, in coordination
with other concerned Municipal Offices to (a) exert efforts in identifying the source
of funds for the unexpended balances totaling ₱1,049,028.32. Otherwise, prepare the
necessary documents to request a write-off from the books as prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2016-005, if warranted; and (b) prepare subsidiary ledger (SL) for each
fund transfer and conduct regular and periodic analysis and validation with the
source NGAs.

1.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) MT to secure SB Resolution to expedite the reactivation and closure of the dormant accounts and transfer the balance to a TF account under the name of the Municipality; and (b) MA and MT to continuously implement the strong internal control over cash management pursuant to existing regulations.

2.4. We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to (a) ensure that (i) only designated and bonded SDOs are granted time-bound CAs, except for official travelling expenses; (ii) the documentary requirements to be attached to LRs are submitted by the implementing Offices concerned for timely verification and audit of accounts; (iii) the cash-handling SDOs are sufficiently bonded and that CAs granted to SDOs are limited to their maximum accountability; and (iv) the payments for regular expenditures are made directly to suppliers in the form of check; and (b) require the SDOs to submit the lacking supporting documents of the excepted transactions.

3.5. We recommended that the LCE instruct (a) all requesting departments to observe proper handling of inventories by receiving the same through the SO and not directly from Suppliers; (b) SO to (i) conduct a regular physical count of inventories and submit the RPCI to the MAO and AT for reference in audit; and (ii) inspect all incoming inventories and prepare RIS and SSMI to document its issuance to end-users; and (c) MA to strictly record all acquisitions of inventories to the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize equivalent expense accounts upon receipt of the SSMI supported with RIS from the SO.

4.4. We recommended that the LCE (a) temporarily substitute or relieve the members of the IC from their usual workload for them to prioritize and complete the annual physical count of PPEs and render the RPCPPE within three months or less; (b) require the MT to (i) as the focal person for land registration, expedite the titling process of the remaining five parcels of land under the name of the Municipality; and (ii) maintain PCs; (c) require the MA to (i) prepare PPELCs and regularly update their records for adequate monitoring of PPEs; (ii) provide the specific details of the PPEs described in generic terms through retrieval of related documents; and (iii) in coordination with the GSO, exert effort to secure the deed of donation or OR-CR of the donated fire truck from the donor or his descendants to facilitate its proper recording and immediate disposal; and (d) require the Municipal Disposal Committee to cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets based on the technical assessment of the COA TSO and provisions of National Budget Circular No. 425.

5.9. We recommended that the LCE require BPLO Chief and MT to continuously work towards achieving the projected income outlined in the appropriation ordinance by (a) maintaining reliable and complete business taxpayers’ ledger cards to monitor collections and identify delinquent taxpayers; (b) conducting random examinations of business taxpayers’ books of accounts; (c) ensuring that all essential documents for the computation of business taxes are maintained and submitted by business taxpayers upon registration and renewal; and (d) levying business taxes in accordance with the provisions of the LRC.

6.9. We recommended that the LCE continuously comply with the requirements on the grant of cash advance and submission of liquidation thereof to ICFAU as provided in the COA–DBM–DILG-Governance Commission for GOCCs - DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

7.9. We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA to immediately return the unutilized fund transfer of ₱15,760.52 to the source agency; (b) require the implementing department to execute the PPAs within the time frame; (c) require the BAC to submit the required documents on the noted transactions and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.

8.18. We recommended that the LCE (a) as head of the MDC, continue to improve and enhance the Municipality's development planning and project prioritization for the 20% DF to guarantee its consistent application of the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; (b) require the ME to fully implement the PPAs within the time frame; (c) require the BAC Chairperson to submit the requisite documents in compliance with the provisions of 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and (d) require the MA to ensure that supporting documents are valid and complete before payments are made to suppliers.

9.8. We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MA to consistently adhere to the above-mentioned pertinent provisions of RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure the availability of DRRMF for the implementation of disaster risk reduction management activities of the Municipality; and (b) MDRRMC members exert their effort to identify cost-effective preparedness and risk reduction projects to be funded by the DRRMF under the STF.

10.6. We recommended that the LCE require (a) the MDRRMO Officer to optimize the utilization of the fund by implementing the programmed PPAs for the year to lessen the socio economic and environmental impacts of a disaster in the community; (b) the MDRRM Council to appropriate the STF on preparedness and mitigation PPAs according to its purpose to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to build a resilient community; (c) the BAC to issue resolutions recommending the use of alternative mode of procurement for approval of the LCE; and (d) the MA to ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payments to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.

11.6. We recommended that the LCE require the SO in-charge of accepting deliveries of procured items to (a) submit the advance copies of PO within the prescribed period; and (b) comply strictly with the submission of delivery documents to the AT within 24 hours upon acceptance thereof.

12.6. We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairperson of the SWMB, in coordination with the MENRO and Barangays, to exert more efforts in their campaign towards safe and sanitary SWM by (a) establishing operational and accessible MRFs in all of its 34 Barangays; (b) requiring business establishments to (i) put preference in using biodegradable packing materials; (ii) install different waste receptacles and eco-friendly signages; (c) discourage littering and open burning by imposing penalties against violators; and (d) focus on the repair and maintenance of damaged/idle machinery to improve its composting and recycling performance.

13.5. We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO and MA to continue to comply with RA No. 7875, as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606 and its RIRR on the withholding and full remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.
14.6. We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO and MA to continue to comply with the timely remittance of withheld GSIS contributions and loan repayments.

15.5. We recommended that the LCE require the HRMO and MA to continuously comply with RA No. 9679 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations on the withholding and remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.
16.4. We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to continuously comply with the pertinent laws on the timely and intact remittance of withheld taxes.

17.4. We recommended that the LCE require (a) the GFPS to (i) continue its faithful compliance with the budgetary requirement for the GAD Fund; and (ii) exert effort in mobilizing its proponent offices to accomplish all the planned PPAs and fully utilize the GAD budget in the succeeding years; and (b) the MPDO to spearhead the setting up, maintenance, and updating of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

18.4. We recommended that the LCE (a) strictly enforce the settlement of the audit disallowances considering that the prescribed periods of settlement had already lapsed; and (b) require the Municipal officials concerned to ensure that succeeding transactions comply with the existing regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

We reiterated our
recommendation that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) require
(a) the City Accountant (CA) to
retrieve the prior years’ records
such as bank statements and
report of collections and deposits
to facilitate the recording of the
deposits that remained
unadjusted/unrecorded in the
books; and
(b) the City Treasurer (CT) and
CA to be persistent in
communicating with the PNB
Head Office to establish the
details of the unidentified bank
account.

We recommended that the LCE
require the CGSO Officer to
(a) reconcile the results of the
physical inventory and property
records with the book inventory;
(b) undertake collaborative
efforts with the CA to ensure
that all PPE included in the
RPCPPE are recorded in PCs;
and
(c) obtain the subsidiary records
from the CAO, compare the
same with their RPCPPE and
expedite the conduct of physical
inventory of all PPE to avail the
“One-Time” cleansing of PPE
accounts.

We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) CT to (i) fast track the
updating of the CLT to be used
in the setting up of RPT/SET
Receivables in the ensuing year;
(ii) compare and validate the
names in the Report of RPT and
SET delinquencies with the
Assessment Roll of the City
Assessor’s Office for any
discrepancies to adopt
appropriate action; and
(iii) intensify tax collection
campaign and institute legal

collections efficiency;
(b) City Assessor to conduct
mapping of properties subject to
RPT/SET to cleanse and provide
a reliable Assessment Roll; (c)
CA to establish the RPT/SET
Receivable accounts based on
the CLT prepared by the CTO;
and
(d) City Officials concerned to
undertake periodic reconciliation
of the balances per master list of
RPT delinquencies with that of
the RPT and SET Receivable

immediately address

We recommended that the LCE
direct the CA to classify and
present the current portion of the
long-term loans payable
amounting to ₱54,651,975.24
under the current liability, and
the remaining amount which is
due for settlement beyond one
year as non-current liability in
the Statement of Financial
Position (SFP).

We recommended that the LCE
direct the CA and the CT to
strictly comply with the
regulations on the submission of
accounts and financial reports to
avoid delays in the verification
and conduct of audit-related
activities.

We recommended that the LCE
instruct the
(a) CHO Officer to provide the
CAO with the monthly report on
the receipt, issuance and
balances of vaccines and other
donations in-kind received by
the City related to COVID-19
vaccination program;
(b) CA to recognize in the books
the cost of the ending inventory
of vaccines as at year-end and to
recognize in the books future
receipts and issuances of the
same; and
(c) closely monitor the
expiration dates of the COVID-
19 vaccines received to prioritize
utilization of those which are
near expiry.

We recommended that the LCE
through the Solid Waste
Management Board
(a) fast track the establishment
of the MRF of Barangay Lawa;
(b) encourage active
participation of Barangay
officials to support the solid
waste management programs of
the City especially the

and
(c) exert efforts in improving the
City’s waste diversion activities
incorporated in its 10-year Solid
Waste Management Plan to
decrease spending on waste
disposal.

We recommended that the LCE
require the HRMO to continue
its compliance with RA No.
7160 as to the hiring of JOS and
COS as well as in the
maintenance of adequate
personnel records.

We recommended that the LCE
instruct the
(a) Local Development Council
(LDC) (i) address the issue on
generic PPAs by including the
brief description and details of
the PPAs in the AIP for the
ensuing year; and
(ii) improve and enhance the
City’s development planning
and project prioritization to
ensure that its manner of
appropriation and utilization of
the 20% DF is compliant with
the prescribed criteria thereon;
(b) City Urban Planning and
Development Office (CUPDO)
to closely monitor the progress
of implementation of PPAs
included in the AIP; and
(c) instruct the implementing
Offices to implement the
developmental projects in
accordance with their
targeted/planned time frame.

We recommended that the LCE
continue to satisfactorily
implement the PPAs funded by
NGAs and promptly return the
unutilized balances to their
respective Agency sources.

We recommended that the LCE
require the CA and CT to
continuously comply with BIR
RMC 23-2012 on the remittance
of taxes withheld within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) require the CA and CT to
continue deducting and remitting
GSIS premiums and loan
amortizations including
government share within the
prescribed period; and
(b) instruct the concerned CAO
personnel to maintain subsidiary
records of all GSIS members for
close monitoring of individual
member’s accounts.

We recommended that the LCE
require the CA and CT to
consistently comply with the
regulations on withholding and
remittance of Pag–IBIG
premiums and loan
amortizations within the
prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE
require the CA and CT to
continue to comply with the
withholding of premiums and
remittance thereof to PhilHealth.

We recommended that the LCE
(a) instruct the (i) officials
involved in the processing of
claims to be more diligent in
evaluating supporting papers of
claims to avoid suspensions and
disallowances in audit; and
(ii) require the responsible and
liable persons the immediate
refund of disallowed claims once
these have become final and
executory; and
(b) CA to comply with the
revised documentary
requirements for common
government transactions
prescribed under COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012 to establish the regularity
of disbursements.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City Mayor require the (a) CGSO Officer to prepare and submit to the OIC, CA the RSMI duly supported with the issued Requisition and Issue Slips (RISs) to enable the (a) OIC, City Accountant to record accurately all the deliveries received and issued in the ensuing years; (b) OIC, CA to (i) immediately furnish the CGSO the proper account classification of the supplies and materials upon receipt of such deliveries by the latter; and (ii) designate a personnel from the CAO and CGSO to update the recording of the inventories to arrive at the correct balance of the Inventory accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the OIC, CA to (a) recognize payables based on valid obligations duly supported with adequate documentation to present an accurate financial position of the City; (b) consider doing the right course of action over other payables aging more than five and ten years in consonance with Articles 1144 and 1149 of the Civil Code; and (c) reclassify to the proper account classification the Interest Income account totaling ₱1,528,253.60 recorded under the Due to LGUs account.
observations.reason: Payables are being recognized based on valid obligations like Delivery Invoices, Inspection and Acceptance Reports and Statement of Work Accomplished. Reclassified to Due to Other Funds –GF Account for subsequent transfer and budgeting purposes per JEV No. 300-23-07-0025 dated 07/05/2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor continuously lead the LDC in the promotion of social, economic, and environmental developments through appropriate planning and utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Budget Officer to (a) see to it that all expenditures charged to an appropriation are relevant to the purpose for which the funds are appropriated; and (b) submit to the Audit Team a justification on the charging of expenditures for anti-rabies vaccines and serums amounting to ₱3,145,690.00 against the funds for COVID-19 Vaccination Program.
observations.reason: Based on the submitted justification, the object of expenditure included all types of medicines, not only COVID-19 vaccines, thus there was no inappropriate charging of anti-rabies vaccines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to coordinate with the OIC, CA in reporting the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF using the prescribed format to facilitate proper review of disaster-related transactions.
observations.reason: The prescribed format of the Report on Utilization was adopted in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Local School Board to (a) ensure that the desired objectives for all purchases under the SEF are met; and (b) support all expenditures with relevant and appropriate documentation.

observations.reason: Guidelines were already submitted and approved by the LCE regarding implementations of LGU rice program. The office strictly followed the approved guidelines; Consolidation of required documents before processing of payments; Continuous review of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to (i) trace the discrepancies between the reported acquisition costs of the PPE accounts and the lapsing schedule for depreciation to provide a reliable balances for depreciation expenses and accumulated depreciation accounts; (ii) exhaust all efforts to retrieve the records of the PPE accounts under the TF books to facilitate their transfer to GF books and provide an allowance for their depreciation; (b) GSO to (i) render a complete RPCPPE for reconciliation with the MAO’s property records; (ii) safekeep the Municipality’s properties; (c) Disposal Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to account for all unserviceable PPEs to facilitate their immediate disposal; and (d) MA and GSO Officer to prepare and maintain their property records. We also recommended and the LCE agreed to facilitate the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for fair presentation of PPE account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
(a) (i) The Municipal Accountant traced the details of the reported PPE, however, some were still unaccounted.
(a) (ii) The Municipal Accounting Office already provided allowance for depreciation expense on some depreciable PPE.
(b) Inventory Committee created a new inventory committee to take on the task of completing the physical count. However, it was not completed and no reconciliation with accounting records was done yet.
(c) Disposal will be accounted for during the inventory count.
observations.reason: Unable to collect all the details of all PPE and there was no proper turn over of the previous in charge for inventory teams

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) Chief and implementing Offices to (i) implement the development PPAs as planned and establish a clear order of priority in their implementation; and (ii) to implement the intended development projects under the continuing appropriations of the 20% DF; (b) in coordination with the Local Development Council (LDC), ensure thar the programmed PPAs are procurement and implementation ready for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents; and (c) to require the MA to (i) submit the lacking documents on the aforementioned disbursements; and (ii) verify the completeness of the supporting documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction:
a) The 20% development fund has undergone consultations with relevant concerned offices, particularly the Municipal Engineering Office, the Municipal and the Municipal Development Council to ensure that development plans are within the municipality priorities and effectively address local needs.
(c) The BAC Secretariat will be instructed to submit all documents required by the Audit Team within the prescribed period. Training on government procurement was attended by BAC Secretariat members and a local training for personnel involved in procurement was initiated by members of the BAC Secretariat. Efforts are being made to place additional dedicated personnel to procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team within the prescribed period all advance copies of perfected contracts, POs and pre-repair evaluation report, and their supporting documents; (b) BAC Members, Secretariat and TWG to strictly comply with the required documentary requirements provided by the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement of projects through public bidding, including the proper mechanism on the use of the PhilGEPS; (c) the Supply/ Inspection Officer responsible for accepting deliveries to notify the AT of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries for verification of the existence and condition of the supplies, materials and equipment pursuant to the above-stated provisions of COA Circulars No. 2009-001, 2009-002 and 95-006; and (d) submit written justification for the noted deficiencies and the requisite documents mentioned above, for evaluation of the AT.
observations.managementAction: BAC Secretariat members attended training on government procurement; a local training for personnel involved in procurement was initiated by members of the BAC Secretariat. Efforts are being made to secure dedicated personnel to procurement. Supply and Inspection officers are promptly informed of any deliveries and were instructed to notify the Audit Team of deliveries. Copies of contracts, POs, notice of deliveries and pre-repair evaluation reports and supporting documents will be submitted by the BAC Secretariat.
observations.reason: Procurement documentation voluminous and some requirements were overlooked; efforts to assign additional dedicated personnel to procurement are ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) ensure the MA and MT’s continuous compliance with the withholding and remittance of appropriate taxes within the prescribed period; and (b) require the MA to (i) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the Due to BIR account balance as at year-end for appropriate action; and (ii) record transactions in the books as they occur.
observations.managementAction: a) MA to continously comply with the withholding and remittance of taxes. b) MA to retrieve prior years' records and to record transactions in the books as they occur.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to (i) update the GSIS records with the status of the municipal employees to avoid the under and over-remittances of premiums and loan repayments withheld from employees’ compensation; and (b) MA to (i) maintain subsidiary records to support the general ledger accounts balance; (ii) retrieve prior years’ records to determine the details of the Due to GSIS account balance at year-end for appropriate action; and (iii) record transactions in the books as they occur.
observations.managementAction: On going. Update of new employee status, continuous update of premiums, appointments received and released by CSC are updated right away. MAO already doing the details. MAO to maintain subsidiary ledgers to support the GL accounts.
observations.reason: Appointment records and personnel status updates were pending; continuous CSC action and updates required

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MA to trace the difference in the Due to LGUs account of ₱2,189.90 between the general ledger and financial statements; (b) HRMO Officer and MA to check the detailed list provided by Pag-IBIG vis-à-vis the payroll to prevent the under and over-remittances of premiums and loan payments to Pag-IBIG.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments to be made. MA traced that the difference was caused by a double entry under SEF which will be adjusted. HRMO and MA to check detailed list provided by Pag-IBIG vis-à-vis payroll.
observations.reason: Differences due to double entry and un-updated employee information; reconciling and adjustments ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) HRMO to (i) verify the employee list uploaded in the EPRS to ensure that the list is correct and up to date to avoid under and over-remittances of PhilHealth premiums; and (ii) counter check the amounts of premiums due per employee on the generated SPA vis-à-vis the withheld per payroll to trace discrepancies; and (b) the MA (i) to maintain subsidiary records of Due to PhilHealth account for proper monitoring of account balance; and (ii) to ensure that payments are recorded in the books based on actual payment date.
observations.managementAction: HRMO and Accounting continuously checking and updating premiums. MA prepared subsidiary ledgers and to maintain details. MA to ensure payments are recorded based on actual payment date.
observations.reason: Un-updated employee information in EPRS caused discrepancies; ongoing verification and reconciliation required
