applicable laws, rules and
regulations on personnel
management and payments for
their services.

We recommended that the LCE require the:
(a) MT to fast track the updating of the Certified List of Taxpayers (CLT) to be used in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in the ensuing year; and
(b) Municipal Accountant (MA) to establish the RPT/SET Receivable accounts based on the CLT prepared by the MTO.

We recommended that the LCE:
(a) utilize the 20% DF as planned through implementation of programmed PPAs to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU;
(b) require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to ensure that the infrastructure projects conform with the documentary requirements provided by the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport; and
(c) require the MA to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects prior to payment.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
(a) RHU Officer to provide the MAO with the monthly report on the receipt, issuance and balances of vaccines and other donations in-kind received by the Municipality related to COVID-19 vaccination program;
(b) MA to recognize in the books the cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end; and
(c) closely monitor the expiration dates of the COVID-19 vaccines received to prioritize utilization of those which are near expiry.

We recommended that the LCE continuously:
(a) implement the PPAs funded by NGAs;
(b) return the unutilized balance of trust funds to source agency; and
(c) submit the MOA pertaining to the funds received from the PENRO for further review.

We recommended that the LCE require the head of the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to
(a) fast track the establishment of MRF of all barangays to fully comply with the law; and
(b) amend the existing Municipal Ordinance on waste segregation in the wake of garbage crisis and impose appropriate sanctions to violators.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and MT to continuously observe the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) (a) commend (a) the MA and MT for complying with
the guidelines on the reporting, (b) Implemented
accounting, regular deposit of
collections, and regular
reconciliation of their records for
early detection and correction of
errors; and (b) advise them to
continuously discharge their
respective functions efficiently and
effectively.

We recommended that the LCE
require the MA to (a) ensure that all (a) Implemented
recorded obligations in the books are
properly supported by valid and (b) Implemented
sufficient evidence and conformed
to by suppliers and authorized
officials of the Municipality; and (b)
prepare the necessary adjusting entry
on the under-recording of AP by
₱43,597.87.

(a) Implemented
We recommended that the LCE (a)
require the members of the Local (b) Implemented
Finance Committee (LFC) to
continue monitoring and conducting
periodic reviews of performance
levels, especially those revenues that
missed the projected income; and (b)
require the MT to intensify further
revenue collection efforts to
generate more income for the
Municipality and enable it to
implement priority development
projects and deliver the basic
services to its constituents.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed, as head of the Local Implemented
Development Council (LDC), to
continue to improve and enhance the
Municipality’s development
planning and project prioritization to
ensure that its manner of
appropriation and utilization of the
20% DF is invariably compliant
with laws, rules and regulations.
Consequently, only PPAs that would
materially contribute to the
development of the Municipality for
the benefit of its constituents should
be included in its AIP.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the MHO to plan
properly the utilization and
distribution of COVID-19 vaccines.

We recommended that the LCE (a) Implemented
require the BAC and Technical
Working Group (TWG) to (a) (b) Implemented
explain in writing their failure to
conduct a thorough evaluation of (c) Implemented
the bidders’ eligibility requirements
during the post qualification
proceedings; (b) submit the requisite
bidding/technical documents on
awarded contracts to prevent the
issuance of notice of suspension of
payments/transactions in post-audit
by the AT; and (c) be more cautious
in evaluating the legal, technical and
financial documents submitted by
the bidders during preliminary
evaluation and post qualification
prior to recommending the award of
contract.

We recommended that the LCE Implemented
require the MA to exercise diligence
in monitoring the monthly
contribution and remittance to
PhilHealth to avoid the incurrence of
a negative balance to Due to
PhilHealth account.

(a) Implemented
We recommended that the LCE
require the MA and MT to (a) (b) Implemented
immediately remit the unremitted
taxes withheld in the amount of
₱159,084.08 to the BIR; and (b)
practice intact remittance of taxes
withheld to the BIR within the
prescribed period to avoid the
incurrence of surcharges and
penalties to be charged against the
erring employees.

We recommended that the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) instruct
(a) the Municipal Assessor to
prepare and submit the Assessment
Roll to the MT on or before the 31st
day of December of each year;
(b) the MT to
(i) use the said roll in
preparing an updated CLT
bearing the taxpayer’s names,
addresses and amounts due
and collectible during the year;
and
(ii) send out notice to
delinquent taxpayers regularly
and employ other collection
strategies that would increase
the collection of RPT/SET;
and
(c) MA to set-up the RPT/SET
Receivable accounts each year
based on the CLT to be prepared
by the MT.

We recommended that the LCE
require the
(a) MA to recognize in the TF
books the unexpended balance of
the LDRRMF QRF and MOOE for
CY 2022 totaling ₱4,669,176.22
under the account “Trust Liability-
DRRMF” at the end of the year;
(b) MT to transfer the
corresponding cash back up in the
TF depository bank; and
(c) strictly follow the relevant
rules and regulations affecting the
recording of the transfer and
utilization of LDRRMF to reflect
the correct balances of the related
accounts.

We recommended that the LCE
require the MA to
(a) strictly review/analyze the prior
years’ transactions to locate the
unidentified discrepancies and take
up the necessary adjustments to
correct the balances of the
reciprocal accounts; and
(b) conduct regular review and
reconciliation of reciprocal
accounts to correct and prevent
discrepancies in the future.

We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal Human
Resource Management Officer to
(a) prepare and issue policy
guidelines to address the issues on
the necessity and propriety of
hiring job orders;
(b) hire job order personnel based
on necessity and
skills/competencies and who
should be identified/attributed to
specific local projects authorized
by the Sanggunian for a period not
exceeding six months; and
(c) avoid the hiring of JO personnel
whose functions overlap/duplicate
those of the regular employees of
the Municipality especially
accountable officers.

We recommended and the Local
Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
require the MT and MA to
(a) regularly reconcile their
respective records for the early
detection and correction of errors;
and
(b) based on the verified OR
reflecting the correct amount of
the refund, prepare the necessary
adjustments in the book to arrive
at the correct balance of the CLT
account.

We recommended and the LCE
(a) MA to
(i) strictly adhere to the PIM in
the recording of inventories for
fair presentation of Inventory
accounts in the financial
statements; and
(ii) maintain an updated SLCs in
required format to account for all
the receipts and issuances of
inventories;
(b) GSO Officer to
(i) conduct periodic physical
count of inventories and submit
the results thereof to the Audit
Team within the prescribed
period;
(ii) maintain SCs in the required
format for the recording of
receipts and issuances of
inventories; and
(iii) perform regular
reconciliation of inventory
records with the MAO.

We recommended and the LCE
(a) Inventory Committee to
(i) conduct an annual physical
inventory of PPE and submit the
RPCPPE to the AT not later than
January 31 of each year; and
(ii) avail of the “One-Time”
Cleansing of PPE account
balances pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006;
(b) GSO to
(i) report all obsolete and
unserviceable PPE in the IIRUP
duly signed by the MA,
Inspector/Appraisal Committee
and approved by the LCE or
authorized representative, and
submit the same to the Disposal
Committee for proper
disposition;
(ii) keep the unserviceable PPE in
a secure place or storage room
and dispose them within a
reasonable time; and
(iii) maintain and update property
cards per category of PPE and
PAR for issuance to end-users;
and
(c) MA to
(i) establish the cost of PPE items
which are part of a set or recorded
in bulk to facilitate proper
recognition in the books;
(ii) effect the adjustment in the
books of PPE items with cost
below capitalization threshold of
₱50,000.00 that were
inappropriately recognized under
the PPE accounts;
(iii) derecognize unserviceable
properties from the books upon
disposal thereof based on the
approved IIRUP;
(iv) maintain the PPELCs to
reflect the correct balance of each
account; and
(v) submit the necessary
supporting documents on the
parcel of lot procured in CY 2022
for evaluation by the AT; and
(d) MT and GSO Officer to
facilitate the immediate
registration of vehicles in the
name of the Municipality and
application of insurance coverage
with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE
(a) Municipal Engineer or
Implementing Office to properly
plan monitor, and evaluate all
developmental projects to
achieve optimal utilization of the
20% DF and timely
implementation and execution of
targeted PPAs; and
(b) MA to submit the requisite
documentary requirements on
noted disbursements to
substantiate their validity and
propriety.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the MHO to
continue
(a) coordinating with the PHO
on the inventory report for the
vaccines procured by the LGU to
properly account for the vaccines
received either through
procurement or donations; and
(b) the efficient discharge of
responsibility and safeguards
over COVID-19 vaccines to
achieve total immunization of
constituents from COVID-19.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to
(a) continuously ensure the
implementation of all PPAs and
maximize the utilization of
available funds to mitigate and
alleviate the adverse effects of the
COVID-19 pandemic and
calamities; and
(b) require the MA to
(i) recognize in the books the
correct amount of transfer to the
unexpended balances of DRRMF
as Trust Liabilities – DRRMF
account; and
(ii) submit the requisite
documentary requirements to
reliably support the claims
thereof.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to
(a) require the LSB to
continuously prepare a balanced
budget compliant with the legal
and regulatory requirements on
SEF budgeting, allocation and
utilization; and
(b) require the BAC and MA to
submit the documentary
requirements prior to payment of
claims.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to
(a) continue to comply with the
budgetary requirement of at least
5% of the Municipality’s total
budget appropriation for GAD
PPAs;
(b) prioritize the implementation
of the identified PPAs as per GPB
to achieve and promote desirable
gender responsive society; and
(c) utilize fully the GAD budget
in order to attain the objective for
which the funds were
appropriated.
We also recommended and the
LCE agreed to require the
involvement of key members of
the GFPS on the use of HGDG
tool to guarantee the integration
of gender concerns in the design,
management, implementation,
monitoring and evaluation of the
agency programs and projects
and to determine the efficiency
and effectiveness of the LGU’s
mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to
(a) require the BAC Secretariat to
submit all copies of perfected
contracts, POs, and pre-repair
evaluation reports, as well as the
supporting documents to the
Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
(b) instruct the GSO to notify the
Audit Team of the time and date
of the scheduled deliveries to
facilitate the AT’s inspection and
validation thereof;
(c) reorganize the composition of
the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to comply with
the required qualifications of its
members.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to require the MA and the
MT to continue to be compliant
with the BIR regulations on the
withholding and remittance of
taxes within the prescribed
period.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to direct the MA and
HRMO
(a) to exert extra effort to trace
back immediately the source of
discrepancies noted and reconcile
their records with the GSIS to
effect the necessary adjustments
in the books; and
(b) together with the MT, strictly
comply with the regulations on
the withholding and remittance of
monthly contributions due to the
GSIS for the employees’
immediate enjoyment of their
benefits.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the
(a) Human Resource
Management Officer to check the
employee list uploaded in the
EPRS to ensure that the list is
correct and up to date; and
(ii) counter check the amounts of
premiums due per employee on
the generated SPA vis-à-vis the
withheld per payroll to trace
discrepancies; and
(b) the Municipal Accountant
(i) to prepare the necessary
detailed records at year end for
proper monitoring; and
(ii) recognize unremitted
contributions and loan
amortization as payables using
the Due to PhilHealth account.

We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the
(a) HRMO to submit in a timely
manner the required payroll
documents to ensure timely
processing and recording of
transactions in the books; and
(b) MA and MT to continue to
comply with the regulations on
the withholding and remittance of
premiums and loan amortizations
within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed
to require the MT and MA to
(i) comply with the guidelines on the reporting and accounting
of collections and deposits pursuant to existing regulations; and
(ii) coordinate and reconcile
their respective records for the
early detection and correction of
errors.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to
continue to comply with BIR No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998 as amended by BIR RR No. 11-
2018 and BIR RMC No. 23-2012 on the correct withholding of taxes and full remittance of
the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the
(a) MA to review the amounts of GSIS contributions withheld and remitted/posted in the
books to ensure correctness of data and avoid discrepancies; and
(b) Human Resources and Management Office (HRMO) to
(i) update the information of employees submitted to the GSIS; and
(ii) ensure that the contributions deducted from each employee are reconciled with GSIS
records.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the HRMO
and MA to continue to comply with RA No. 9679 and its IRR on the withholding and full
remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the HRMO
and MA to continue to comply with RA No. 7875, as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606
and its RIRR on the withholding and full remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the
prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to identify the problems
that face real property tax collection of the Municipality and enforce the procedures and
remedies in addressing tax delinquencies as provided under Section 174 of RA No. 7160 to
improve revenue collection.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
(a) require the HRMO to
(i) facilitate the submission of accomplishment reports to substantiate payment of wages
of the JO and COS personnel; and
(ii) attach a certification stating that the activities/services of COS personnel cannot be
provided by regular or permanent personnel of the Municipality to support their payroll for
first claim pursuant to paragraphs 1.2.1 and 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012, respectively; and
(b) Bids and Awards Committee to
(i) submit the PhilGEPS registration of the consultants as well as the copy of the NOA and
NTP to the AT for further audit;
(ii) strictly comply with the guidelines for alternative methods of procurement set forth
under Annex H of the 201 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
(iii) subject the procurement of consultancy services to public bidding unless the
conditions for the use of the alternative mode of procurement are met.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Provincial Governor concurred to require the (a) Provincial Assessor, in coordination with the Provincial Titling Committee, to act with dispatch on their plan to conduct ocular inspection of lands in which the titles are in the custody of PGSO and under the name of the Republic of the Philippines and City of Valenzuela and, if warranted, have the titles be transferred under the name of the PGB; (b) PGSO Officer, in coordination with the Provincial Assessor’s Office, to work on the reconstitution of titles for the parcels of land owned by the Province in which copies of titles could no longer be provided by the Registry of Deeds; and (c) PA to coordinate with the PGSO and Provincial Assessor’s Office to effect the derecognition in the books of the value of parcel of land with TCT transferred to a new owner as a form of prize.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Provincial Governor is amenable to require the PA to (a) support with adequate and valid documentation the recorded payables in the books; and (b) furnish the Audit Team with the necessary supporting documents and reports to validate the recorded payables not presented during audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 9

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to instruct the (a) BENRO Officer, PA, and the PT to (a) deposit enhancement fees collected from quarry/mining activities in AGDB under a TF account and to book collections therefrom under a trust liability account; and (b) heads of other Offices concerned to formulate a concrete plan of PPAs that will effectively rehabilitate quarry/mining sites, and address environmental concerns attached to said activities using the EEFs collected.

1.4. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a)
Provincial Accountant (PA) to prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) for the
adjustment of stale checks; and (b) PA and Provincial Treasurer (PT) to (i) send a
written notice to the payees of the unclaimed checks at least one month before they
become stale; and (ii) exert vigorous efforts to cause the possible production of
corroborative records to establish the existence of the PNB Malolos, Municipal
account with balance in the books of ₱209,767.59.

2.6. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA
to have a computation of the actual payments made by the TMWD to determine
actual amount collectible from the TMWD and prepare an adjustment in the books
accordingly; (b) Provincial Legal Officer (PLO) (i) to make a representation with the
BCDA to locate the ALTO Project and inquire on the status of the North Rail Project
for proper action on the advance payment made to the latter; and (ii) PAO, PCEDO,
and PPDO, to coordinate and continue improving the collection strategies of the PGB
by sending regular collection and demand letters to collect the receivables due from
all defaulting debtors/ beneficiaries/ associates/ cooperatives.

3.5. We reiterated our recommendation and the Provincial Governor agreed to
require the (a) PA (i) to record the issuance of drugs and medicines and medical and
dental supplies to BMC and DHs on the basis of the SSMI submitted by the hospitals;
and (ii) to record in the books the inventories at the BMC and DHs as of year-end of
39
2021 totaling ₱81,668,305.26; (b) MD Head and PA to determine the difference of
₱813,585.77 on fuel and oil for appropriate action; (c) MD Head to refrain from
assigning a single employee to handle the issuance, recording and reporting of issued
fuel and oil; (d) GSO Officer to (i) submit the RPCI of inventories recorded in the
books totaling ₱8,954,441.72 and on a semestral basis thereafter; (ii) religiously notify
the Audit Team of the deliveries within 24-hours from receipt to facilitate inspection;
(d) Provincial Information Technology Office (PITO) Officer to develop an Inventory
Management System to have a complete track of inventories from purchasing,
issuance, and posting of inventories to SCs and SLCs; and (e) officials of the BMC
and DHs to direct their supply officers to submit the SSMI and RPCI on a monthly
and semestral basis, respectively, to the Audit Team for their reference in audit.

3.6. We also recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to assign a GSO
staff at the BMC and DHs for monitoring and reporting of transferred and issued
inventories thereat.

4.5. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a)
PSWDO Officer and GSO Officer to furnish the Audit Team with the Summary/List
of in-Kind Donations and Distribution Report in accordance with existing rules and
regulations; (b) PSWDO Officer to (i) demand from the beneficiaries of in-kind
donations the disclosure of the needed information in the ARs for validation by the
Audit Team; and (ii) submit the inventory of undistributed in-kind donations as of
year-end to the PAO for appropriate recording in the books.

5.5. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA
to (i) maintain subsidiary records for the CIP account for easy reference of
information on the details of the account; and (ii) retrieve the records affecting the
CIP account to facilitate the necessary adjustments in the books of the undetermined
balance under the TF; and (b) Provincial Engineer (PE) to submit the status of the
infrastructures completed as of year-end to the PAO for the timely recognition of CIP
and infrastructure assets in the books.

6.9. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a)
members of the IC to (i) immediately conduct physical inventory of all PGB-owned
PPE to avail of the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts; and (ii) undertake the
disposal of unserviceable properties still recognized in the books to prevent the
further deterioration and loss of commercial value of obsolete PPE; (b) PA to (i)
derecognize the unserviceable/obsolete PPE in the books of accounts on the basis of
the Inventory of Unserviceable PPE prepared by the GSO and IC; (ii) provide
53
depreciation for depreciable assets for fair presentation of PPE account balances in
the books; (iii) to reclassify the tangible items below the capitalization threshold of
₱15,000.00 to the appropriate inventory account or expense account upon issuance;
and (c) PA and GSO Officer to maintain complete PPELCs, PCs and renew the ARE
every three years for control and monitoring purposes.

7.6. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA
to recognize in the books the donated medical ambulance and equipment; (b) GSO
Officer to install effective procedural guidelines on the receipt, recording and
documentation of donated property and equipment to ensure timely recognition in
the books; and (c) members of the PGB’s Appraisal Committee to conduct appraisal
of donated properties in case information on the acquisition cost of the items could
not be obtained from the donors.

8.6 We reiterated our recommendation and the Provincial Governor agreed to
require (a) the Provincial Assessor’s Office, in coordination with the Provincial
Titling Committee, to act with dispatch on their plan to conduct ocular inspection of
parcels of land in which the titles are in the custody of GSO and under the name of
the RP and City of Valenzuela, and if warranted, have the titles transferred under the
name of the PGB; (b) the GSO, in coordination with the Provincial Assessor’s Office
and PLO, to (i) sustain their vigorous efforts in working on the reconstitution of titles
on parcels of land owned by the PGB in which copies of titles could no longer be
provided by the RD; and (ii) have the title of newly acquired parcel of land
transferred under the name of the PGB; and (c) the PA to coordinate with the GSO
and Provincial Assessor’s Office to derecognize in the books the one parcel of land
granted by the previous administration to a recipient as form of prize.

9.7 We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA
and GSO Officer to furnish the Audit Team with valid supporting documents on
unconfirmed APs; (b) PA to record only those transactions supported with valid
documentation under the AP account.

10.5. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA
and PT (i) to make representation with the Management of the identified source
agencies to facilitate the liquidation/return of TFs that remained dormant in the
books for a longer period of time; (b) PA to maintain a detailed SL for Due to NGAs
account; and (c) facilitate the transfer of 30% share to PNP upon completion and
submission of necessary documents to the PTO and PAO such as the MOA signed by
and between the PNP and PGB, among others.

11.5. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the hospital
directors/chiefs to direct their personnel in-charge of MAIP and Malasakit Center funds
to submit a monthly ROD to the PAO and, copy furnish the Audit Team thereof, for
the timely recording of the uses of funds by the former.

13.9. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed that (a) as head of the
Provincial Disaster Management Council (PDRRMC), warrant the utilization of the
30% QRF on PPAs that will quickly address the needs of the victims of
disaster/calamity; (b) require the implementing Offices concerned to implement
priority projects in a timely manner to achieve disaster avoidance and mitigation of
possible impacts of disaster; (c) require the PSWDO, in coordination with the
members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC), to make certain that the cash
assistance payouts are immediately delivered to victims of calamities; and (d)
require the PA and PT to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF
to TF.

14.10. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to direct the Provincial
Engineer (PE) (a) to fully implement all development projects proposed to be
undertaken during the budget year to attain the objective of providing timely socio-
economic benefits to its constituents; and (b) to require the concerned project
engineers, officers and employees to be prudent in overseeing the timely
implementation of PPAs under their responsibility.

15.5. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed as Chairman of the
PLSB to require the (a) School Superintendent to (i) submit to the Audit Team the
certified correct distribution report for the purchased self-learning modules, duly
received by the school principals/officials concerned; and (ii) expedite the
establishment of the studio room for timely and immediate enjoyment of its
educational benefits to the students; and (b) PE to regularly monitor the status of
accomplishments of on-going projects to meet the goals and targets set for the period.

16.10. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed (a) that in coordination
with the members of the PSWMB and Council to implement the goals and plans
embedded in its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan such as setting up a fully
97
functional CMRF and introduce the conversion of wastes into useful products to help
the Province in the reduction of wastes and hauling costs; and (b) to direct the
BENRO Officer to substantiate accomplishments with clear and complete
documentation for validation.

17.3. We reiterated our recommendations and the Provincial Governor agreed to
require the members of the GFPS and TWG to (a) coordinate with the GFPS of the
Cities/Municipalities and request that their GAD mechanisms, plans and processes
be incorporated in the GAD database of the PGB to facilitate the completion of the
GAD database for the whole Province of Bulacan; (b) restate in the succeeding year’s
GPB all the GAD PPAs that were not implemented in CY 2021 due to the pandemic.

18.10. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) GSO
or the members of the BAC to submit the bidding and/or negotiation documents
executed prior to the signing of the MOA, company, personnel profile, and other legal
documents of the MSA; and (b) BMC officials concerned to (i) submit a feasibility
study to determine the need to engage the services of MSA for its pulmonary services
prior to the renewal of the MOA in 2022; (ii) assign a personnel to oversee and
monitor the operation of the MSA at the BMC; and (iii) ensure that the BPC and CS
are signed and attested by the assigned BMC personnel at the Pulmonary Department
for counter-checking of correctness of charges.

19.8. We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a)
members of the LFC to (i) monitor and conduct periodic review of performance level
especially on collections from Real Property Tax and Rent Income which comprise the
biggest amount of revenues that missed projections; (ii) perform a systematic and
realistic forecasting of revenues/income taking into account the prior years’ experience
and figures; and (iii) set up the performance targets on income accounts that do not
have projections; and (b) the PT and other departments concerned to analyze the
reasons for the shortfall in actual revenues vis-à-vis the targets and propose alternative
measures to improve collections.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA and PT to (i) determine the status of the recorded Cash-in-Bank account with the PNB Malolos Branch through personal representation; exert all efforts to gather/retrieve all information regarding the dormant bank accounts. (ii) send a written notice to the payee of the existence of an unclaimed check at least one month before a check becomes stale; (iii) gather all the documents pertaining to the unrecorded credit memos/deposits of ₱9,112,853.00 and record the same in the books; (b) PT to gather/retrieve all information/records regarding the dormant peso and dollar bank accounts, and analyze the details for proper disposition; and (c) PA to (i) return the balance to the source agency of dormant accounts that represent unexpended balance of previous years’ fund transfer for a specific purpose; and (ii) close and transfer the dormant and unexpended accounts to the GF bank account proper, if warranted, for use to other PPA of the PGB through a supplemental budget to be enacted by the Sangguniang Panglalawigan (SP).

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PT and the LO to prepare the consolidated RCD with all the details and information prescribed and with the former’s acknowledgement to establish accountability and provide check and balance; (b) COs and LO to (i) report in the RCD, the collections from PhilHealth together with other collections for the day, for integrated reporting of collections and deposits; and (ii) maintain a complete cashbook for Cash-in-Treasury and Cash-in-Bank accounts ruled, footed, and certified correct by the concerned accountable officer concerned; and (c) COs prepare the RCD and remit the collections daily to the LO.
observations.managementAction: The Cashier has been designated been designated as LO due to heavy workload of PT. As a remedy, a revision on Summary of RCD/RCLO by Liquidating Officer was made.
observations.reason: The Cashier has been designated as LO due to heavy workload of PT.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to direct the (a) Chiefs of Hospitals to (i) standardize the Per Capita Budget for Meal Provision of Patients based on the 1,800 calorie or an equivalent of at least ₱150 pesos/day meal allowance pursuant to DOH AO No. 2016-0020; and (ii) install a system of check and balance in the management system over food supplies by segregating the multiple functions of the NDs, requiring their immediate supervisors to check on the ND’s works and to maintain complete records of receipts and issuances over procurement and safekeeping of food supplies; (b) Chief of Hospitals and ND of FTRMH to plan the menu items served to inpatients within the day, and conduct patient’s satisfaction survey on meals served to inpatients; (c) Chiefs of Hospitals and NDs of BMC, SMDH, and EGPMDH to allocate the budget solely for meal subsistence of its patients; (d) Provincial Budget Officer, PA and PT to prioritize the processing of disbursement vouchers (DVs) for the replenishments of cash advances for vital operations of the district hospitals; (e) NDs to (i) adopt canvass from at least three suppliers for purchases of food supplies involving ₱1,000.00 and above in compliance with the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184; and (ii) submit complete and sufficient documents supporting the DVs.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) PA, PT and the Provincial Budget Office (PBO) Officer to fast track the processing of replenishment checks by hospitals to ensure that funds are available for their emergency requirements, and to make certain that replenishments are done when disbursements reach at least 75%, or as the need requires; (b) Chief of Hospitals and Administrative Officers to direct the accountable officers to (i) improve their procurement planning of supplies and materials and refrain from using the cash advance on non-recurring and non-emergency types of procurement so as the cash advance would be available for priority and emergency expenses; (ii) maintain sufficient records for supplies and materials purchased and issued for monitoring purposes; and (iii) ensure that payments out of cash advance are supported with sufficient documentation; and (c) PA to oversee the recording of transactions to their appropriate accounts for an accurate reporting of transactions/disbursements.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to direct the (a) PA to (i) consistently record the inventories in their respective asset accounts and recognize as expense the value of issued inventories by requiring the PGSO to submit the RSMI promptly; and (ii) restore back to the inventory account the undistributed/unconsumed items as of year-end; (b) PGSO Officer to (i) create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of inventories at the PGSO and PGB operated hospitals; and (ii) submit the RPCI in the prescribed format to the Audit Team; and the (c) PSWD Officer to submit to the Audit Team the ARs issued for the donations in-kind received.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to direct the PGSO Officer to (a) immediately conduct physical inventory of PPE and the PA to reconcile the book balances with physical inventory records/reports; (b) submit the records of disposed unserviceable properties to the PA to complete the derecognition of the PPE that were disposed of years ago; (c) report all obsolete and have already unserviceable PPE in the IIRUP duly signed by the PA, Inspector/Appraisal Committee and approved by the LCE, and submit the report to the Disposal Committee for proper disposition to avoid further deterioration; and (d) keep all the unserviceable PPE in a secured place and dispose them within a reasonable time.
observations.managementAction: The PGSO was not able to conduct a physical inventory in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Physical inventory will be resumed once the circumstances permit management to conduct the physical inventory. Management submitted the unserviceable properties which were disposed in 2019. No disposal was conducted in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: The PGSO was not able to conduct a physical inventory in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation and the Provincial Governor concurred to direct the PA and the PT (a) continue to be compliant with the terms and conditions of the loan agreement entered into with the bank; (b) maintain special accounts in the GF and maintain subsidiary records for every project funded by the secured loans to easily monitor the status and implementation of the funded projects; and (c) retrieve the documents/records for the payments made out of the secured loans to determine if these were utilized in accordance with their intended purposes.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to require the (a) PT to install an effective control system and monitor the issuance of accountable forms to collecting officers/accountable officers; (b) LO to (i) closely verify the RCDs submitted particularly the details of accountable forms issued and balances to ensure that all issued accountable forms are correctly reported in the RCD; and (ii) affix her signature on the triplicate copy of the last ORs issued; and (c) AFC to (i) consider reducing the quantity of accountable forms being issued to COs based on the average volume of issued accountable forms per day/month/period to COs; (ii) refrain from issuing accountable forms to individuals who have no authority to receive/request accountable forms; (iii) prepare a complete CRAAF to include all types of accountable forms, and those issued/sold to other LGUs; and (iv) prepare the CRAAF in sequential order to permit close and easy monitoring on the completeness of accountable forms issued.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to require the (a) head of the PHRMO to submit the copy of the Minutes of Meeting directing the Department Heads to do the scheduling of employees who reported to duty during the pandemic; (b) head of the PHRMO and PA to review the payrolls with discrepancies and submit a written satisfactory explanation for the discrepancies noted; (c) Department Heads to submit the schedule of employees required to work during the pandemic stating the dates and purpose of work as directed by the LCE; and (d) PA to closely verify the correctness of the payrolls/disbursements and effect correction as needed, prior to payment.

We recommended that the Provincial Governor require the (a) BENRO Officer to (i) impose stricter measures on quarrying/mining activities that could help in the attainment of target collections; (ii) provide enough personnel to man the checkpoints twenty four hours and consider creating a task force that will be responsible in frequent monitoring and inspection of quarrying/mining activities; (iii) require the checkers/watchmen to maintain a record book/logbook to record the details of every transport trucks passing through the checkpoints such as delivery receipt, transport slip number, name of permittee/operator, date and time of activity, among others, for reporting and reference in the verification of quarry/mining tax collections; (iv) prepare monthly report of receipt and issuance of DR and TS and furnish the Audit Team with the report for verification; (v) submit a detailed report of the issued DR and TS for CY 2020 with identification of corresponding official receipts details issued by the PTO; (vi) submit a written satisfactory explanation for the discrepancy of 1,392 booklets noted in the report of the receipt and issuance of DR and TS and the disposal of old forms; and (vii) fill-out completely the Order of Payment to facilitate the complete filing-out of billing statement by the PTO; (b) PT and BENRO Officer to discuss and study the possible transfer of the handling and issuance of DR and TS from the BENRO to the PTO to eliminate single-source control; and (c) members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC), and BENRO the adoption of higher penalty to be imposed to individuals, permittees, and operators who will be caught doing illegal quarrying/mining activities and violation of environmental laws and regulations through enactment of an Ordinance by the SP.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) Chief of Hospitals to submit the schedule of PHWs required to work during the pandemic as directed by the LCE; and (b) Chief of Offices concerned to submit the authority of the personnel in the payroll to physically report for work in their assigned hospital/health facility, offices or work stations, the prescribed official working hours and dates attended during ECQ a requirement for the payment of SRA to qualified personnel.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor is amenable to require the (a) PBO Officer to continue to allocate at least 5% of revenue from regular sources for the LDRRMF; (b) Provincial Planning Officer, in coordination with the members of the PDC, to incorporate in the AIP all PPAs to be implemented for the year; and (c) PA and PBO Officer to (i) reconcile their records at least once every quarter to identify those obligations that have not yet been processed to avoid accumulation of payments; and (ii) if said obligations are not processed within a reasonable time, cancel the obligated amounts to accommodate priority expenditures of the PGB.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to require the (a) PDRRMO Officer to (i) plan for PPAs to address the needs of the intended recipients/beneficiaries of the cash donations; and (ii) implement the priority programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMFIP of the STF; (b) members of the LFC to transfer to the STF all unexpended balances of the LDRRMF as of year-end; and (c) PA to (i) revert to the GF the unutilized STFs, aged more than five years, which shall be made available for other social services after enactment by the local sanggunian; (ii) submit the documents pertaining to the payable on the completed project in CY 2014 under the TF for verification, and recognition in the books; and (iii) comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the recording of financial transactions under the STF of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to require the (a) PA to (i) record the drugs and medicines, and medical and supplies distributed to BMC and other DHs in accordance with Sections 182, 188 and 189, of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1 and IPSAS No. 12; and (ii) verify the completeness and authenticity of documents supported to the DVs prior to payment; (b) PBO Officer to closely monitor the incurrence of expenditures by the concerned Offices within the approved appropriations; and (c) PT to stamp “PAID” to the documents attached to the paid DVs to avoid their re-use.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor is amendable to direct the (a) Provincial Engineer (PE) to (i) adequately plan the infrastructure projects to be included in the AIP; and (ii) ensure timely and efficient implementation of projects to avoid further delays in the project completion; (b) Provincial Planning and Development Officer to prepare a supplemental AIP to include COVID-19-response-related PPAs for the approval of the SP; (c) PGSO Officer to submit the necessary documents to support the repairs and maintenance of infrastructure assets; (d) PA to strictly follow the perpetual inventory system in the recognition in the books of purchases of construction materials used in repairs and maintenance projects; (e) PBO Officer, PA and PT to (i) promptly process DVs with their supporting documents to avoid delays in paying the contractors/suppliers; and (ii) implement a document tracking system that will monitor the status of documents by tracking down slow moving documents and identifying those responsible for the delays in the processing.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to (a) require the (a) PGSO Officer to (i) investigate and take appropriate action on the irregular and stoppage of collection of hazardous wastes from the SMDH and other DHs by the CESI and STPI, and demand written explanation from them, in order that any reason for the deficiency can be dealt with accordingly; and (ii) require a written satisfactory explanation from the STPI for the deviation in the schedule of collection of general wastes at the transfer station; (b) the BENRO Officer to (i) ensure that hazardous wastes are not mixed-up with the general wastes at the transfer station by making certain that these are collected by the CESI and STPI on a regular basis and in accordance with the contract; and (ii) comply with the DOH regulations in the transport of hazardous waste to avoid its exposure to human health and environment; and (c) PA to verify thoroughly the DVs to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents prior to their payment.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to direct the (a) Chief of Hospital of SMDH to monitor and ensure full liquidation of the remaining balance of IRM fund; and (b) Chief of Hospitals to instruct the hospital personnel concerned to forward a report to the PAO of reimbursements/claims to PHIC on real time which shall be basis of the PAO to recognize in the books the revenue and receivable from PHIC.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the PA concurred to (a) maintain sufficient records/details of TFs and send monitoring reports to the concerned implementing offices their use to ensure the implementation of projects in accordance with their purpose and objectives; (b) exert vigorous efforts to determine the details of the Due to NGAs account; (c) confirm the correctness of the balance of the unliquidated fund transfers by sending confirmation letters, coordinating with the source agencies, and returning the unutilized balances, if warranted; and (d) file a request for authority to write-off dormant fund transfers to the COA after the conditions and requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 have been fully satisfied.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) Members of the GFPS and TWG to (i) coordinate with the GFPS of the Cities/Municipalities and request from them their own GAD database to be incorporated to the GAD database of the PGB to facilitate gradual completion of GAD database for the whole Province of Bulacan; (ii) conduct gender analysis of GAD PPAs through the administration of assessment tools; (iii) restate in the CY 2021 GPB the GAD PPAs that were not implemented this current year due to pandemic; and (iv) consider the establishment of a GAD Office to maintain a focused, concentrated, and coordinated response in the administration, planning, implementation, and reporting of GAD related PPAs; and (b) Provincial Planning Development Officer to identify whether the PPAs listed in the AIP are GAD related or included in the GPB, as well as the implementing offices, in order that the PBO and/or the PAO could maintain a registry/subsidiary records of all obligated, implemented, and paid GAD PPAs.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor is amenable to require the (a) members of the BAC-TWG to submit the complete bidding documents on the procurement of self-learning modules to the Audit Team; (b) members of the Provincial School Board to submit (i) the distribution sheet for learning modules, approved program design to support the purchase, and the ABC; (ii) the distribution sheet for the sports supplies given to participants of the CY 2020 CLRAA, the activity which was cancelled due to COVID-19 crisis; (iii) a written satisfactory explanation for undelivered rubber shoes by the Supplier as of year-end in spite of full payment; (c) PGSO Officer to demand the immediate delivery of 661 pairs of rubber shoes from the Supplier to be stored at the stockroom of the Province; (d) DepEd Supply Officer and Chief Procurement Division to explain in writing the untruthful statement in the AIR and DR; and (e) Provincial Engineer to facilitate the full implementation of infrastructure projects in accordance with the plan and budget.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor concurred to direct the PHRMO, PAO, and PTO to continue consistent compliance with the pertinent laws and regulations on the withholding and remittance of premiums, contributions, loan repayments, and applicable taxes to GSIS, PHIC, HDMF, and BIR, respectively.

We recommended and the Provincial Governor agreed to require the (a) concerned persons responsible to immediately settle and/or submit the requirements of the issued NSs to avoid the issuance of ND pursuant to the abovementioned regulations; (b) PA to monitor the (i) payments of liable personnel concerned on issued NDs upon approval of the request for installment payment though salary deduction; and (ii) monitor the settlement of NSs of concerned responsible personnel; and (iii) comply with the revised documentary requirements for common government transactions prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension of transactions.
